Hotel Tagline: Corner suites look out over the IJ harbour, with Centraal Station's roofline stretching beyond.
Summary: The six corner suites have water-facing balconies looking over the IJ harbour and the Centraal Station roofline. For height, the Malabar sky bar on the 8th and 9th floors extends the panorama. We’d book a corner suite facing the harbour and time the arrival by boat from Centraal.
Description ⚙: Hotel Jakarta Amsterdam is an eco-conscious property with 200 rooms and suites in the Eastern Docklands. The contemporary glass facade provides harbour views from the moment of arrival. Natural elements including timber and tropical plants adorn the atrium, with sustainability throughout. Rooms and suites feature floor-to-ceiling windows; many include a water-facing balcony, with the six corner suites particularly favoured. The Malabar sky bar on the 8th and 9th floors offers panoramic views of Amsterdam including the IJ harbour and Centraal Station roofline. WellCome Wellness provides a swimming pool, sauna, and fitness facilities.
Address: Javakade 766, 1019 SH Amsterdam, Netherlands
Nearest Airports: AMS · Schiphol · 22 km

Hotel Tagline: Every room faces the IJ River, where boat traffic after dark becomes a moving display of lights.
Summary: Every room in the building faces the IJ River — the Mövenpick’s specific promise in Amsterdam, and it holds. As night falls, the boat traffic creates a moving tableau of lights visible from any floor. The Passenger Terminal Amsterdam is directly outside.
Description ⚙: Mövenpick Hotel Amsterdam City Centre is positioned along the IJ River, with every room in the hotel offering views of the River IJ and Amsterdam’s city centre. As night falls, the boat traffic creates a moving display of lights visible from any floor. The Passenger Terminal Amsterdam is directly outside, making this an ideal base for cruise travellers. Muziekgebouw aan ’t IJ, a venue for contemporary music concerts, is within arm’s reach. The Muziekgebouw Bimhuis tram stop provides direct access to Amsterdam’s city centre and the Museum District.
Address: Piet Heinkade 11, 1019 BR Amsterdam, Netherlands
Nearest Airports: AMS · Schiphol · 22 km

Hotel Tagline: Twenty-three floors above De Pijp, the Amstelkanaal, Rembrandt Tower, and Munttoren share the same frame.
Summary: Twenty-three floors above De Pijp, with the Amstelkanaal, the Rembrandt Tower, and the Munttoren all in the same frame. Request a high-floor room at check-in. The Twenty Third Bar on the top floor is the public version of the same view; Ciel Bleu, directly below it, holds two Michelin stars.
Description ⚙: With its soaring 23 floors, Hotel Okura Amsterdam offers panoramic views of the Dutch capital. High-floor rooms provide sights of the Amstelkanaal, Amstel River, Rembrandt Tower, the Munttoren, and many of Amsterdam’s emblematic churches. The Twenty Third Bar on the 23rd floor is the hotel’s signature public bar with panoramic views of Amsterdam. Ciel Bleu restaurant holds two Michelin stars. Yamazato holds one Michelin star for Japanese cuisine. Nagomi Spa & Health offers saunas, an indoor pool, and treatment rooms. Condé Nast Traveler 2025 Readers’ Choice Awards: #1 Hotel in Amsterdam. Situated in the multicultural De Pijp area, near the Albert Cuyp Market.
Address: Ferdinand Bolstraat 333, 1072 LH Amsterdam, Netherlands
Nearest Airports: AMS · Schiphol · 15 km; RTM · Rotterdam The Hague · 67 km

Hotel Tagline: Cliffside above Mermerli Beach, where the Mediterranean meets the Taurus Mountains from a rooftop hot tub.
Summary: The suite to request: a Grace Penthouse with rooftop terrace, outdoor hot tub and unobstructed views of the Mediterranean and the Taurus range from the same deck. Built into the cliffs above Mermerli Beach — adults 18+ — this is the most distinctive view address in Antalya’s old-town fringe.
Description ⚙: Perge Hotels – Adult Only is a boutique adults-only (18+) hotel set into the limestone cliffs above Mermerli Beach in Antalya’s city centre. The Grace Penthouse suites feature private rooftop terraces with outdoor hot tubs and unobstructed panoramic views across the Mediterranean Sea and the Taurus Mountains. The fine-dining restaurant LANTHE is perched above the water with the same dual sea-and-mountain backdrop. A private beach cove with cabanas completes one of the most distinctive view properties in the city.
Address: Gençlik, 1311. Sk. No:5, 07100 Muratpaşa/Antalya, Türkiye
Nearest Airports: AYT · Antalya · 16 km

Hotel Tagline: Two perpendicular glass walls hold the Mediterranean on one side, the Taurus ridge on the other.
Summary: Floor-to-ceiling glass on two perpendicular walls means facing the Mediterranean from one side and the full Taurus ridge from the other — simultaneously. All-inclusive, with free shuttle access to Land of Legends. The sea-and-mountain suite is the room that defines this hotel.
Description ⚙: Rixos Downtown Antalya is a 5-star all-inclusive hotel set in the Atatürk Culture Park on Antalya’s seafront. Its standout rooms are the dual-panorama suites, which feature floor-to-ceiling glass on two perpendicular walls, framing simultaneous views of the Mediterranean Sea and the Taurus Mountain ridge. The hotel has a private beach, two outdoor pools, the Anjana Spa, and all-inclusive access to the Land of Legends theme park via free shuttle. The rooftop terrace provides a supplementary angle over the Gulf of Antalya.
Address: Meltem, Sakıp Sabancı Blv., 07030 Muratpaşa/Antalya, Türkiye
Nearest Airports: AYT · Antalya · 22 km

Hotel Tagline: From the 10th floor, the Gulf of Antalya spreads across the full panoramic sweep.
Summary: Renovated and reopened in March 2025, Akra Hotel’s infinity pool frames the Mediterranean and the Taurus Mountains in a single seafront composition. The 10th-floor Asmani Restaurant adds a full panoramic sweep over the Gulf of Antalya — a compelling case for a city-hotel stay on the coast.
Description ⚙: Akra Hotel is a 5-star seafront hotel in Antalya, comprehensively renovated and reopened in March 2025. Every room features a private balcony with views of the Mediterranean Sea or the Bey Mountains; the seafront infinity pool frames both in one composition. The 10th-floor Asmani Restaurant delivers a full panoramic sweep over the Gulf of Antalya. The hotel also has a private beach platform, the LifeCo spa, and four restaurants, including the rooftop Pablito Bar.
Address: Şirinyalı, Lara Cd. No:24, 07100 Muratpaşa/Antalya, Türkiye
Nearest Airports: AYT · Antalya · 15 km

Hotel Tagline: From the rooftop, the Mediterranean opens flat to the horizon with Taurus snowfields above the treeline.
Summary: Upper-floor sea-view rooms have private balconies and hot tubs; the rooftop sun deck — wooden loungers, open Mediterranean, Taurus snow caps above the treeline — adds the wider angle. The Sunset Jazz Bar and eight restaurants complete one of Belek’s most polished all-inclusive packages.
Description ⚙: Voyage Belek Golf & Spa is a 5-star all-inclusive resort on a private Mediterranean beach in Belek, with rooms on upper floors offering private balconies, hot tubs, and direct sea views. The rooftop sun deck provides a 180-degree panorama encompassing the Mediterranean and the snow-capped Taurus Mountains above the treeline. The resort includes an adjacent 18-hole golf course, six outdoor pools, the Sunset Jazz Bar and eight à la carte restaurants.
Address: Belek, Günübirlik Cd. No: 2, 07506 Serik/Antalya, Türkiye
Nearest Airports: AYT · Antalya · 34 km

Hotel Tagline: Balconies face the Mediterranean directly, with the illuminated pier below at dusk.
Summary: One of the region’s most reliably reviewed large resorts: sea-view rooms face the Mediterranean directly, eight à la carte restaurants and Carpe Diem Spa justify the length of stay. The illuminated pier and lantern-lit beach lounge are the image guests photograph at dusk.
Description ⚙: Concorde De Luxe Resort is a 5-star beachfront all-inclusive hotel on Lara Beach in Antalya, one of the region’s top-rated large resorts. Sea-view rooms feature spacious balconies with direct Mediterranean views. The property’s illuminated pier, beach bar and lantern-lit lounge area on the sand are a signature feature at sunset and after dark. The Carpe Diem Spa, eight à la carte restaurants, four pools and a private sandy beach complete the package.
Address: Kemerağzı, Yaşar Sobutay Bulvarı No:44, 07980 Lara/Antalya, Türkiye
Nearest Airports: AYT · Antalya · 16 km

Hotel Tagline: Upper-floor balconies hold the pool gardens, open Mediterranean and Taurus ridge in a single unbroken frame.
Summary: Consistently among Turkey’s top-rated resorts on Booking.com. Upper-floor balconies sweep from the pool landscape to the open Mediterranean — one continuous frame that includes the Taurus ridge on the horizon. An 18-hole Montgomerie course, seven pools and a private beach complete the picture.
Description ⚙: Maxx Royal Belek Golf Resort is a 5-star all-inclusive resort on a private Mediterranean beach in Belek, consistently rated among the top resorts in Turkey. Upper-floor rooms deliver balcony views across the resort’s pool landscape, palm gardens and the open Mediterranean; sea-view rooms also face the Taurus Mountains. The resort features an 18-hole Montgomerie golf course, seven pools, nine restaurants, 16 bars and a full-service spa.
Address: Belek, İskele Cad Maxx Royal Blok No: 21/14 İç Kapı No: Z1, 07505 Serik/Antalya, Türkiye
Nearest Airports: AYT · Antalya · 36 km

Hotel Tagline: From upper floors, the Mediterranean stretches unbroken to the horizon above the Belek coastline.
Summary: The room to request: an upper-floor sea-view suite where wide sliding doors open to a balcony with full Mediterranean panoramas over the Belek coastline. Lagoon Suites add direct pool access; an 18-hole golf course and 200-yard private beach complete one of Belek’s flagship resorts.
Description ⚙: Kaya Palazzo Golf Resort is a 5-star all-inclusive resort in Belek, set on a 200-yard private Mediterranean beach. The Lagoon Suites offer direct pool access and views across the lagoon to the sea; rooms on upper floors deliver unobstructed Mediterranean panoramas, with the Taurus range visible in the distance. The resort includes an 18-hole golf course, five à la carte restaurants, two poolside bars, a full-service spa and a water park.
Address: Kadriye Üçkum Tepesi Mevki, Kadriye, 07506 Serik/Antalya, Türkiye
Nearest Airports: AYT · Antalya · 35 km

Hotel Tagline: Five floors from the Sacred Rock, the rooftop pool aligns with the Parthenon.
Summary: The rooftop infinity pool aligns with the Parthenon — five floors between you and the Sacred Rock. The Acropolis Junior and Luxury Junior suites, plus the Acropolis Apartment, offer the view from the room. Designed by Andreas Kriezis; the building took five years to restore. 46 rooms, Monastiraki.
Description ⚙: Majestically overlooking the Acropolis hill and located in the vibrant Monastiraki neighborhood, The Dolli at Acropolis stands as one of Athens' most elegant properties. The rooftop infinity pool offers an unparalleled view of the Parthenon. With 46 rooms, suites, and apartments, the Acropolis Junior and Luxury Junior suites, along with the Acropolis Apartment, deliver direct sightlines to the Sacred Rock. Designed by acclaimed architect Andreas Kriezis, the listed building underwent a meticulous five-year restoration.
Address: Mitropoleos 49, Athina 105 56, Greece
Nearest Airports: ATH · Athens · 43 km

The 10th-floor rooftop garden adds a pool and restaurant at the same elevation. Built in 2016 over the ruins of the Themistocles Wall, which are preserved and visible to guests.
Description ⚙: Five-star hotel completed in 2016, situated in the heart of Athens, built over the ruins of the Themistocles Wall — an ancient archaeological site preserved and visible to guests. Designed by architect Patroclos Karantinos in the Greek modernist style. The 216 rooms and suites include Acropolis-view categories with private balconies. The 10th-floor rooftop garden features a pool and Mediterranean restaurant with panoramic Acropolis and Lycabettus Hill views.

Description ⚙: Landmark luxury hotel on Syntagma Square, open since the 1870s. The 320 rooms and suites blend old-world elegance with modern amenities; the 58 suites include personalized Butler Service. The hotel underwent a grand restoration in the early 2000s with an investment exceeding 80 million euros. The rooftop offers breakfast and dining with panoramic Acropolis views, and the rooftop pool frames the Parliament, Olympic Stadium, and Lycabettus Hill.

The 278 rooms and suites — among the city’s largest — include balconies with Acropolis views. The rooftop will revive the iconic Galaxy bar as Galaxy Dispensary later in 2026.
Description ⚙: The €340M redevelopment of the former Hilton Athens, which opened in April 2026 as Conrad Athens The Ilisian at Vasilissis Sofias Avenue. The 278 rooms and suites — among the largest in Athens — include private balconies with Acropolis, Lycabettus Hill, and Aegean Sea views. The suite collection includes three Presidential Suites, a Royal Suite, and the Omega Penthouse (400 sqm, private pool). Nine dining concepts led by Executive Chef Angelos Lantos include Byzantino brasserie and the Galaxy Dispensary rooftop, opening later in 2026. Wellness spans 2,000+ sqm including a Longevity Clinic and a 700-metre outdoor running track.

Hotel Tagline: From the 17th floor, the Sky Tower fills the glass as afternoon light catches the harbour beyond.
Summary: The Sky Tower dominates the frame from the 17th-floor Chairman Suite — floor-to-ceiling windows on two sides, with the harbour catching the afternoon light beyond. For other room types, the Club Lounge on the upper floors keeps the same landmark reliably in sight.
Description ⚙: Situated within Auckland's vibrant uptown district, the Cordis, Auckland epitomizes a five-star boutique hotel experience at the heart of the city's cultural scene. Offering captivating views of the Auckland Skyline and the iconic Sky Tower, the Cordis, Auckland exudes refined elegance. With 640 rooms and suites, it stands as one of Auckland's finest hotels, renowned for exceptional quality. The 17th-floor Chairman Suite with Club Access steals the show with its expansive size and floor-to-ceiling panoramic windows. A multitude of dining choices awaits guests, ranging from wholesome breakfasts at Eight Restaurant to delectable treats at the Chandelier Lounge. The Cordis, Auckland offers pampering treatments at the Chuan Spa, invigorating workouts at the Health Club, and a serene rooftop pool and jacuzzi. Families travelling with children can embrace the Cordis Kids Club.
Address: 83 Symonds Street, Grafton, Auckland 1010, New Zealand
Nearest Airports: AKL · Auckland · 19 km

Summary: Opened in January 2024, this is Auckland’s newest five-star waterfront property. Harbour View Rooms face the Waitematā directly — automated blinds reveal the water at the press of a button. The Club InterContinental lounge adds an elevated, harbour-facing vantage point for upper-tier guests.
Description ⚙: Opened on 30 January 2024, InterContinental Auckland by IHG marks the debut of the brand in New Zealand and brings a new benchmark of contemporary luxury to the waterfront precinct adjacent to Commercial Bay. The 139-room property occupies a position steps from Princes Wharf and just four minutes on foot from Viaduct Harbour, with the Waitematā Harbour as the visual anchor for the hotel's most sought-after accommodations. Harbour View Rooms feature floor-to-ceiling windows framing the glistening expanse of the harbour, with automated blackout blinds that reveal the water at the press of a button. Club InterContinental delivers private check-in, personalised service, and access to a dedicated Club Lounge with harbour views. The hotel's signature restaurant, Advieh, was named New Zealand Hotel Restaurant of the Year 2024 at the HM Aotearoa-Pacific Awards. The property also holds Qualmark Sustainable Tourism certification.
Address: 1 Queen Street, Auckland CBD, Auckland 1010, New Zealand

Description ⚙: Park Hyatt Auckland is a haven where sophistication meets stunning vistas in Wynyard Quarter. East and south-facing rooms and suites offer extraordinary views of the city skyline and the iconic Sky Tower, while north-facing accommodations provide a picturesque outlook over the vibrant harbour, with the Auckland Harbour Bridge visible in the distance. The Harbour Suite and Executive Rooftop Suite are two of the most exquisite offerings among the hotel's 195 rooms. The hotel's Captain's Bar features an inviting terrace revealing fascinating Auckland vistas. Onemata restaurant — named New Zealand Hotel Restaurant of the Year at the 2025 HM Aotearoa-Pacific Awards — serves a seasonally driven menu celebrating the finest local ingredients. The 25-metre heated infinity pool overlooks Wynyard Quarter's marina. The Spa at Park Hyatt Auckland offers vitality pools and a full suite of bespoke wellness treatments.

Description ⚙: voco Auckland City Centre opened in 2022 as the brand's debut in New Zealand. The hotel boasts 201 spacious rooms and suites, each showcasing voco's signature warm style. Guests can opt for either sweeping harbour scenes or iconic Sky Tower vistas from their rooms, with the highest floor rooms between the 28th and 37th floors presenting the best perspectives. At the pinnacle of the hotel, 38 floors above the ground, Bar Albert is the highest hotel rooftop bar in New Zealand, offering uninterrupted views of Auckland's landmarks from the Sky Tower to the Waitematā Harbour. The hotel integrates eco-friendly elements including eco rain showers and environmentally conscious bedding.

Hotel Tagline: Sixteen storeys above Britomart, the Waitematā Harbour fills the northern horizon.
Summary: Sixteen storeys in Auckland’s Britomart precinct, with the Waitematā Harbour framing the view to the north. HI-SO, the rooftop bar, delivers the clearest harbour panorama from this quarter of the city; one floor below, Harbour Society restaurant puts the same water view beside the meal.
Description ⚙: Housed in a heritage building in Auckland's Britomart precinct, SO/ Auckland brings an avant-garde design philosophy to one of the most characterful sites in the CBD. Sixteen storeys of boldly conceived rooms and suites, each inspired by New Zealand's volcanic landscape, sweep upward to commanding views over the Waitematā Harbour and the city skyline. Selected suites include private balconies and separate lounge areas. The hotel's most dramatic view destination is HI-SO, the rooftop bar at the summit of the building, where cocktails are served against an unobstructed panorama of the harbour and the Auckland skyline. One floor below, Harbour Society serves reimagined Asian-inspired dishes with the Waitematā Harbour as a luminous backdrop. At street level, Javalab Café blends French patisserie craft with artisan coffee. SO/SPA offers a curated menu of treatments supported by an indoor heated pool.
Address: 67 Customs Street East, Auckland CBD, Auckland 1010, New Zealand
Nearest Airports: AKL · Auckland · 21 km

Hotel Tagline: From the upper floors, the Waitematā Harbour spreads across floor-to-ceiling windows above Auckland's CBD.
Summary: Linked by sky bridge to the SkyCity complex, the Grand Deluxe Harbour View Suites look north across the Waitematā from the upper floors. Higher rooms widen the panorama considerably. The East Day Spa and the full SkyCity entertainment precinct are within the same connected building.
Description ⚙: Found in the heart of Auckland's vibrant CBD, The Grand by SkyCity enjoys a prime location connected to the SkyCity Auckland complex through a sky bridge, where a range of entertainment options coexist including several restaurants, a convention center, and a casino. With floor-to-ceiling windows, this luxurious hotel offers enchanting vistas of the Waitematā Harbour and panoramic views of Auckland City. The most coveted accommodations are the Grand Deluxe Harbour View Suites, providing guests with the sensation of having Auckland at their feet. The East Day Spa offers guests a haven of tranquility and serenity. Guests can also partake in chauffeured day tours, rejuvenating spa treatments, or utilise the state-of-the-art fitness centre.

Nearest Airports: AKL · Auckland · 21 km

Hotel Tagline: From the sixth-floor rooftop, the harbour, marina, and Sky Tower spread across the skyline.
Summary: The sixth-floor Rooftop at QT earns the stay — a sweeping view over the harbour and the marina below, with the Sky Tower to the city side. A handful of suites offer partial Sky Tower glimpses in-room. Esther restaurant and the rooftop bar make this a complete view-and-dining combination.
Description ⚙: QT Auckland opened at the end of 2020, joining the ranks of its iconic counterparts in Queenstown and Wellington. True to its boutique style, even with a room count of 150, QT Auckland embraces a hospitality philosophy that celebrates uniqueness and flair. The sixth-floor rooftop generously showcases the harbour and the cityscape. QT Auckland boasts Esther, a Mediterranean-inspired restaurant that has swiftly claimed its spot as one of the top dining destinations in the city, and the Rooftop at QT bar with panoramic views over the harbour and marina. Perched near the Viaduct Harbour, QT Auckland offers easy access to Auckland's treasures including the Auckland Fish Market, Silo Park, and the Sky Tower.
Address: 4 Viaduct Harbour Avenue, Westhaven, Auckland 1010, New Zealand
Nearest Airports: AKL · Auckland · 21 km

Hotel Tagline: Sailboats rest in the Viaduct below, the waterfront promenade visible from marina-facing balconies.
Summary: Marina-facing balconies look directly onto the Viaduct — sailboats in the foreground, the waterfront promenade below. La Marée, the hotel’s French-inspired restaurant, carries the same outlook through lunch and dinner. The Club Millésime adds the private elevated component for breakfast.
Description ⚙: Sofitel Auckland Viaduct Harbour offers a sanctuary of tranquility and luxurious amenities. From room and suite balconies or the floor-to-ceiling windows, guests who book marina-facing accommodations are treated to soothing views of the glistening Viaduct Harbour. The Club Millésime confers executive breakfast, private check-in, and all-day access to food and beverages. The French Press café embodies classic café culture with buttery pastries, fresh baguettes, and distinctive crêpes. La Marée, subject to a revamp in 2023, is a French-inspired restaurant offering an exquisite dining experience with picturesque water views. The hotel's spa invites guests to indulge with a sauna, steam room, and two indoor pools. A Technogym-equipped gym faces the pool.
Address: 21 Viaduct Harbour Avenue, Auckland CBD, Auckland 1010, New Zealand
Nearest Airports: AKL · Auckland · 21 km

Hotel Tagline: Harbour water on three sides, the city left behind the moment you step aboard.
Summary: Built over a wharf, the Hilton puts the harbour on three sides — no other Auckland hotel positions guests this close to the water. Harbour-facing balconies amplify the ship-like character. The FISH restaurant faces the water directly, with natural light on both sides through lunch and dinner.
Description ⚙: Perched uniquely over a wharf, the Hilton Auckland offers guests a captivating sensation akin to being aboard a ship. The glistening waters of the harbour take centre stage, and harbour-facing rooms with balconies allow guests to fully embrace the setting. The FISH restaurant offers an awe-inspiring harbour view and serves the finest locally sourced seafood. A slender outdoor pool creates an illusion of merging with the harbour's waters. A scenic ferry ride to charming Devonport is available nearby, and an invigorating hike up Takarunga / Mount Victoria reveals mesmerising vistas of Auckland's spectacular skyline.
Address: 147 Quay Street, Auckland CBD, Auckland 1010, New Zealand
Nearest Airports: AKL · Auckland · 22 km

Hotel Tagline: Sky Tower and the harbour share the same panoramic frame from the balconies above Albert Park.
Summary: Once Auckland’s tallest residential tower, the Avani places the harbour, the Sky Tower, and the green expanse of Albert Park in the same frame — all visible from the panoramic balconies of the one- and two-bedroom suites. Upper floors widen each element into a fuller urban panorama.
Description ⚙: Once reigning as Auckland's tallest residential building until 2020, the Avani Metropolis Auckland Residences offers panoramic views that stretch across the Auckland skyline, Auckland Harbour, and the tranquil expanse of Albert Park. Many of the hotel's one and two-bedroom serviced suites come with expansive windows and panoramic balconies that showcase Auckland's natural and urban beauty, from its vast harbour to the iconic Sky Tower. This four-star property offers guests an indoor lap pool and a fully-equipped gym. Nearby attractions include Viaduct Harbour, the Sky Tower, Aotea Centre, and the Auckland Art Gallery.
Address: 40 Kitchener Street, Auckland CBD, Auckland 1010, New Zealand
Nearest Airports: AKL · Auckland · 21 km

Hotel Tagline: Princes Wharf fills the foreground, with the Waitematā opening wider the higher you climb.
Summary: Princes Wharf fills the foreground from the Harbour Suite floors — wall-to-wall windows and a direct line to the Waitematā beyond. Floor height matters here: the higher the room, the further the harbour opens. The Viaduct dining and nightlife precinct is immediately across the street.
Description ⚙: M Social Hotel Auckland seamlessly complements the vibrant views of Princes Wharf, a hub of dining, shopping, and nightlife activities just across the street. The hotel's Harbour Suite category, all located on the upper floors, offers a perfect blend of functionality, space, and breathtaking water views framed by wall-to-wall windows. The signature restaurant, Beast & Butterflies, offers a delightful array of international cuisine on the waterfront. An equipped fitness centre caters to wellness enthusiasts. Leisure travellers are surrounded by iconic landmarks including the New Zealand Maritime Museum, the lively Viaduct Harbour, the iconic Sky Tower, and the Auckland Art Gallery Toi o Tamaki.

Nearest Airports: AKL · Auckland · 21 km

Hotel Tagline: The Sky Tower fills the frame from the Junior King Suite's balcony terrace.
Summary: At the Junior King Suite with Balcony terrace, the Sky Tower fills the frame — the Club Lounge on the upper floors delivers the same sightline for guests in standard rooms. Five dining venues within the building, from Ember’s bistro fare to Katsura’s teppanyaki, cover most occasions.
Description ⚙: Grand Millennium Auckland is a QualMark five-star-rated hotel offering 452 rooms and suites with floor-to-ceiling windows. The Junior King Suite with Balcony frames the Sky Tower directly to the north; the Club Lounge on the upper floors reproduces the same view for standard room guests. Five distinct dining venues include Ember Restaurant, a relaxed bistro using seasonal New Zealand produce; Katsura Japanese Cuisine, serving teppanyaki and sashimi since 1989; Estelle's, a Mediterranean-inspired wine and cocktail lounge; and The Aviary, the hotel's lobby bar. The Grand Ballroom was relaunched in March 2024 following an extensive refurbishment with a custom lighting sculpture by James Russ Studio. Facilities include a heated indoor swimming pool and a sauna.
Address: 71 Mayoral Drive, Cnr Vincent Street, Auckland CBD, Auckland 1010, New Zealand
Nearest Airports: AKL · Auckland · 20 km

Hotel Tagline: From Queen Street, the Sky Tower fills the window close enough to read its full structure.
Summary: On Queen Street, the Sky Tower is close enough to read in detail from the room — the tower-facing suites deliver a clean, head-on sightline with the full structure in frame. The Churchill Bar on the 20th floor broadens the same view into a city panorama above the surrounding roofline.
Description ⚙: Placed in the heart of Auckland's Queen Street, the Four Points by Sheraton Auckland hotel offers a blend of prime location, contemporary elegance, and modern comfort. The expansive city views are nothing short of spectacular; the Sky Tower rooms and suites are ideal for those wanting that postcard-perfect view from the privacy of their quarters. The Queens Head Bar & Eatery offers a delectable array of international cuisines. The Churchill Bar on the 20th floor provides panoramic views over Auckland and is among the city's highest rooftop bar experiences. The hotel's location ensures guests are always close to the pulse of Auckland's vibrant offerings, with upscale shopping and fine dining on Queen Street.
Address: 396 Queen Street, Main Reception Entrance 110 Mayoral Drive, Auckland CBD, Auckland 1010, New Zealand
Nearest Airports: AKL · Auckland · 20 km

Hotel Tagline: From corner rooms above the 20th floor, Albert Park, the Sky Tower, and Waitematā Harbour align in one unbroken view.
Summary: Rooms and suites on the 20th floor and above unlock the full view sequence — Albert Park, the Sky Tower, and the Waitematā Harbour simultaneously visible from corner positions. The Club Lounge on level 28 consolidates the panorama into a single space, with canapés and drinks each evening.
Description ⚙: Crowne Plaza Auckland by IHG effortlessly combines first-rate facilities and services for business and leisure guests. Rooms and suites on the 20th floor and above provide stunning vistas of Auckland City, Albert Park, and the Waitematā Harbour. The Club Lounge on level 28 consolidates the panorama into a single elevated space with canapes and drinks each evening. Guests enjoy complimentary access to the fitness centre and can unwind in the on-site sauna and spa. Aria Bar & Restaurant beckons with its delectable meals and world-class cocktails. For eco-friendly exploration, guests can also rent electric vehicles directly at the hotel through the Cityhop initiative.
Address: 128 Albert Street, Auckland CBD, Auckland 1010, New Zealand
Nearest Airports: AKL · Auckland · 21 km

Hotel Tagline: Private terraces hold the Sky Tower east, the harbour north, and Victoria Park directly below.
Summary: Every SuperSuite comes with a private terrace — the Sky Tower to the east, the harbour to the north, and Victoria Park spread directly below. Full kitchens in every unit make longer stays considerably more practical than a hotel room at a comparable price point in this part of the city.
Description ⚙: The Swiss-Belsuites Victoria Park Auckland provides a luxurious haven five minutes from the Viaduct Harbour and SkyCity Casino. The SuperSuites deliver the best views, with private terraces offering the Sky Tower to the east, the harbour to the north, and Victoria Park spread directly below. Each suite features top-tier modern finishes and a fully equipped kitchen. The hotel offers a sauna and a dining area serving international and local cuisines. Nearby, guests can explore the Sky Tower, shop on Queen Street or K'Road, and enjoy Victoria Park Market.

Hotel Tagline: From a corner on Princes Street, harbour and skyline share the frame above Albert Park's canopy.
Summary: A corner position on Princes Street produces the view combination that distinguishes this property — urban skyline and harbour simultaneously in frame, with Albert Park’s tree canopy adding green to the foreground. The One Bedroom Deluxe Apartment pairs this outlook with a private balcony.
Description ⚙: The luxurious Pullman Auckland Hotel & Apartments is situated in the heart of Auckland's CBD overlooking the picturesque Albert Park. Offering mixed urban and sea views from its corner position, the One Bedroom Deluxe Apartment includes a private balcony and is the perfect option for longer stays. Guests can indulge in international cuisines at the on-site Tapestry Dining restaurant and unwind with a refreshing cocktail at the Tapestry Bar & Terrace. The hotel provides babysitting upon request, an indoor pool and spa, and is in close proximity to landmarks such as Viaduct Harbour, Auckland Art Gallery, Auckland Waterfront, and Auckland Zoo.

BODA restaurant on the 13th floor lifts the same panorama to table height for dinner.
Description ⚙: Situated along the picturesque banks of Auckland's Waterfront district and rebranded in 2022, the Mövenpick Hotel Auckland offers captivating views of sailboats and yachts gracefully gliding across the harbour, with the iconic Auckland skyline adorning the horizon. All rooms and suites categorised as ocean view deliver wonderful panoramic vistas from their windows. Enjoying exceptional views continues at BODA, the hotel's Korean fusion restaurant on the 13th floor. Encircled by upscale shopping at the nearby Commercial Bay Mall, the hotel is an enchanting choice for group travellers and couples. Prominent nearby landmarks include the Sky Tower, Aotea Centre, K'Road, and the Auckland Harbour Bridge.
Address: 8 Customs Street East, Auckland CBD, Auckland 1010, New Zealand
Nearest Airports: AKL · Auckland · 21 km

Description ⚙: Reigning over Jimbaran Bay with a 57-meter beachfront infinity pool that runs parallel to the sand, Four Seasons Resort Bali at Jimbaran Bay is one of the most storied luxury resorts on the island. The resort's 147 villas are arranged across a terraced hillside, each with a private pool and a direct or oblique view of the Indian Ocean. The Imperial Villa, at the pinnacle of the property, frames three bedrooms, adult and kids pools, and an unbroken ocean panorama.

Summary: Sixteen open-air bales with no partition between bed and jungle — the Ayung River, a cascading waterfall, Buahan Valley, and seven mountain peaks fill a 180-degree sightline. Curtains are available for guests who want them. Adults only, northern Ubud.
Description ⚙: Perched in the heart of Bali's lush jungle in northern Ubud, Buahan, a Banyan Tree Escape, is an adults-only retreat built entirely without walls. Sixteen open-air bales face the Ayung River, a cascading waterfall, Buahan Valley, and seven mountain peaks across a 180-degree sightline. The design, conceived in collaboration with local architect Gede Kresna, eliminates the barrier between the sleeping space and the landscape entirely — curtains are available for guests who want them.
Address: Buahan Kaja, Payangan, Gianyar Regency, Bali 80572, Indonesia
Nearest Airports: DPS · Ngurah Rai · 46 km

Description ⚙: Perched on the southern edge of the Bukit Peninsula, The Ungasan Clifftop Resort commands sweeping views of the Indian Ocean from its clifftop position. The One Bedroom Ocean View Suite at the cliff edge pairs a private pool with a full coastal panorama.

Description ⚙: Set at high altitude in North Bali's coffee country, Munduk Moding Plantation Nature Resort & Spa offers an infinity pool that appears to hover above the cloud line, with the island's northern volcanoes and the Bali Sea filling the horizon. The working coffee plantation on site produces some of the most cited beans in North Bali. The Istana Negara Presidential Suite adds a heated infinity pool at the same altitude.

The resort is undergoing a full transformation in 2026 and is scheduled to reopen in August 2026.

Hotel Tagline: Perched 150 meters above the Indian Ocean, where the Bukit Peninsula cliffs meet open water.
Summary: 150 meters above the Indian Ocean on the Bukit Peninsula cliffs, with private beach access via a dedicated inclinator. The fine dining case is Il Ristorante — Niko Romito: tasting menus by the three-Michelin-star chef, served against an unobstructed ocean backdrop.
Description ⚙: Reigning over Bali's hospitality scene from 150 meters above sea level, the Bvlgari Resort Bali offers an unbeaten vantage point over the Indian Ocean. Every accommodation is an individual villa or mansion, ranging from 300 to 4,415 square meters. The cliff-edge Il Ristorante – Niko Romito delivers tasting menus by the three-Michelin-star chef, served against an unobstructed ocean panorama. Private beach access is via a dedicated inclinator.
Address: Jl. Goa Lempeh, Pecatu, Kec. Kuta Sel., Kabupaten Badung, Bali, Indonesia
Nearest Airports: DPS · Ngurah Rai · 17 km

Hotel Tagline: From a protected ridge, the Valley of the Kings disappears into guaranteed jungle as far as you can see.
Summary: Forty heated pool villas on a ridge above the Valley of the Kings, on legally protected land — the jungle views are guaranteed. Upper tiers for the deepest sightline. Apéritif Restaurant holds a MICHELIN Key and Best Wine Selection in Asia award (2024).
Description ⚙: Forty heated pool villas crowning a protected ridge above the Valley of the Kings — Viceroy Bali's jungle views are legally guaranteed against future development. Recognised as the World's No.1 Resort by Condé Nast Traveler Readers' Choice Awards in 2018, and holding a MICHELIN Key alongside a Forbes Five-Star rating for three consecutive years through 2025. Apéritif Restaurant & Bar won Best Wine Selection in Asia at the World Luxury Hotel Awards in 2024.
Address: Br. Nagi, Jl. Lanyahan, Petulu, Kecamatan Ubud, Kabupaten Gianyar, Bali 80571, Indonesia
Nearest Airports: DPS · Ngurah Rai · 43 km

Hotel Tagline: Ocean panoramas stretch across the Kuta Peninsula's southern coast from every private pool.
Summary: 123 villas, all with private pools and ocean panoramas over the Kuta Peninsula’s southern coast. Sunset Villas and Ocean Villas are the clearest propositions. At cliff-top Akasa, flame-grilled Asian cuisine pairs with one of the longer sunset sightlines in Uluwatu.
Description ⚙: 123 villas, all with private pools and ocean panoramas over the Kuta Peninsula's southern coast. Sunset Villas and Ocean Villas deliver the clearest sightlines. At cliff-top Akasa restaurant, flame-grilled Asian cuisine pairs with one of the longer sunset views in Uluwatu.
Address: Kawasan Pecatu Indah Resort, Jl. New Kuta Beach, Pecatu, Kec. Kuta, Kabupaten Badung, Bali 80361, Indonesia
Nearest Airports: DPS · Ngurah Rai · 16 km

Hotel Tagline: Above the forest canopy, Mount Agung and active Mount Batur rise from every suite and villa.
Summary: Mount Agung and the active Mount Batur appear above the forest ridge from all 85 suites and villas — the clearest view of both volcanoes from any luxury property near Ubud. Opened October 2024. Lower-terrace villas give the deepest jungle frame in the foreground.
Description ⚙: Opened in October 2024 in the jungle highlands of Payangan, Anantara Ubud Bali Resort positions all 85 suites and pool villas to face the forest ridge where Mount Agung and the active Mount Batur appear above the canopy — the most direct view of both volcanoes from any luxury property near Ubud. Two heated communal infinity pools supplement each villa's private pool. The Amerta restaurant is built around open-fire cooking with hyperlocal sourcing.
Address: Jl. Raya Puhu Payangan No.88, Puhu, Kec. Payangan, Kabupaten Gianyar, Bali 80572, Indonesia
Nearest Airports: DPS · Ngurah Rai · 50 km

Hotel Tagline: Pool terraces extend to the valley edge, the rice terraces of Tegallalang filling the view below.
Summary: Twenty-four private pool villas positioned directly above the Oos River Valley, with the rice terraces of Tegallalang forming the view rather than serving as backdrop. The pool terraces extend to the valley edge. Light on the terraces shifts significantly at golden hour.
Description ⚙: Twenty-four private pool villas positioned directly above the Oos River Valley in Tegallalang, with the rice terraces forming the view from every villa terrace rather than serving as distant backdrop. The pool terraces extend to the valley edge. Light on the terraces shifts significantly at golden hour, making the late afternoon the strongest time for views from the property.
Address: Kelabang Moding, Tegallalang, Kec. Tegallalang, Kabupaten Gianyar, Bali 80571, Indonesia

Hotel Tagline: Every room balcony looks out over the same Payangan River Valley and bamboo forests below.
Summary: 148 rooms with private balconies over the Payangan River Valley and bamboo forests — one of the few five-star properties in the area where every room category shares the same valley exposure. The infinity pool mirrors the same panorama at a longer horizontal scale.
Description ⚙: 148 rooms and suites with private balconies over the Payangan River Valley and bamboo forests — one of the few five-star properties in the Ubud area where every room category shares the same valley exposure. The infinity pool mirrors the same panorama at a longer horizontal scale. The signature Nest dining pod, woven from bamboo and elevated above the rice fields, is one of the more unusual dining positions in the region.

Hotel Tagline: Stilts over a jungle ravine, with only forest canopy and the sound of the valley below.
Summary: Rooms and villas on stilts over a jungle ravine above the Ayung River — no beach, no rice terrace: only the forest canopy and the sound of the valley below. The infinity pool sits where two rivers converge. Architecture by Kerry Hill; 15 minutes from central Ubud.
Description ⚙: Rooms and villas suspended on stilts over a jungle ravine above the Ayung River in Payangan, 15 minutes from central Ubud. The infinity pool sits at the convergence of two rivers, overlooking only forest canopy and the sound of the valley below. There is no beach or rice terrace view here — the experience is entirely immersive jungle. The architecture was designed by Australian firm Kerry Hill.
Address: Melinggih Kelod, Ubud, Gianyar Regency, Bali 80572, Indonesia
Nearest Airports: DPS · Ngurah Rai · 47 km

Summary: Set against the Sidemen hillside with Mount Agung framing the view and rice paddies at ground level — one of the clearest positions for Bali’s highest volcano available from a luxury property. The open-air Sleeping Gajah Kitchen overlooks the same ridge.
Description ⚙: Set against the Sidemen hillside with Mount Agung framing the view and rice paddies at ground level, Wapa di Ume Sidemen offers one of the clearest positions for Bali's highest volcano available from a luxury property. The private villa infinity pools face the same ridge as the open-air Sleeping Gajah Kitchen restaurant.
Address: Banjar Dinas, Jl. Raya Tebola, Sangkan Gn., Kec. Sidemen, Kabupaten Karangasem, Bali 80864, Indonesia

Summary: Three stacked infinity pools modeled on the contours of Tegalalang, each tier opening toward a jungle peak. Adults-only, 30 minutes north of Ubud in Bresela village — a stretch of the highlands noticeably quieter than the valley below. Honeymooner-rated property.
Description ⚙: Three stacked infinity pools modeled on the contours of Tegalalang, each tier opening toward a jungle peak. The Kayon Jungle Resort is adults-only, set in Bresela village 30 minutes north of Ubud — a stretch of the highlands noticeably quieter than the valley below. Consistently rated among Ubud's most highly regarded honeymoon properties.
Address: Br, Bresela, Kec. Payangan, Kabupaten Gianyar, Bali 80572, Indonesia

Summary: Set on the eastern hillside above Virgin Beach, with the Indian Ocean and Mount Agung visible simultaneously from the panoramic infinity pool — one of the more unexpected dual views on the island. A 2022 opening, boutique in scale, eastern Bali in character.
Description ⚙: Set on the eastern hillside above Virgin Beach near Manggis, Neano Escape opened in 2022 as a boutique property where the panoramic infinity pool simultaneously frames the Indian Ocean and Mount Agung — one of the more unexpected dual views on the island. Eastern Bali in character: quieter, less visited, and positioned away from the main tourist circuits.

Hotel Tagline: Every one of the 101 balconies faces the Chao Phraya River directly.
Summary: All 101 rooms and villas face the Chao Phraya from private balconies or terraces — the entry-level Riverfront Premier starts at 61 sqm, the villas add a jacuzzi at water’s edge. Côte by Mauro Colagreco handles the riverside dining. Named the world’s best hotel in 2024. We’d book floors 5–10 for the widest unobstructed angle.
Description ⚙: Capella Bangkok occupies the Charoen Krung bank of the Chao Phraya River with all 101 suites and villas facing the water from private balconies or terraces — the entry-level Riverfront Premier starts at 61 square metres, riverside villas add a jacuzzi at water's edge. Côte by Mauro Colagreco handles riverside dining. Named the world's best hotel in 2024.
Address: 300 2 ถ. เจริญกรุง Khwaeng Yan Nawa, Khet Sathon, Krung Thep Maha Nakhon 10120, Thailand

Summary: All 257 rooms in the rebuilt Dusit Thani — reopened September 2024 after a USD 1.37 billion reconstruction — face Lumpini Park through six-metre cantilevered windows designed by André Fu Studio. The room to ask for is a Club Corner on the upper floors, where the city opens beyond the tree canopy.
Description ⚙: Dusit Thani Bangkok reopened in September 2024 after a USD 1.37 billion reconstruction. All 257 rooms face Lumpini Park through six-metre cantilevered windows designed by André Fu Studio. The hotel features an infinity pool with park views and the 39th-floor Club lounge with panoramic city and park outlooks.

Hotel Tagline: Balconies in the State Rooms hang directly over the Chao Phraya River.
Summary: The Chao Phraya runs below the State Rooms’ balconies, with teak panels and Jim Thompson silks inside. Le Normandie — now in a new chapter under Anne-Sophie Pic since September 2025 — frames the same river at dinner. We’d request a high floor in the Garden Wing and stay for the Bamboo Bar jazz.
Description ⚙: The Mandarin Oriental Bangkok has occupied the Chao Phraya River bank since 1876. State Rooms feature teak panelling and Jim Thompson silks with balconies facing the river at close range. Le Normandie — under chef Anne-Sophie Pic since September 2025 — frames the same river at dinner. The Bamboo Bar is one of Bangkok's longest-established jazz venues.
Address: 48 Oriental Ave, Khwaeng Bang Rak, Khet Bang Rak, Krung Thep Maha Nakhon 10500, Thailand
Nearest Airports: DMK · Don Mueang · 28 km; BKK · Suvarnabhumi · 37 km

River-front terraces start at the standard rooms, and the private pier puts the rest of Bangkok within shuttle distance. Worth staying for the Riva del Fiume dinner with the river alongside.
Description ⚙: Four Seasons Hotel Bangkok at Chao Phraya River delivers the full riverside resort experience — river-front terraces begin at the standard rooms, and the three-bedroom penthouse suite adds a private rooftop pool above the water. The hotel's private pier connects directly to the rest of Bangkok by river shuttle. Riva del Fiume restaurant faces the Chao Phraya at dinner.

Inside Central Embassy, ten minutes from three major malls.
Description ⚙: Park Hyatt Bangkok occupies floors 8 to 36 of Central Embassy in the Pathum Wan embassy corridor. King Corner rooms deliver 180-degree city views through floor-to-ceiling glass. The four-level Penthouse Bar and Grill at floors 34 to 36 extends the panorama into the evening. A 40-metre saltwater pool on the 9th floor holds the same skyline at midday.

Corner suites on the highest floors frame both the Mediterranean and the Barcelona skyline simultaneously. Enoteca Paco Pérez, the two-Michelin-starred restaurant, is located within the hotel.

Hotel Tagline: The rooftop pool frames the Palau Reial Major and Santa Maria del Mar above the Gothic Quarter.
Summary: The infinity pool on the rooftop frames the Palau Reial Major and the Basilica of Santa Maria del Mar — genuinely one of the best-positioned pools in the city. The fifth-floor Grand Suite reads like a luxury apartment. Worth staying for both the rooftop and the position between El Born and the Gothic Quarter.
Description ⚙: Formerly an apartment complex from the 1920s, the hotel is centrally located between El Born and the Gothic Quarter. The rooftop infinity pool frames the Palau Reial Major and the Basilica of Santa Maria del Mar. The fifth-floor Grand Suite reads like a luxury apartment with views of Barcelona's most relevant medieval complex.
Address: Via Laietana, 30, Ciutat Vella, 08003 Barcelona, Spain
Nearest Airports: BCN · El Prat · 16 km

Hotel Tagline: From the Tibidabo ridge, Barcelona and the Mediterranean stretch below for five hundred metres of open air.
Summary: Perched on the Tibidabo ridge 500 metres above the city, the hotel delivers a sustained aerial view of Barcelona and the Mediterranean that no hotel in the centre can match. Room 208 is the corner unit that guests return to specifically. The 37-metre lap pool looks out over the same panorama.
Description ⚙: Perched on the Tibidabo ridge 500 metres above the city, the hotel delivers a sustained aerial view of Barcelona and the Mediterranean that no hotel in the centre can match. All dining venues sit above the city. The 37-metre lap pool looks out over the same panorama. The hotel was conceived by Doctor Andreu in the early twentieth century.
Address: Ctra. de Vallvidrera al Tibidabo, 83-93, Sarrià-Sant Gervasi, 08035 Barcelona, Spain
Nearest Airports: BCN · El Prat · 24 km

Hotel Tagline: From the 173 Rooftop Terrace, the Sagrada Família anchors a full 360-degree panorama.
Summary: The 173 Rooftop Terrace delivers a full 360-degree read: the Sagrada Família, the Montjuïc cable car, the Torre de Collserola, and the Cathedral are all visible from the pool deck. The hotel sits in the quieter Montjuïc corridor — a Michelin-starred kitchen below, the Magic Fountain ten minutes on foot.
Description ⚙: Located in the Montjuïc corridor, the 173 Rooftop Terrace delivers a 360-degree panorama: the Sagrada Família, the Montjuïc cable car, the Torre de Collserola, and the Cathedral of Barcelona are all visible from the pool deck. The hotel is steps from the Magic Fountain of Montjuïc.
Address: Av. de Rius i Taulet, 1-3, Sants-Montjuïc, 08004 Barcelona, Spain
Nearest Airports: BCN · El Prat · 13 km

Hotel Tagline: Ninth-floor terraces hold the Sagrada Família in direct sightlines, between both Gaudí buildings on the Passeig de Gràcia.
Summary: The ninth-floor penthouses are the tier to request: expansive private terraces, direct sightlines to the Sagrada Família, and a position on the Passeig de Gràcia between both Gaüdí buildings. La Dolce Vitae rooftop is open to all hotel guests — the penthouses add the terrace that the lower floors miss.
Description ⚙: Positioned on the Passeig de Gràcia between both Gaudí buildings, the hotel's ninth-floor penthouses feature expansive terraces with direct sightlines to the Sagrada Família. The La Dolce Vitae rooftop terrace is open to all guests. The hotel opened in 1918 and was subject to extensive renovation in 2013.
Address: Pg. de Gràcia, 68, Eixample, 08007 Barcelona, Spain
Nearest Airports: BCN · El Prat · 16 km

Hotel Tagline: From the rooftop lap pool, Barcelona spreads out beneath an open city sky.
Summary: The heated rooftop lap pool sits above a hotel that converted from a 1970s office building — it runs year-round. The top-floor suite adds a private terrace with city views. Xerta Restaurant below dedicates its menu entirely to the produce of the Terres de l’Ebre delta — rice, seafood, and seasonal delta ingredients.
Description ⚙: A boutique hotel converted from a 1970s office building, designed by local architect Daniel Isern. The rooftop lap pool runs year-round with heated water. Xerta Restaurant on the ground floor showcases the cuisine of the Terres de l'Ebre delta region. Kintsugi, a Japanese restaurant using the same local produce, offers an Omakase menu. The hotel holds a MICHELIN Key for excellence in hospitality.
Address: Carrer de Còrsega, 289, Eixample, 08008 Barcelona, Spain
Nearest Airports: BCN · El Prat · 16 km

Hotel Tagline: From the rooftop pool, Gaudí's towers rise clear above the Eixample grid.
Summary: The rooftop pool commands a direct sightline to the Sagrada Família above the Eixample grid — one of the clearest reads of Gaüdí’s towers from a hotel pool in the city. Deluxe Terrace rooms have private balconies over Plaça de Catalunya. The hotel opens for stays from August 2026 under the BLESS Collection brand.
Description ⚙: At Plaça de Catalunya, the BLESS Hotel Barcelona opens for stays from August 2026 under the BLESS Collection Hotels brand by Palladium Hotel Group. The rooftop pool commands a direct sightline to the Sagrada Família above the Eixample grid. Deluxe Terrace rooms have private balconies over Plaça de Catalunya.
Address: Pl. de Catalunya, 10, Eixample, 08002 Barcelona, Spain
Nearest Airports: BCN · El Prat · 15 km

Hotel Tagline: Rooftop water and Port Vell's marina meet at the same sightline, with Rambla de Mar below.
Summary: The rooftop pool looks directly over the Port Vell marina and the palm-lined Rambla de Mar. The Grand Suite Mediterraneo is the suite worth asking for — standalone bathtub, private dining room, and a sea-view terrace. The Informal restaurant has earned the hotel a reputation for Catalan cooking that extends beyond the room count of 28.
Description ⚙: A five-star boutique hotel at the edge of the Gothic Quarter, drawn by guests worldwide for its rooftop pool overlooking the Port Vell marina and the palm-lined Rambla de Mar. The Grand Suite Mediterraneo features a standalone bathtub, private dining room, and sea-view terrace. The Informal restaurant specialises in contemporary Catalan cuisine.
Address: Pg. de Colom, 9, Ciutat Vella, 08002 Barcelona, Spain
Nearest Airports: BCN · El Prat · 16 km

Hotel Tagline: From the eighth floor, the Eixample roofline stretches toward Tibidabo.
Summary: Azimuth, on the eighth floor, pairs tapas and cocktails with a view across the Eixample roofline toward Tibidabo. The penthouse suites step above even that — ask for one when the terrace view is the priority over everything else. The hotel’s facade of square windows is one of the most recognisable on Gran Via.
Description ⚙: A five-star boutique hotel on the Gran Via distinguished by its ultra-modern facade of square windows. The Azimuth rooftop terrace on the eighth floor pairs tapas and cocktails with views across the Eixample roofline. Penthouse suites add a private terrace above the rooftop level.
Address: 619-621, Gran Via de les Corts Catalanes, Eixample, 08007 Barcelona, Spain
Nearest Airports: BCN · El Prat · 16 km

Hotel Tagline: From the rooftop, the Passeig de Gràcia stretches to the horizon where the Sagrada Família rises.
Summary: The Mirador Casa Fuster rooftop bar reaches the full length of the Passeig de Gràcia, with the Sagrada Família and the Tibidabo mountain visible on the horizon. The building itself, designed by Lluís Domènech i Montaner in 1908, is a Modernisme landmark. Balcony suites face the avenue directly from the room.
Description ⚙: A Modernisme landmark designed by Lluís Domènech i Montaner in 1908, extensively renovated in 2004. The Mirador Casa Fuster rooftop bar is one of the most celebrated in the city, offering clear views of the full extension of the Passeig de Gràcia with the Sagrada Família and Tibidabo on the horizon. Some suites have private balconies facing the avenue.
Address: Pg. de Gràcia, 132, Gràcia, 08008 Barcelona, Spain
Nearest Airports: BCN · El Prat · 18 km

Hotel Tagline: From the rooftop pool, the Sagrada Família and Torre Glòries share the same skyline.
Summary: The rooftop pool and bar frame both the Sagrada Família and the angular Torre Glòries from a 4-star address a short walk from Plaza Cataluña. Some rooms have private terraces with direct Sagrada Família vistas. A reliable option for the sightline at a price point below the five-star properties on the same grid.
Description ⚙: A four-star hotel housed in a heritage building close to Plaza de Catalunya. The rooftop pool and bar frame both the Sagrada Família and the angular Torre Glòries. Some rooms have private terraces with direct Sagrada Família vistas.
Address: Rda. de Sant Pere, 32, Eixample, 08010 Barcelona, Spain

Hotel Tagline: At night, the Eixample grid glows from the Twenty One rooftop terrace.
Summary: The Twenty One rooftop terrace is the hotel’s focal point — city centre views over the Eixample grid that hit best at night, when the avenues below glow. The rooms are compact but the address, between Plaza de Catalunya and La Rambla, is hard to beat for those who want the centre without the five-star price.
Description ⚙: A four-star hotel between Plaza de Catalunya and La Rambla. The Twenty One rooftop terrace delivers city centre views particularly striking at night. The hotel is an accessible option for guests who want the Eixample address at a four-star price.
Address: Ronda de la Univ., 21, Eixample, 08007 Barcelona, Spain
Nearest Airports: BCN · El Prat · 15 km

Hotel Tagline: Directly opposite La Pedrera, close enough to read Gaudí's stone detail from the balcony.
Summary: Superior rooms with balconies face La Pedrera-Casa Milà directly across the street — close enough to read Gaüdí’s stone detail from the room itself. Alaire, the eighth-floor terrace bar, extends that view to the illuminated facade after dark. The rooftop houses a small infinity pool with open city views.
Description ⚙: Housed in the 1872 Casa Daurella, positioned directly opposite La Pedrera-Casa Milà on the Passeig de Gràcia. Superior rooms with balconies face the Gaudí facade at close range. Alaire, the eighth-floor terrace bar, extends the view to include the illuminated La Pedrera after dark. The rooftop houses a small infinity pool.
View Subjects: La Pedrera (Casa Milà), Passeig de Gràcia
Address: Pg. de Gràcia, 73, Eixample, 08008 Barcelona, Spain
Nearest Airports: BCN · El Prat · 16 km

Hotel Tagline: Sixth-floor balconies look directly across Las Ramblas to the Church of Bethlehem.
Summary: The sixth-floor units have balconies and terraces above Las Ramblas, looking across to the Church of Bethlehem on the adjacent corner. The rooftop infinity pool and tapas bar add a sky-level read of the surrounding roofscape. Thirty-one rooms — one of the smallest footprints on La Rambla, which is part of the appeal.

Hotel Tagline: Above Las Ramblas, the rooftop pool sits high enough that the street noise disappears.
Summary: La Isabela, the rooftop terrace, sets a pool with sun loungers above Las Ramblas — the noise from the street below disappears at that height. The Colonial Suite brings a private terrace with a plunge pool. The building started as a tobacco factory in the nineteenth century and converted to a hotel in 2005.
Description ⚙: A historic tobacco factory from the nineteenth century, converted to a hotel in 2005. La Isabela, the rooftop terrace, has a pool with sun loungers set above Las Ramblas, where the noise from the street below disappears. The Colonial Suite offers a private terrace with a plunge pool.

Hotel Tagline: Rooftop hot tub, medieval Barcelona below, the Palau Reial Major directly opposite.
Summary: The Sunset Lounge Rooftop has a panoramic hot tub with views across the medieval rooftops of Barcelona’s historic centre, with the Palau Reial Major directly opposite. The city view rooms are the ones to request. Eighty-eight rooms, opposite Jaume I metro, with El Born and the Gothic Quarter immediately on foot.
Description ⚙: A boutique hotel in Santa Caterina, opposite the Palau Reial Major and a short walk from the Gothic Quarter and El Born. The Sunset Lounge Rooftop has a panoramic hot tub with views across Barcelona's medieval rooftops and the Cathedral. City view rooms are the preferred booking.
Address: Via Laietana, 24, Ciutat Vella, 08003 Barcelona, Spain

Hotel Tagline: From the highest terrace in the Eixample, Sagrada Família, Montjuïc, and the Mediterranean spread before you.
Summary: The 83.3 Terrace Bar is the highest hotel terrace in the Eixample — open year-round, with sightlines sweeping from the Sagrada Família to the hill of Montjuïc and the Mediterranean Sea on clear days. Upper rooms and suites face the Sagrada Família. La Pedrera-Casa Milà sits on the next corner.
Description ⚙: The 83.3 Terrace Bar is the highest hotel terrace in the Eixample, open year-round, with sightlines sweeping from the Sagrada Família to the hill of Montjuïc and the Mediterranean Sea on clear days. Upper rooms and suites face the Sagrada Família, and La Pedrera-Casa Milà stands on the adjacent corner.

Hotel Tagline: Port Vell marina stretches out from the waterfront edge of the Gothic Quarter.
Summary: A 19th-century palace at the waterfront edge of the Gothic Quarter, steps from the Port Vell marina. La Teressa del Duquesa rooftop is worth a visit independently — dusk over the port, candlelit tables, a small pool for the warmer months. Ask for a balcony room for the sea-facing orientation from the room itself.
Description ⚙: A 19th-century palace at the waterfront edge of the Gothic Quarter, steps from the Port Vell marina. La Teressa del Duquesa rooftop terrace offers dusk views over the port and marina. A small rooftop pool is available in the warmer months. Balcony rooms provide sea-facing orientation from the room.

Hotel Tagline: Balconies on the top floor open directly over the full breadth of Plaza de Catalunya.
Summary: Top-floor rooms have private balconies presiding over the full breadth of Plaza de Catalunya — a view that fills the room from the moment the blinds open. The Nineteen restaurant sits within walls shared with the 12th-century Santa Anna Church. The square is the most usefully central address in Barcelona.
Description ⚙: Top-floor rooms have private balconies presiding over the full breadth of Plaza de Catalunya — a view that fills the room from the moment the blinds open. The Nineteen restaurant shares its walls with the 12th-century Santa Anna Church. The square is the most usefully central address in Barcelona.

Summary: Ask for the Jen 75 Skyline Plus or the JEN 150 Suite — both place the CCTV Headquarters and China World Trade Center in the window. San Wu Tang, the all-day restaurant, serves the same sightline from an open kitchen directly below, and does it well enough to justify a dinner reservation from outside.

Hotel Tagline: From the 80th floor, the Forbidden City appears at the top of the frame.
Summary: Rooms occupy floors 64 to 80, making this the only Beijing hotel where every guest is already above the city. The Premier Suite aligns a standalone bathtub with the horizon; Atmosphere on the 80th floor extends the same perspective to dinner, with the Forbidden City visible from the top of the frame.
Description ⚙: China World Summit Wing, Beijing occupies floors 64 to 80 of the China World Tower, the tallest section of the China World Complex on Jianguomenwai Avenue, Chaoyang. Every room in the hotel is above the surrounding city; the Premier Suite aligns a standalone bathtub with the Beijing skyline. Dining venues include Grill 79, Atmosphere on the 80th floor, and The Lounge — all sharing the panoramic outlook. The hotel's 25-metre indoor heated pool creates an impression of floating above Beijing. The Forbidden City is visible on the far horizon from upper floors.
Address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Chao Yang Qu, 建国门外大街1号 邮政编码: 100020
Nearest Airports: PEK · Capital · 25 km; NAY · Nanyuan · 28 km; PKX · Daxing · 61 km

Hotel Tagline: Chaise lounges face the CCTV Headquarters directly, the pool holds the same sightline from the 6th floor.
Summary: The Premier Rooms are the ones to request — by-the-window chaise lounges face the CCTV Headquarters directly, and the 6th-floor indoor pool beneath its glass ceiling holds the same sightline. The Sense Spa’s Sand & Wood treatment, based on centuries-old Chinese medicine, is worth building in.
Description ⚙: Rosewood Beijing is a 283-room five-star hotel in the Jing Guang Centre, Hujialou, Chaoyang. The property combines Chinese cultural references with a contemporary design and faces the CCTV Headquarters, which is visible from Premier Rooms equipped with by-the-window chaise lounges. The 6th-floor indoor pool operates beneath a high glass ceiling with city views. The Sense Spa offers the Sand and Wood treatment, based on traditional Chinese medicine techniques. Rosewood Beijing is one of the closest luxury hotels to the CCTV site in the Chaoyang business district.
Address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Chao Yang Qu, Chao Yang Men Wai Da Jie, 1号京广中心 邮政编码: 100020
Nearest Airports: PEK · Capital · 24 km; NAY · Nanyuan · 28 km; PKX · Daxing · 62 km

Hotel Tagline: From the rooftop terrace, the Forbidden City spreads across the skyline at dusk.
Summary: Forbidden City views frame many of the 73 rooms — the Premier Suite has them best. The MO Bar rooftop terrace at dusk extends the view outdoors; the Mandarin Grill, the only luxury hotel grill in Beijing with Forbidden City garden-terrace views, holds that same panorama over dinner.
Description ⚙: Mandarin Oriental Wangfujing, Beijing opened in March 2019 as the brand's Beijing debut, occupying the top two floors of WF Central on Wangfujing Street, Dongcheng. The 73 rooms and suites, designed by Hirsch Bedner Associates, are among the largest in the city, starting at 55 square metres. The hotel was built within the Forbidden City's protected zone under a 32-metre height restriction; the building steps down westward to respect the palace compound. Many rooms have direct Forbidden City views through floor-to-ceiling windows. The Premier Suite offers the most unobstructed sightline; the Oriental Suite adds a six-seat dining table and private terrace; the Garden Suite extends to a 300-square-metre private courtyard. MO Bar's outdoor terrace and the Mandarin Grill — the only luxury hotel grill in Beijing with Forbidden City garden-terrace views — both open onto the rooftop garden. Café Zi serves contemporary Cantonese cuisine. The hotel holds a Forbes Travel Guide 5-star rating.
Address: No. 269 Wangfujing Street, 东城区 China, 100006
Nearest Airports: PEK · Capital · 28 km; NAY · Nanyuan · 31 km; PKX · Daxing · 55 km

Hotel Tagline: A riverside terrace above the Liangma River, with the embassy district spreading beyond.
Summary: Worth a stay for the Bvlgari Bar terrace — a riverside perch above the Liangma River in the embassy district. The Premium Suites add floor-to-ceiling river views from the bedroom; Il Ristorante — Niko Romito, holding one Michelin star since 2020, occupies the park-facing room downstairs.
Description ⚙: Bvlgari Hotel Beijing is a 119-room five-star urban resort in the Genesis Beijing compound, Embassy District, Chaoyang, designed by ACPV ARCHITECTS Antonio Citterio Patricia Viel. The hotel received Two MICHELIN Keys in the 2025 Michelin Guide hotel selection and appears on the Condé Nast Traveler Gold List. Rooms average over 60 square metres; the Bvlgari Suite at 380 square metres offers views of the Liangma River, private gardens, and the embassy compound. The Serpenti Suite launched in January 2025. Il Ristorante — Niko Romito has held one Michelin star for six consecutive years since 2020 and overlooks the riverside park. The Bvlgari Bar's outdoor terrace faces the Liangma River. The Bvlgari Spa occupies two floors and 1,500 square metres, incorporating traditional Chinese medicine treatments. An indoor pool with golden tile finish is available to all guests.
Address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Chao Yang Qu, CN CN 北京市 朝阳区新源南路8号院2号楼 邮政编码: 100027
Nearest Airports: PEK · Capital · 21 km; NAY · Nanyuan · 32 km; PKX · Daxing · 66 km

Hotel Tagline: From Level 66's glass pyramid, the CCTV Headquarters and China World Trade Center fill a 360-degree panorama.
Summary: China Grill on Level 66 delivers 360-degree CBD views beneath a glass pyramid — the CCTV Headquarters and China World Trade Center both in range. The 59th-floor Sky Pool holds the same panorama during laps; the fitness center beside it faces the skyline at the same height.
Description ⚙: Park Hyatt Beijing occupies the upper sections of the central tower of Beijing Yintai Centre, a 250-metre complex on Chang'an Avenue designed by John Portman & Associates. Rooms and suites are located on floors 37 to 49 and 59 to 66, directly opposite the China World Trade Center and the CCTV Tower. All 237 rooms, including 22 suites, face large windows with city views; suites range from 45 to 240 square metres. China Grill on Level 66 is a 360-degree panoramic restaurant topped by a glass pyramid; China Bar on Level 65 serves cocktails with the same citywide outlook and live entertainment. The Dining Room on Level 63 offers Chinese cuisine and seasonal afternoon tea. The 59th-floor Sky Pool is a 25-metre heated indoor lap pool with CBD views; the fitness centre occupies the same floor. Tian Spa provides treatments, whirlpool, steam room, and sauna.
Address: 2 Jian Guo Men Wai Da Jie, Chao Yang Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100020
Nearest Airports: PEK · Capital · 25 km; NAY · Nanyuan · 27 km; PKX · Daxing · 60 km

Hotel Tagline: Floor-to-ceiling glass frames the Liangma River, China Zun, and the Beijing skyline beyond.
Summary: The Ambassador suites are the right category for Liangma River and China Zun views with space to match. The 26th-floor executive lounge serves all-day snacks next to city vistas; Cai Yi Xuan’s Michelin-starred Cantonese kitchen warrants a dinner reservation in its own right.
Description ⚙: Four Seasons Hotel Beijing is a five-star property on Liangmaqiao Road, Chaoyang, opened in 2012. Guest rooms and suites in the Ambassador category face the Liangma River, China Zun (CITIC Tower), and the Beijing skyline through floor-to-ceiling windows. The Terrace Suite extends the view to a private outdoor terrace. The hotel hosts two Michelin-recognised restaurants: Cai Yi Xuan, which has held one Michelin star for seven consecutive years, and Mio, recognised by the Michelin Guide Beijing. The 26th-floor executive lounge provides all-day snacks and refreshments alongside city vistas. The hotel offers an indoor pool, a round-the-clock fitness center, and spa facilities for both adults and children. Helicopter tours over the Great Wall can be arranged through the concierge.
Address: 48 Liang Ma Qiao Lu, Chao Yang Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100125
Nearest Airports: PEK · Capital · 21 km; NAY · Nanyuan · 32 km; PKX · Daxing · 65 km

Hotel Tagline: At dusk, the Forbidden City's gilded rooflines glow beneath Jingshan Park's pavilions from the rooftop garden.
Summary: Five terraces provide varied angles across the historic city, but the rooftop garden is worth timing at dusk — the Forbidden City’s gilded rooflines glow beneath Jingshan Park’s pavilions. On the ground floor, a quiet room at the far end of the lobby faces Coal Hill directly.
Description ⚙: The PuXuan Hotel and Spa is a five-star hotel on Wangfujing Street, Dongcheng, designed by Ole Scheeren for the Urban Resort Concepts group. The building faces the National Art Museum of China and shares its complex with the Guardian Art Center. Five terraces provide varied angles across the historic city; the rooftop garden looks directly onto Jingshan Park and the gilded rooftops of the Forbidden City. A quiet room at the ground-floor lobby end faces Coal Hill (Jingshan) with unobstructed park views. The UR Spa occupies three floors and offers treatments including anti-aging protocols and HuFu massages. The hotel is ranked consistently among the top properties in China for design and editorial distinction.
Address: No.1 WangFuJing Street, 东城区 China, 100006
Nearest Airports: NAY · Nanyuan · 22 km; PEK · Capital · 29 km; PKX · Daxing · 55 km

Description ⚙: Set at Paradise Bay (Cennet Koyu) on the northern tip of the Bodrum Peninsula in Göltürkbükü, with access to two private sandy beaches. Forbes Travel Guide Five-Star for three consecutive years (2023–2025); named among Condé Nast Traveler's 25 Best Resorts in Europe, 2024 Readers' Choice Awards. All 59 rooms and suites (from 72 sqm), 31 apartments, and villa accommodation face the open Aegean Sea. Sea-view suites on the upper floors capture the widest panorama. Dining includes Hakkasan (Chinese), Gigi Bodrum (Mediterranean), and the Pool Bar & Restaurant. SENSE spa with indoor and outdoor pools. Seasonal, typically open April to October.

Description ⚙: Adults-only design hotel on the hillside above Bodrum city center and marina, in the Yokuşbaşı district. The free-form outdoor pool delivers a 180° panorama covering Bodrum Bay, the Castle of St. Peter, the harbor, and Kos on the horizon — the only hotel in Bodrum city where all four appear simultaneously. Rooms facing south on the upper floors capture the castle and the bay in the same frame. The hilltop restaurant opens onto the sea view. Free daily beach shuttle to Flamm Beach in Türkbükü. Year-round operation.
Address: Yokuşbaşı, Sulu Hasan Cd. No:18, 48400 Bodrum/Muğla, Türkiye

Description ⚙: Marriott Luxury Collection in the Asarık area, Gümbet district, on the western coast of the Bodrum Peninsula overlooking Gümbet Bay and the open Aegean Sea. All sea-view rooms face the water from a terraced layout; the pool terrace and beach club share the same Aegean horizon, with a single island visible offshore. Three restaurants; Buddha Bar at the beach club. Seasonal outdoor pool, indoor pool, children's pool, full-service spa, and hammam.
Address: Asarlik Mevkii, Gümbet, Adnan Menderes Cd. No: 89 PK 225, 48400 Bodrum/Muğla, Türkiye
Nearest Airports: BJV · Milas-Bodrum · 42 km

Two restaurants, a hammam, and yoga on site. The room to ask for is on the upper terrace of the terraced stone building, facing the water.
Description ⚙: Opened in May 2026 as OKU's second property, following OKU Ibiza. Located on the north bay of Göltürkbükü, in the Küçükbük area — adults-only, beachfront, with direct sea access via private jetties. Two restaurants (Japanese and Mediterranean), hammam, spa, yoga classes, and fitness on site. Rooms have private balconies; upper floors capture the widest sightlines over Göltürkbükü Bay toward the open sea.

Hotel Tagline: Corner suites above the third floor hold both the Palace Pier and the West Pier ruins in a single frame.
Summary: The £17m renovation completed in 2025 by Garuda Design has reset every room across seven floors; the corner suites above the third floor deliver the widest channel sightline. The L.RAPHAEL spa and Cyan Brighton restaurant make this the most complete seafront address in Brighton.
Description ⚙: Brighton's original 'Palace by the Sea', opened in 1864, completed a £17 million renovation in 2025 designed by Garuda Design — all 205 rooms and suites across seven floors were reworked with sea- and sky-inspired palettes, marble bathrooms, and local artwork. Corner suites above the third floor deliver the widest English Channel sightline on the Kings Road strip, with the Palace Pier and the West Pier ruins both in frame. Cyan Brighton restaurant and the L.RAPHAEL spa, added in September 2025, complete a property that now matches the scale of its Victorian facade.
Address: 97-99 Kings Rd, Brighton and Hove, Brighton BN1 2FW, UK
Nearest Airports: LGW · Gatwick · 48 km

Summary: Upper-floor sea-facing doubles with a balcony frame Brighton Beach and the Palace Pier in full — and the guest scores are among the highest on the seafront. Boutique scale, Georgian façade, Kemptown quiet: the right base if the view is the point of the trip.
Description ⚙: A boutique hotel in a Georgian townhouse on Marine Parade in Kemptown, one minute from Brighton Beach. Upper-floor sea-facing doubles with bay windows and balconies frame the English Channel and Brighton Palace Pier. Among the highest guest scores on the Brighton seafront, the hotel offers a quieter base than the Kings Road strip while maintaining direct channel views.
Address: 41 Marine Parade, Kemptown, Brighton and Hove, Brighton BN2 1PE, UK
Nearest Airports: LGW · Gatwick · 48 km

Description ⚙: A 20-room boutique hotel in a pair of Regency townhouses on Marine Parade, part of A Curious Group of Hotels since 2023. The Marvelous Room category places a freestanding bathtub directly in front of floor-to-ceiling sash windows facing the English Channel. Attic rooms on the fourth floor add a wrought-iron balcony. The 24-hour lounge bar delivers the same channel views without a room commitment. Brighton Palace Pier is a five-minute walk.
Address: Marine Parade, Kemptown, Brighton and Hove, Brighton BN2 1PE, UK
Nearest Airports: LGW · Gatwick · 48 km

Description ⚙: A 210-room hotel directly opposite Brighton's beachfront promenade, with upper-floor sea-view rooms facing the channel and the Palace Pier. The Atrium Bar has a glass ceiling and a seafront position. The hotel's leisure club — indoor pool, sauna, and steam room — is included in the room rate, alongside a pamper studio. The Royal Pavilion is a five-minute walk from the Kings Road entrance.

Summary: Sea-view rooms with balcony frame the West Pier ruins and the open channel from above road level. The Salt Room restaurant occupies the building’s corner with a covered terrace and uninterrupted ocean views — one of the better seafront dining positions in Brighton, open to non-guests.
Description ⚙: A Victorian-era hotel on the Brighton seafront, designed by Alfred Waterhouse in 1890, with sea-view rooms and balconies facing the West Pier ruins and the open English Channel. The Salt Room, a standalone seafood restaurant occupying the building's corner, has a covered terrace with uninterrupted ocean views and is open to non-guests. The hotel also has an indoor pool, gym, sauna, and steam room.

Description ⚙: A 79-room hotel at the centre of the Kings Road seafront strip, with the majority of rooms facing the English Channel directly. Deluxe Sea View rooms and the Panoramic Suite include binoculars. HarBAR + Kitchen serves all day with floor-to-ceiling windows facing the promenade and the beach. The subterranean HarSPA includes a heated indoor pool, a hydrotherapy pool, two Scandinavian hot tubs, a steam room, and a sauna.

Summary: The De Mar suite has a superking bed, a balcony over the marina, and a double rainfall shower. Unusually for Brighton, the hotel sits inside the marina itself: the water view is enclosed and immediate, with no seafront road between you and it.

The De Mar suite has a superking bed, a balcony directly over the water, and a double rainfall shower. The hotel's brasserie and bar sit on the waterfront. Free parking is available, differentiating it from the central seafront properties.

Description ⚙: A four-star aparthotel on the former site of Brighton's ice rink, five minutes on foot from the beach. The top-floor suite has a panoramic terrace framing St Paul's Parish Church tower and the English Channel on the horizon. Spread across four floors, the property offers serviced apartments with full kitchen facilities and gym access — a practical option for longer stays or for those who prefer a city-centre base over a direct seafront position.
