Hotel Tagline: From the 21st floor, the Brisbane River stretches beneath South Bank Parklands and the city beyond.
Summary: The 21st-floor rooftop Terrace frames South Bank Parklands and the Brisbane River from above. The infinity pool shares the same panorama. We'd request a river-facing suite with balcony — the view extends across the water to the city, and the first five-star to open in South Bank still sets the pace.
Description ⚙: The chic Emporium Hotel South Bank made its grand entrance into Brisbane's hotel scene in 2018, swiftly establishing itself as a pinnacle in Queensland's bustling capital. The establishment, the first five-star hotel in Brisbane's South Bank, was awarded "Australia's Leading Boutique Hotel" the following year. The hotel's 21st-floor rooftop Terrace is its crown jewel, unveiling jaw-dropping South Bank Parklands and Brisbane River views, while the rooftop infinity pool offers a panoramic view of Brisbane's skyline. River-facing suites with balconies are the preferred accommodation for guests seeking unobstructed river and city vistas.
Address: 267 Grey St, South Brisbane QLD 4101, Australia
Nearest Airports: BNE · Brisbane · 19 km

Hotel Tagline: Adjacent to the Story Bridge, with the Brisbane River visible from the balcony.
Summary: Adjacent to the Story Bridge, the river and the bridge are visible from the room rather than through it. The River Master Balcony category puts you closest to the water. The Fiume rooftop pool and bar deliver the same sightline for non-room hours — Story Bridge in one direction, the CBD in the other.
Description ⚙: One of Brisbane's top five-star hotels, Crystalbrook Vincent is uniquely positioned adjacent to the iconic Story Bridge at Howard Smith Wharves. Guests in the River and River Master Balcony rooms are treated to awe-inspiring panoramas of the bridge and the Brisbane River's serene waters, with Brisbane's magnificent skyline extending to the right. The Fiume rooftop pool and bar deliver the same sightline to non-room guests — Story Bridge in one direction, the CBD in the other. The hotel integrates Vincent Fantauzzo's artwork throughout its 103 rooms.
Address: 5 Boundary St, Brisbane City QLD 4000, Australia
Nearest Airports: BNE · Brisbane · 20 km

Hotel Tagline: From the rooftop pool to the King Suite, the Brisbane River runs through every sightline.
Summary: The WET Deck rooftop pool runs 24 hours, which means the Brisbane River at night is an option, not a compromise. Most of the 312 rooms reach the river and Story Bridge, with views extending to Mt. Coot-tha. The room to ask for is the King Suite with River View — river on one side, city on the other.
Description ⚙: The five-star W Brisbane is known for its remarkable Brisbane River views from the CBD. Most of its 312 contemporary accommodations offer complete or partial Brisbane River vistas, including the Story Bridge and views extending to Mt. Coot-tha. The King Suite with River View — Fantastic Suite is the standout category. Guests can enjoy their favorite cocktails at the WET Deck rooftop pool, which operates 24 hours with the Brisbane River as its scenic backdrop.
Address: 81 N Quay, Brisbane City QLD 4000, Australia

Hotel Tagline: From the 65th floor, Brisbane's CBD, river, South Bank, and Scenic Rim mountains fill the frame together.
Summary: Inside the 73-floor Infinity Tower, the Altitude suites begin at the 65th floor — from there, Brisbane's CBD, the river, South Bank, and the Scenic Rim mountains are all in frame at once. Anything above the 11th floor already clears the skyline. We'd book into the Altitude range.
Description ⚙: Meriton Suites Herschel Street, Brisbane is proudly installed within the towering 73-floor Infinity Tower. Accommodations above the 11th floor offer breathtaking vistas of Brisbane. For sky-high panoramas, the Altitude suites from the 65th floor upwards showcase Brisbane's CBD, the meandering Brisbane River, the lively South Bank, and the enchanting Scenic Rim mountains.
Address: 43 Herschel St, Brisbane City QLD 4000, Australia
Nearest Airports: BNE · Brisbane · 18 km

Hotel Tagline: Story Bridge and the city skyline stretch across the view from rooftop to suite.
Summary: Iris, the rooftop bar and restaurant atop Hotel X, won best hotel bar in Australia at the 2022 AHA National Awards — Story Bridge and the city skyline are part of that case. The One Bedroom Junior Suite frames the same panorama from inside. The rooftop pool adds a third angle on the view.
Description ⚙: Sitting in Fortitude Valley, the five-star Hotel X Brisbane Fortitude Vly stands as a true gem within the Vignette Collection under the IHG umbrella. Atop the boutique hotel, Iris, the Spanish Rooftop Bar & Restaurant, boasts the prestigious title of best hotel bar in Australia for 2022, awarded by the Australian Hotels Association National Awards for Excellence.

Hotel Tagline: From the rooftop, the Story Bridge and city skyline hold the horizon together.
Summary: A low-rise in New Farm with a rooftop bar that frames the Story Bridge and the city skyline — the view earns its place alongside the five-star properties closer to the CBD. Worth staying for the 17 art-deco suites and the quiet of New Farm as much as for the panorama above it.
Description ⚙: Located in lively New Farm, only moments from Brunswick Street and Fortitude Valley, Spicers Balfour Brisbane is one of Brisbane's best-kept secrets. The hotel's rooftop bar delivers one of the finest Brisbane panoramas, featuring direct views of the heritage-listed steel cantilever Story Bridge and Brisbane's impressive skyline. Its 17 distinct suites blend 1940s art-deco décor with exquisite antique furnishings.
Address: 37 Balfour St, New Farm QLD 4005, Australia

Hotel Tagline: Story Bridge, the Kangaroo Point Cliffs, and the Brisbane River stretch across the windows at the Botanic Gardens' edge.
Summary: Positioned at the edge of the City Botanic Gardens, the views here cover Story Bridge, the Kangaroo Point Cliffs, and the Brisbane River. The Stamford Suite is the address — baby grand piano, generous square footage, and panoramic windows. The 3rd-floor pool shares the same sightline.
Description ⚙: Conveniently situated in Brisbane's city center, just moments from the lush Brisbane City Botanic Gardens, the Stamford Plaza Brisbane offers one of the finest river view hotels in Queensland's capital. Its 252 lavishly furnished rooms and suites all offer awe-inspiring vistas, including the scenic Kangaroo Point Cliffs Park and the iconic Story Bridge.

Hotel Tagline: From the 74th floor, the Story Bridge, river, CBD, and Mount Coot-tha share the same window.
Summary: Suites reach the 74th floor, high enough that the Story Bridge, the CBD, the river, and Mount Coot-tha are all visible from the same window. The 3-bedroom altitude suites on the upper floors offer the broadest read of the city — practical for longer stays, exceptional for the view.
Description ⚙: Meriton Suites Adelaide Street stands among Brisbane's skyscraping marvels, with suites reaching up to the 74th floor for a rarefied urban living experience. Unparalleled views command the senses — the Brisbane skyline, the meandering river, Story Bridge, and Mount Coot-tha adding a touch of grandeur.

Hotel Tagline: The Wheel of Brisbane sits in the window, with South Bank stretching across the upper floors.
Summary: A heritage-listed building on George Street, where studios to three-bedroom apartments reach the Brisbane River and South Bank. The panoramic balcony apartments are the ones to ask for — The Wheel of Brisbane sits in the window, and the larger upper floors add South Bank to the same frame.
Description ⚙: Centrally located in a heritage-listed building on George Street, the riverfront Adina Apartment Hotel Brisbane offers an unbeatable combination of location, modern facilities, spacious accommodations, and unobstructed views. Accommodations include spacious studios and one, two, and three-bedroom apartments, most with views of the Brisbane River or city skyscrapers — with South Bank area and The Wheel of Brisbane visible from many windows.

Hotel Tagline: From the upper floors of Brisbane's tallest skyscraper, the Brisbane River bends further into frame.
Summary: Brisbane's tallest skyscraper, with Sky High apartments from levels 42 to 81. The panoramic indoor pool at level 66 is one way to take in the Brisbane River and South Bank together; the upper-floor apartments do it from the living room. The higher the floor, the further the river bends into frame.
Description ⚙: Perched at the apex of luxury and sophistication, CLLIX Brisbane Skytower Apartments stands as Brisbane's tallest skyscraper. The Sky High apartments span from levels 42 to 81, affording guests unobstructed panoramas of the Brisbane River and cityscape. A panoramic indoor pool at level 66 offers a serene retreat allowing guests to unwind while surrounded by the city's vistas.

Nearest Airports: BNE · Brisbane · 20 km

Hotel Tagline: Story Bridge fills the view from riverside rooms and Motion Dining's tables, lit by LED displays until late.
Summary: Connected to Howard Smith Wharves via the City Reach Boardwalk, Story Bridge is the constant view from the rooms and from Motion Dining's river-side tables. After a $20 million renovation completed in 2019, the bridge-facing rooms are the ones to request — the LED lighting display runs until late.
Description ⚙: The five-star Brisbane Marriott Hotel is situated between the ever-busy CBD and the more laid-back Fortitude Valley, directly connected to the popular Howard Smith Wharves eateries through the City Reach Boardwalk. After a $20 million renovation completed in 2019, the hotel re-emerged as one of the best in the city, with the signature restaurant Motion Dining pairing contemporary cuisine with romantic Brisbane River views.
Address: 515 Queen St, Brisbane City QLD 4000, Australia
Nearest Airports: BNE · Brisbane · 16 km

Hotel Tagline: From the rooftop pool, Brisbane's tallest skyscrapers fill the skyline ahead.
Summary: The rooftop pool faces the tallest Brisbane skyscrapers — the Valley Suite captures the same CBD panorama from inside. The hotel's art-forward identity, with 103 rooms, bespoke wallpaper, and commissioned works throughout, makes Fortitude Valley feel like a destination rather than a base.
Description ⚙: The multi-award-winning Ovolo Brisbane, Fortitude Valley is a boutique hotel at the vibrant heart of Fortitude Valley. Its rooftop pool faces the tallest Brisbane skyscrapers, and the Valley Suite captures the same CBD panorama from inside. The hotel's art-forward identity features custom wallpaper, eccentric furniture, and commissioned artworks throughout its 103 rooms.

Summary: In Hamilton, the Brisbane River bends close enough that the River Vista corner suites achieve a 180-degree panorama — river on the curve, the city skyline beyond. We'd request one of those corner units and use the river-facing restaurant for the evenings.
Description ⚙: View Brisbane is a four-star Hamilton hotel planted by the Brisbane River, just across the manicured Newstead Park and a pleasant 20-minute stroll to the ever-buzzing Fortitude Valley. The property's river and skyline views are nothing short of spectacular, especially from the River Vista corner suites that dazzle guests with a 180-degree panoramic view.

Nearest Airports: BNE · Brisbane · 12 km

Summary: Thirty-plus levels in quieter Milton, ten minutes from Suncorp Stadium on foot. The executive and river view categories deliver Brisbane River sightlines from the upper floors; the outdoor pool looks directly down onto the stadium. A calmer base than the CBD, with views that keep up.
Description ⚙: Situated on the fringes of Brisbane's bustling city center, The Milton Brisbane is set in a serene locality adorned with elegant cafes, restaurants, and tranquil parklands. A standout feature is its close proximity to Suncorp Stadium, a mere 10-minute stroll away. The executive and river view categories offer Brisbane River sightlines from the upper floors.

Hotel Tagline: From floors 17 to 26, the Brisbane River and city skyline sit in full frame.
Summary: Floors 17 to 26 put the Brisbane River and the city skyline in full frame — river on one side, Queen Street Mall on the other. On level 4, the Hibiscus Bar & Terrace keeps both landmarks in view beside the 20-metre heated infinity pool. We'd book a high floor and work down from there.
Description ⚙: Positioned right on Queen Street Mall, the luxurious Hyatt Regency Brisbane stands as a top-tier haven in the heart of Brisbane's Central Business District. Boasting 292 guestrooms and suites, this five-star hotel offers phenomenal views of the city skyline and the meandering Brisbane River from floors 17 to 26.

Nearest Airports: BNE · Brisbane · 19 km

Hotel Tagline: Three infinity pools at 37 metres, the Sky Deck at 100, the Brisbane River below both.
Summary: Opened in August 2024 at Queen's Wharf, The Star Grand Brisbane delivers river views from three directions: from River King rooms facing South Bank, from the Leisure Deck's infinity pools 37 metres up, and from the Sky Deck at 100 metres above the river.
Description ⚙: Opened in August 2024 at the Queen's Wharf precinct, The Star Grand Brisbane is the city's newest five-star hotel, with 340 rooms and sweeping views of the Brisbane River and South Bank. River King rooms and River Suites face the water directly; the Skyline Penthouse Suite, on levels 16 and 17, adds a panoramic city arc. The Leisure Deck on level 7, set 37 metres above street level, holds three infinity-style pools with unobstructed river views. One hundred metres higher, the Sky Deck rooftop runway of restaurants and bars delivers a 250-metre stretch of city and river panorama.
Address: 33 William St, Brisbane City QLD 4000, Australia
Nearest Airports: BNE · Brisbane · 19 km

Hotel Tagline: Twenty-seven rooms face the Hungarian Parliament Building across the Danube, every one of them.
Summary: Floor-to-ceiling panoramic windows facing Parliament across the river — twenty-seven rooms, all with the same view, none of them incidental. A sunrise here over the Danube is the kind of thing that recalibrates a trip. The complimentary afternoon tea at Jenő Hubay Music Hall is a quiet touch.
Address: Budapest, Bem rkp. 11, 1011 Hungary
Nearest Airports: BUD · Ferenc Liszt · 22 km

Hotel Tagline: From one balcony, the Budapest Eye and St. Stephen's Basilica share the frame.
Summary: The Contemporary Suites with balcony face a partial view of the Budapest Eye and St. Stephen’s Basilica — two of the city’s defining landmarks from a single terrace. The BiBo rooftop bar adds the wider panorama. Piero Lissoni’s design across three historic buildings gives the interior its own reason to look.

Hotel Tagline: From French balconies, the Recoleta parks stretch toward the distant Río de la Plata.
Summary: Established in 1932, this Recoleta icon holds the Río de la Plata in view from its “Le Mirador” suites — charming vistas over the cemetery’s marble canopy from a French balcony. The 11th-floor Alvear Roof Bar extends the panorama for anyone not in a view room.
Description ⚙: Established in 1932, the Alvear Palace Hotel occupies a landmark Belle Époque building on Avenida Alvear in Recoleta. The hotel's 'Le Mirador' suites face the Recoleta parks and the distant Río de la Plata from French balconies, while the 11th-floor Alvear Roof Bar extends the panorama to the full Buenos Aires skyline. The property combines gilded Louis XV interiors with a private spa, an indoor pool, and the Alain Ducasse-trained culinary program that has defined the address for nearly a century.
Address: Av. Alvear 1891, C1129AAA Cdad. Autónoma de Buenos Aires, Argentina
Nearest Airports: AEP · Aeroparque Jorge Newbery · 6 km; EZE · Ezeiza · 39 km

Hotel Tagline: From the 14th floor, Plaza Rodríguez Peña and the Sarmiento Palace compose themselves below.
Summary: Floor-to-ceiling windows are the standard throughout this Recoleta boutique, but the 14th-floor executive suites add something the lower rooms lack: height enough to reduce Plaza Rodríguez Peña and the Sarmiento Palace to a composition below. Book one of those.
Description ⚙: The Palladio Hotel Buenos Aires - MGallery occupies a Recoleta building on Avenida Callao, steps from Plaza Rodríguez Peña and the Sarmiento Palace. Floor-to-ceiling windows are the standard throughout, but the 14th-floor executive suites add a height that places the city below rather than beside — Plaza Rodríguez Peña and the Sarmiento Palace reduced to composition. The Negresco Bistró and the proximity of El Ateneo Grand Splendid, one of the world's great bookshops, make the location as considered as the design.
Address: Av. Callao 924, C1023AAP Cdad. Autónoma de Buenos Aires, Argentina
Nearest Airports: AEP · Aeroparque Jorge Newbery · 7 km; EZE · Ezeiza · 39 km

Alvear Icon Hotel, Buenos Aires: Modern spa with indoor pool, floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking the city skyline at sunset

Alvear Icon Hotel, Buenos Aires: Panoramic window view of the city skyline, Río de la Plata, and iconic architecture from a high-rise room

Hotel Tagline: Thirty-two floors above Puerto Madero, with the Río de la Plata on one side and the Ecological Reserve on the other.
Summary: Thirty-two floors over Puerto Madero, with the Río de la Plata on one side and the Ecological Reserve on the other. Junior Grand View suites between the 15th and 17th floors have the best balance of height and price; the 32nd-floor Crystal Bar extends the view into the evening.
Description ⚙: The Alvear Icon Hotel rises 32 floors above Puerto Madero, with the Río de la Plata on one side and the Costanera Sur Ecological Reserve on the other. Junior Grand View suites between the 15th and 17th floors have the best balance of height and price among the room categories. At the top, Kayla Restaurant on the 31st floor and the Crystal Bar on the 32nd extend the view from a meal into the evening. The hotel operates both an outdoor pool facing the docks and an indoor heated pool on the 19th and 20th floors.
Address: Aimé Painé 1130, C1107 Cdad. Autónoma de Buenos Aires, Argentina
Nearest Airports: AEP · Aeroparque Jorge Newbery · 9 km; EZE · Ezeiza · 39 km

Faena Hotel Buenos Aires rooftop pool with turquoise water, red loungers, umbrellas, and brick buildings in Buenos Aires

Hotel Tagline: River and city share the same frame, with the Ecological Reserve completing the view below.
Summary: Among the theatrical rooms and suites here, “Skyline View Room” is the category that actually delivers on the address — river and city in the same frame, with the Ecological Reserve completing the panorama. El Cabaret downstairs runs tango nightly for those who want the full Buenos Aires arc.
Description ⚙: The Faena Hotel Buenos Aires occupies the converted El Porteño building in Puerto Madero, designed by Baz Luhrmann and Phillipe Starck. Among the theatrical rooms and suites, the 'Skyline View Room' category delivers river and city in the same frame, with the Ecological Reserve completing the panorama below. El Cabaret runs tango nightly and The Library Lounge provides a quieter register. The hotel is positioned along the waterfront between the Ecological Reserve and the historic dock buildings.
Address: Martha Salotti 445, C1107CMB Cdad. Autónoma de Buenos Aires, Argentina
Nearest Airports: AEP · Aeroparque Jorge Newbery · 9 km; EZE · Ezeiza · 39 km

Hotel: Casa Lucia Member of Meliá Collection
Alt Text: Casa Lucia Buenos Aires hotel room with balcony view, wooden dresser, wall-mounted TV, and modern furnishings

Images.alt: Casa Lucia Buenos Aires hotel room with balcony view, wooden dresser, wall-mounted TV, and modern furnishings

Hotel Tagline: From the Tower Suites, the Río de la Plata fills the same horizon the original lighthouse was built to watch.
Summary: The 1929 tower was built to watch ships cross the Río de la Plata — the Tower Suites at the top still hold that view, with the Buenos Aires skyline completing the frame. Opened in January 2024 and named Best Hotel in Argentina 2025, it is currently one of the best cases for Recoleta.
Description ⚙: The 1929 Edificio Mihanovich was designed as a lighthouse — a 20-story tower on Calle Arroyo built so its commissioner could watch his ships depart across the Río de la Plata. Nearly a century later, the building reopened in January 2024 as Casa Lucia, member of the Meliá Collection, and was named Best Hotel in Argentina 2025. Set in the heart of Recoleta, it pairs an art deco silhouette with interiors inspired by Argentina's golden age of 1880 to 1940: warm timber paneling, marble bathrooms, and black-and-white photographs of Buenos Aires by Argentine photographer Ricardo Piñeiro. Of the 142 rooms and suites, the Tower Suites at the top frame panoramic views of the Buenos Aires skyline and the Río de la Plata — the same river the original lighthouse was built to overlook. Cantina restaurant is led by chef Julián del Pino; Le Club Bacan is the ground-floor cocktail bar with a Buenos Aires skyline mural painted by local artist Alejandra Winkhaus.
Address: Arroyo 841, C1007AAB Cdad. Autónoma de Buenos Aires, Argentina
Nearest Airports: AEP · Aeroparque Jorge Newbery · 6 km; EZE · Ezeiza · 41 km

Two guests enjoy panoramic views of Buenos Aires' Puerto Madero district from a high-rise balcony at SLS Buenos Aires, overlooking the waterfront, iconic yellow cranes, and cityscape

Hotel Tagline: Fifty-eight balconies look out over the docks and the Buenos Aires skyline.
Summary: Piero Lissoni designed the 58 rooms here, each with a private furnished balcony facing the docks and the Buenos Aires skyline. Small by Puerto Madero standards — a deliberate choice that keeps service tight. The rooftop Ciel Spa adds an outdoor pool to the equation.
Description ⚙: The SLS Buenos Aires Puerto Madero opened in May 2022, designed by Piero Lissoni across 58 rooms — each with a private furnished balcony facing the docks and the Buenos Aires skyline. The deliberate restraint of scale keeps service concentrated in a building where the Ciel Spa occupies the rooftop with an outdoor pool. The Leynia restaurant, run by chef José Ignacio, delivers wood-fired cooking in a setting that makes the waterfront address its backdrop.
Address: Juana Manso 1725, C1107CHI Cdad. Autónoma de Buenos Aires, Argentina
Nearest Airports: AEP · Aeroparque Jorge Newbery · 10 km; EZE · Ezeiza · 38 km

Hotel Tagline: From the 15th floor, Buenos Aires resolves into a skyline that reaches the Río de la Plata.
Summary: Aim for the 15th-floor Art Executive Suite — from there, the city panorama extends all the way to the River Plate, well beyond what the standard rooms deliver. The rooftop heated pool has a retractable roof, which means the skyline view survives whatever the weather is doing.
Description ⚙: The Alvear Art Hotel occupies a Retiro address on Suipacha, steps from the gallery corridor of Calle Arroyo. The 15th-floor Art Executive Suite delivers a city panorama that extends toward the Río de la Plata — the level from which Buenos Aires begins to resolve into a legible skyline rather than a streetscape. The Contraluz Restaurant and Artesano Bar operate on the ground floor; the rooftop heated pool has a retractable glass roof that keeps the city view available regardless of weather.
Address: Suipacha 1036, C1008 Cdad. Autónoma de Buenos Aires, Argentina
Nearest Airports: AEP · Aeroparque Jorge Newbery · 6 km; EZE · Ezeiza · 38 km

Hotel Tagline: Garden views face the French Embassy facade, while Tower rooms look out over the city.
Summary: Two buildings, two registers: the Tower’s modern rooms look out over the city, while La Mansión’s suites face the garden and the French Embassy facade next door. The outdoor heated pool occupies a garden that belongs to one of Retiro’s most composed addresses.
Description ⚙: The Four Seasons Hotel Buenos Aires occupies two buildings in Retiro: the Tower with modern, airy rooms facing the city, and La Mansión with suite-level rooms facing the garden and the facade of the French Embassy next door. The hotel's equestrian heritage is expressed in the decor throughout. Elena, a long-standing presence on the Latin America's 50 Best Restaurants list and featured in the Michelin Guide Argentina 2025, tantalizes the palate with exquisite steaks and an extensive breakfast buffet. An outdoor heated pool occupies the garden between the two buildings.
Address: Posadas 1086 88, C1011ABB Cdad. Autónoma de Buenos Aires, Argentina
Nearest Airports: AEP · Aeroparque Jorge Newbery · 7 km; EZE · Ezeiza · 39 km

Hotel Tagline: Glass walls face the Ecological Reserve and the Río de la Plata simultaneously from the upper floors.
Summary: The glass walls are structural logic here: almost every room faces the Ecological Reserve or the Río de la Plata directly. High-floor rooms on the river side catch both in the same window. The rooftop outdoor pool doubles as a sundeck above the waterfront.
Description ⚙: The Hilton Buenos Aires sits on the Puerto Madero waterfront on Macacha Güemes, its glass curtain walls designed to face the Costanera Sur Ecological Reserve and the Río de la Plata. Almost every room faces the reserve or the river directly, and high-floor rooms on the river side catch both in the same window. The outdoor rooftop pool doubles as a sundeck above the waterfront. The hotel operates three dining outlets — El Faro, Alberto's, and Croutons — and is connected to the Puerto Madero boardwalk.
Address: Macacha Güemes 351, C1106BKG Cdad. Autónoma de Buenos Aires, Argentina
Nearest Airports: AEP · Aeroparque Jorge Newbery · 8 km; EZE · Ezeiza · 39 km

Alt Text: Hotel Madero Buenos Aires offers elegant rooftop dining with panoramic views of the Río de la Plata and historic port buildings

Images.alt: Hotel Madero Buenos Aires offers elegant rooftop dining with panoramic views of the Río de la Plata and historic port buildings

Hotel Tagline: Balconies face either the Río de la Plata and Ecological Reserve, or the historic port cranes and Buenos Aires skyline.
Summary: Nearly 200 rooms, all with private balconies — the pick depends on what you’re facing. River-side rooms look toward the Ecological Reserve and Río de la Plata; the rooftop spa jacuzzi, open to the sky above Puerto Madero, is available regardless of which floor you’re on.
Description ⚙: Hotel Madero Buenos Aires, WorldHotels Elite, occupies Dique 2 in Puerto Madero. Nearly 200 rooms, all with private balconies — river-side rooms look toward the Ecological Reserve and the Río de la Plata, while dock-side rooms face the historic port cranes and the Buenos Aires skyline beyond. The rooftop spa includes a jacuzzi open to the sky above Puerto Madero, available regardless of room assignment. The Rëd Resto & Lounge operates on the ground floor along the dock.
Address: Dique 2, Rosario Vera Peñaloza 360, C1107CLA Cdad. Autónoma de Buenos Aires, Argentina
Nearest Airports: AEP · Aeroparque Jorge Newbery · 9 km; EZE · Ezeiza · 38 km

Hotel: Park Tower, A Luxury Collection Hotel, Buenos Aires

Hotel Tagline: Terraces on the upper floors look out over the Río de la Plata from the edge of Retiro.
Summary: Senator and Governor Suites have the terraces — walk-in closets, River Plate views, and enough space to make the suites feel like a separate category from the standard rooms. Adjacent access to the Sheraton’s outdoor pool and tennis courts adds range to the stay.
Description ⚙: The Park Tower, A Luxury Collection Hotel, Buenos Aires stands on Avenida Leandro N. Alem at the edge of Retiro, adjacent to Plaza San Martín. The Senator and Governor Suites add walk-out terraces to the River Plate views available at altitude — a category above the standard rooms in both space and position. Guests have adjacent access to the Sheraton's outdoor pool and tennis courts. The location places the hotel between the Microcentro, Puerto Madero, and Recoleta within comfortable walking range.
Address: Av. Leandro N. Alem 1193, C1001 Cdad. Autónoma de Buenos Aires, Argentina
Nearest Airports: AEP · Aeroparque Jorge Newbery · 6 km; EZE · Ezeiza · 40 km

View from InterContinental Buenos Aires window showing historic church tower with golden dome amid modern skyscrapers in Buenos Aires

Hotel Tagline: From high floors, the San Juan Bautista roofline gives way to the financial district beyond.
Summary: A 19-story tower in Montserrat where the view is a function of floor. Premium high-floor rooms are the move — city panorama and the San Juan Bautista Church roofline below, with Palacio Barolo and the Galería Güemes on the approach. Well-positioned for Plaza de Mayo and the historic centre.
Description ⚙: The InterContinental Buenos Aires by IHG is a 19-story tower in Montserrat on Moreno Street. Premium high-floor rooms frame a city panorama that includes the San Juan Bautista Church roofline below and the financial district towers beyond, with Palacio Barolo and the Galería Güemes visible on the approach. The hotel's position makes Plaza de Mayo, the Casa Rosada, and the historic centre of Buenos Aires accessible on foot — an operational advantage that the Puerto Madero and Recoleta properties cannot match.
Address: 809 Moreno St, C1091AAQ Cdad. Autónoma de Buenos Aires, Argentina
Nearest Airports: AEP · Aeroparque Jorge Newbery · 8 km; EZE · Ezeiza · 37 km

Alt Text: Marriott Mena House Cairo breakfast balcony with Great Pyramid of Khufu and Pyramid of Khafre framed through Moorish arch railing

Images.alt: Marriott Mena House Cairo breakfast balcony with Great Pyramid of Khufu and Pyramid of Khafre framed through Moorish arch railing

Hotel Tagline: At first light, the Great Pyramid fills the window from corner to corner.
Summary: At first light, the Great Pyramid fills the window from corner to corner — not a postcard, but the actual stones, the actual shadow still crossing the plateau below. This is as close as a hotel room gets to Khufu. The 139 Pavilion bar holds the same view at dusk, both pyramids in frame.
Description ⚙: Marriott Mena House, Cairo is a 331-room, 5-star resort set within 40 acres of garden at the foot of the Giza Pyramid Complex, less than 1 km from the Great Pyramid of Khufu. Premium Pyramid View rooms and suites face the plateau directly; the 139 Pavilion rooftop bar and the heated outdoor pool hold the same orientation. The hotel includes three restaurants, a heated outdoor pool, and a full-service spa.
Address: 6 Pyramids Road، Kafr Nassar, Al Haram, Giza Governorate 12556, Egypt
Nearest Airports: SPX · Sphinx International · 35 km; CAI · Cairo International · 38 km

Images.alt: The St. Regis Cairo luxury suite bathroom with gold fixtures and panoramic view of the Nile River, Qasr El Nil Bridge and Cairo Tower

Hotel Tagline: Feluccas trail white wakes across the Nile below, Gezira Island's canopy filling the middle distance.
Summary: High enough that the Nile reads as a moving surface — feluccas trailing white wakes, Gezira Island's canopy filling the middle distance, the city banking away on both sides. At night the water catches the lights differently on each bank. The rooftop suite adds a private plunge pool above all of it.
Description ⚙: The St. Regis Cairo comprises 232 guest rooms, 50 suites, and 78 serviced apartments in a 39-story tower on Corniche El Nil, directly facing Gezira Island. Nile-view rooms and suites span the tower across all floors; the indoor and outdoor pools, the Water Garden terrace, and the rooftop suite with private plunge pool share the river-facing aspect. The hotel includes five restaurants, a full-service spa, and butler service for all suites.
Address: 1189 Nile Corniche, Boulaq Num.5, Bulaq, Cairo Governorate 11221, Egypt
Nearest Airports: CAI · Cairo International · 23 km; SPX · Sphinx International · 42 km

Hotel Tagline: From a teak terrace on the Corniche, the Nile runs directly below.
Summary: Step onto the teak terrace of a Corniche Suite and the river is immediately below — not framed by glass, not across a road, but present. On clear afternoons from the Presidential Suites on the upper floors, the Giza silhouette appears on the western horizon as the light drops.
Description ⚙: Four Seasons Hotel Cairo at Nile Plaza occupies floors 6 to 30 of the Nile Plaza complex on Corniche El Nil in Garden City, with 365 rooms and suites offering panoramic views of the Nile River, Gezira Island, and the Cairo skyline. Premier Nile View rooms and Corniche Suites have private teak-furnished terraces; the Presidential Suites on floors 25 to 28 include a Pyramid sightline on clear days. The hotel features five restaurants, two outdoor pools, an indoor pool, and a spa.
Address: 1089 CORNICHE EL NIL, Qasr El Nil, Cairo Governorate 11519, Egypt
Nearest Airports: CAI · Cairo International · 22 km; SPX · Sphinx International · 44 km

Hotel Tagline: Three sides of Nile, with sunrise and sunset visible from the same balcony.
Summary: The hotel is in the river, not beside it. From almost every room, water on at least one side — the island's position making sunrise and sunset two different shows from the same balcony. The infinity pool edge meets the Nile at the same level, no gap between them.
Description ⚙: Sofitel Cairo Nile El Gezirah is a 433-room, 5-star tower situated on the southern tip of Gezira Island (Zamalek) in central Cairo, surrounded by the Nile on three sides. Almost every room in the cylindrical tower faces the river directly, and the hotel's infinity pool sits at the water's edge with views of both banks. Facilities include four restaurants, a casino, a spa with hammam, and a private Nile promenade.
Address: 3 El Thawra Council St Zamalek, El Orman, الزمالك، محافظة القاهرة‬ 11518, Egypt
Nearest Airports: CAI · Cairo International · 22 km; SPX · Sphinx International · 42 km

Hotel Tagline: From the rooftop, the Nile, the Egyptian Museum, and the Pyramids align in a single sightline.
Summary: Three things in the same sightline: the river below, the Egyptian Museum a short walk to the right, the Pyramids on the western horizon at the Nox rooftop bar. It's an orientation that takes a moment to process when you arrive and doesn't stop being striking.
Description ⚙: The Nile Ritz-Carlton, Cairo is a 329-room luxury hotel positioned between the Nile River and Tahrir Square in downtown Cairo, adjacent to the Egyptian Museum. Rooms and suites overlook either the Nile or the Egyptian Museum; select suites have private balconies, and the rooftop Nox bar extends views to the Giza Pyramids on the western horizon. The hotel features more than nine restaurants and bars, an outdoor pool, and a full-service spa.
Address: 1113 Nile Corniche, Ismailia, El Nil, Cairo Governorate 11221, Egypt
Nearest Airports: CAI · Cairo International · 21 km; SPX · Sphinx International · 42 km

Hotel Tagline: From the 10th floor, the Nile opens north toward Cairo Tower and curves below through Garden City.
Summary: The Jazz Bar on the 10th floor opens onto the Nile in both directions — Cairo Tower to the north, the Garden City embankment curving below. At the rooftop pool, the river is at eye level and the city sounds rise and fall beneath you as the light shifts.
Description ⚙: Kempinski Nile Hotel Cairo is a 191-room, 5-star property in Garden City, directly on Corniche El Nil above the Nile. River-facing suites include kitchenettes and panoramic river windows; the Jazz Bar on the 10th floor and the rooftop pool share the same Nile orientation. The hotel offers two restaurants, butler service, heated floors, and a full-service spa.
Address: 12 Ahmed Ragheb, Qasr El Nil, Cairo Governorate 11519, Egypt
Nearest Airports: CAI · Cairo International · 21 km; SPX · Sphinx International · 43 km

Hotel Tagline: Thirty-two floors up, the Nile lies directly below and the Pyramids hold the western skyline.
Summary: From the rooftop pool deck the geometry becomes clear: the Nile directly below, the Pyramids on the western skyline thirty-two floors up. There are few points in Cairo where these two landmarks align in the same field of vision. This is one of them.
Description ⚙: Fairmont Nile City is a 562-room, 5-star hotel within the Nile City Towers complex on Corniche El Nil in Bulaq, Cairo. The rooftop pool deck offers views across the Nile River and toward the Giza Pyramid Complex on the western horizon; Fairmont Gold rooms include 24-hour butler service and access to the Gold Lounge. The hotel operates six restaurants, a full-service spa, a casino, and a nightclub.
Address: Nile City Towers 2005 B، Nile Corniche, Boulaq Num.1, Bulaq, Cairo Governorate 11311, Egypt
Nearest Airports: CAI · Cairo International · 21 km; SPX · Sphinx International · 46 km

Hotel Tagline: From a circular arched window in the spa, the Nile fills the frame on the Giza bank.
Summary: The spa's treatment room has a circular arched window — the kind of detail you don't expect to frame a river. The Nile fills it from the Giza bank while the table stays still below. Select suites hold the same aspect; so does the pool terrace, four floors above the gardens.
Description ⚙: Four Seasons Hotel Cairo at The First Residence is a 262-room, 5-star property in Giza, 100 meters from the Nile River. Select suites and the spa's upper-floor treatment rooms have Nile views; the outdoor pool on the fourth floor includes complimentary cabanas and a Nile aspect. The hotel offers six restaurants, a full-service spa, a casino, and direct access to the First Mall shopping complex.

Hotel Tagline: Directly opposite the Grand Egyptian Museum, Khufu and Khafre meet the sky 2.3 kilometers away.
Summary: Directly opposite the Grand Egyptian Museum, the Pyramid View rooms look out over 2.3 kilometers of desert to where Khufu and Khafre meet the sky. At dawn the plateau is grey and quiet; by mid-morning the silhouettes sharpen against the blue. The pool holds the same frame.
Description ⚙: Steigenberger Pyramids Cairo is a 132-room, 5-star hotel in Giza, 2.3 km from the Great Pyramid of Khufu and directly opposite the Grand Egyptian Museum. Pyramid View rooms and suites have private balconies facing the Giza plateau; the outdoor pool is oriented toward the same landmark. The property offers a restaurant, bar, fitness center, and spa.
Address: Pyramids, Cairo - Alexandria Desert Rd, Kafr Nassar, Al Haram, Giza Governorate 3515011, Egypt

Alt Text: Sheraton Cairo Hotel corner suite with wrap-around floor-to-ceiling windows showing panoramic Nile River view and Zamalek island

Images.alt: Sheraton Cairo Hotel corner suite with wrap-around floor-to-ceiling windows showing panoramic Nile River view and Zamalek island

Hotel Tagline: From the western bank at Galaa Square, the Nile bends wide below private balconies.
Summary: Since 1975 the tower has stood on the western bank at Galaa Square — one of the older addresses on the Corniche, and one with the broadest Nile arc. The private balconies lean out over the water, and on clear mornings the Pyramid outline appears behind the roofline to the west.
Description ⚙: Sheraton Cairo Hotel & Casino is a 665-room, 5-star hotel on the western bank of the Nile at Galaa Square in Dokki, opened in 1975 and operating from two connecting towers. Nile-view rooms include private balconies overlooking the river and the Zamalek embankment; the Botanica 25 bar on the upper floor extends that panorama toward the Giza Pyramids on clear days. The property features two outdoor pools, a spa, a casino, and multiple dining venues.
Address: Charles de Gaulle, Ad Doqi A, Dokki, Giza Governorate 3750412, Egypt
Nearest Airports: CAI · Cairo International · 23 km; SPX · Sphinx International · 42 km

Images.alt: Nizuc Resort Spa Cancun balcony at sunset with louvered panels framing the private beach and Caribbean pier at Punta Nizuc

Hotel Tagline: Two private beaches and the Mesoamerican Barrier Reef, visible from the master suites.
Summary: Set apart in Punta Nizuc with two private beaches — one exclusively for adults — and the Mesoamerican Barrier Reef just offshore. The master suites face the water directly. La Punta delivers Caribbean seafood with the reef as backdrop. Forbes Five-Star, 2026.
Description ⚙: Set apart in Punta Nizuc with two private beaches — one exclusively for adults — and the Mesoamerican Barrier Reef just offshore. Forbes Travel Guide Five-Star for 2024, 2025, and 2026. The master suites face the Caribbean directly; La Punta delivers fresh seafood with the reef as backdrop.
Address: Blvd. Kukulcan, 03 Km 21.26, Nizuc, Zona Hotelera, 77500 Cancún, Q.R., Mexico
Nearest Airports: CUN · Cancún · 14 km

Hotel Tagline: Every one of the 310 suites opens onto the Caribbean through a private terrace.
Summary: Reopened in May 2026 after a complete transformation, on the widest beach in the Hotel Zone. All 310 redesigned suites open onto the Caribbean via private terraces. The Swim-Up Suites connect directly to the main pool. Zen Spa handles the wellness program from an oceanfront gazebo.
Description ⚙: Reopened in May 2026 after a complete top-to-bottom renovation, on the widest stretch of beach in the Hotel Zone. All 310 redesigned suites open onto the Caribbean via private terraces; the Swim-Up Suites connect directly to the main pool. Adults-only, all-inclusive. Zen Spa handles the wellness program from an oceanfront gazebo.
Address: Blvd. Kukulcan KM 11.5, Punta Cancun, Zona Hotelera, 77500 Cancún, Q.R., Mexico

Hotel Tagline: From the northern tip of the Hotel Zone, the beach stretches toward Isla Mujeres beyond the pool.
Summary: A long meandering pool is all that separates this all-inclusive from the beach at Punta Cancun — the northern tip of the hotel zone. Upper floors extend views from the sand to Isla Mujeres. The Ocean Front Suite King on the 4th floor adds a panoramic balcony.
Description ⚙: A five-star all-inclusive at Punta Cancun — the northern tip of the Hotel Zone — where a meandering pool separates the resort from the beach. Upper floors extend views from the sand to Isla Mujeres in the distance. The Ocean Front Suite King on the 4th floor adds a panoramic private balcony.

Hotel Tagline: From the beach, Isla Mujeres and Isla Contoy appear where the Caribbean faces three directions.
Summary: Twelve miles north of the Hotel Zone, on the Isla Blanca peninsula where the Caribbean faces three directions. Opened in February 2024, AAA Five Diamond from the start. The Preferred Club adds butler service; Isla Mujeres and Isla Contoy are visible from the beach.
Description ⚙: Opened February 2024 on the Isla Blanca peninsula, twelve miles north of the Hotel Zone, where the Caribbean faces three directions. AAA Five Diamond from its first year of operation. The Preferred Club adds butler service and upgraded room categories; Isla Mujeres and Isla Contoy are visible from the beach on clear days.
Address: Carretera Punta Sam Manzana 2 Lote 10, 77400 Quintana Roo, Q.R., Mexico

Hotel Tagline: Isla Mujeres sits on the horizon from the rooftop infinity pool.
Summary: Opened in March 2024 in Costa Mujeres — the Caribbean debut of a brand known for design-driven luxury. All 88 suites are oceanfront with private terrace hot tubs; the rooftop infinity pool frames Isla Mujeres to the north. Booking.com score: 9.6 after its first year.
Description ⚙: Opened March 2024 in Costa Mujeres — the Caribbean debut of the brand behind Puerto Vallarta's AAA Five Diamond adults-only property. All 88 suites are oceanfront with private terrace hot tubs and bold architectural interiors. The rooftop infinity pool frames Isla Mujeres on the horizon. Booking.com score: 9.6 after its first year of operation.

Hotel Tagline: Between the Caribbean and a championship golf course, with the sea below the rooftop.
Summary: Art runs through every surface of this 593-suite resort between the Caribbean and a championship golf course. The Inspira suites reach an exclusive rooftop pool above the sea. María Dolores by Edgar Núñez — Michelin Plate 2024 and 2025 — is the table to book well ahead.
Description ⚙: Five-star adults-only all-inclusive resort positioned between the Caribbean and a championship golf course north of Cancun. The Inspira suites access an exclusive rooftop pool above the sea. María Dolores by Edgar Núñez — Michelin Plate 2024 and 2025 — is the signature dining experience. 13 restaurants total; NUUP Spa integrates Mayan healing practices.
Address: Complejo, Prolongacion, Bonampak S/N, 77400 Playa Azul, Q.R., Mexico
Nearest Airports: CUN · Cancún · 32 km

Hotel Tagline: Private coastline stretches unobstructed to the Caribbean beyond the infinity-style pool.
Summary: South of the Hotel Zone on a private stretch of Mayan Riviera coastline with 250 acres of landscaped grounds. The Serenity Club Swim-Out Junior Suite Ocean Front pairs an infinity-style pool with unobstructed sea views. Vora Mar is the Italian option; MySenses handles the spa.
Description ⚙: South of the Hotel Zone on a private stretch of Mayan Riviera coastline with 250 acres of landscaped grounds. Adults-only, all-inclusive. The Serenity Club Swim-Out Junior Suite Ocean Front pairs an infinity-style pool with unobstructed Caribbean views. Vora Mar handles Italian dining; MySenses Spa anchors the wellness program.
Address: Carretera Federal #307 km. 335, Juárez, 77569 Chetumal, Q.R., Mexico
Nearest Airports: CUN · Cancún · 12 km

Images.alt: JW Marriott Cancun Resort suite living area with arched floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking turquoise Caribbean Sea

Hotel Tagline: Caribbean views stretch from private balconies across the 12th and 14th floors.
Summary: Across the 12th and 14th floors, 80 Club91 suites share a panoramic lounge and private balconies facing the Caribbean. Sasi Thai is the dinner choice for sunset views. The Sens Yah Spa carries a Forbes four-star rating — one of the few hotel spas in Cancun to hold it.
Description ⚙: Five-star hotel (non-all-inclusive) on the Hotel Zone at Km 14.5. Across the 12th and 14th floors, 80 Club91 suites share a panoramic lounge and private balconies facing the Caribbean. Sasi Thai delivers outdoor dining with sea views. The Sens Yah Spa holds a Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star rating.
Address: Km 14.5, Blvd. Kukulcan Lote 40-A, Zona Hotelera, 77500 Cancún, Q.R., Mexico
Nearest Airports: CUN · Cancún · 21 km

Alt Text: Kempinski Hotel Cancun guest room with ornate balcony railings and turquoise Caribbean Sea view through large windows

Images.alt: Kempinski Hotel Cancun guest room with ornate balcony railings and turquoise Caribbean Sea view through large windows

Hotel Tagline: Private balconies look out over open Caribbean from every one of the 363 rooms.
Summary: Six restaurants, a powdery-white private beach, and a club level sequestered from the main hotel. The Club Seafront Room sits on a secluded floor with access to the private Club Lounge. Casitas puts outdoor Mexican dining against the Caribbean. KAYANTÁ Spa for the four-hands treatment.
Description ⚙: Five-star hotel on a powdery-white private beach with 363 rooms, all featuring private balconies with sea views. The Club Seafront Room occupies a secluded floor with access to the private Club Lounge. Six restaurants include Casitas, which delivers alfresco Mexican dining directly against the Caribbean. KAYANTÁ Spa specializes in multi-therapist treatments.
Address: Rtno. del Rey 36, Zona Hotelera, 77500 Cancún, Q.R., Mexico
Nearest Airports: CUN · Cancún · 20 km

Images.alt: Waldorf Astoria Riviera Maya Cancun outdoor fire pit lounge at dusk with palm trees and Caribbean Sea at the horizon

Hotel Tagline: Every balcony soaking tub faces open Caribbean, with no neighboring towers in sight.
Summary: Set on 100 acres of Mayan coastline between the airport and the Hotel Zone, 20 minutes south of the hotel strip. All 173 rooms and suites have ocean views and private balconies with soaking tubs. Malpeque handles the seafood alfresco, facing the sea. No neighboring towers.
Description ⚙: Set on 100 acres of Mayan coastline behind a mangrove nature preserve, 20 minutes south of the Hotel Zone. All 173 rooms and suites have direct ocean views and private balconies with soaking tubs. Malpeque is the signature seafood grill, serving alfresco against the Caribbean. The Waldorf Astoria Spa features 21 treatment rooms drawing on Mexican healing traditions.
Address: Carr Federal Libre 307 Cancun-Tulum 248-868, SM-36, MZ-1 LT-1-01 UPE-1E, 77580 Cancun, Q.R., Mexico
Nearest Airports: CUN · Cancún · 14 km

Alt Text: Stephanie Inn Cannon Beach aerial view at sunset with beach and Haystack Rock

Images.alt: Stephanie Inn Cannon Beach aerial view at sunset with beach and Haystack Rock

Hotel Tagline: Haystack Rock sits in the frame at dawn, its texture readable from oceanfront windows.
Summary: The rooms here face the Pacific directly, and Haystack Rock sits in the frame from most ocean-facing windows — close enough to read its texture at dawn. The only dedicated luxury inn in Cannon Beach: adults only, fireplaces in every room, and the kind of deliberate quiet that makes the tide audible at night. We’d book an oceanfront room and stay two nights minimum.
Address: 2740 Pacific Ave, Cannon Beach, OR 97110, USA
Nearest Airports: AST · Astoria · 38 km

Images.alt: Surfsand Resort Cannon Beach woman enjoys a drink facing Haystack Rock

Hotel Tagline: Haystack Rock stands in view from the balconies of this beachfront stretch of Oregon coast.
Summary: A 2024 renovation brought the rooms in line with what the position already offered — beachfront on one of the most photographed stretches of the Oregon coast, with Haystack Rock visible from many balconies. The spa and outdoor fire pits give it enough reason to stay on the property. The Wayfarer restaurant is worth booking separately.
Address: 148 W Gower Ave, Cannon Beach, OR 97110, USA
Nearest Airports: AST · Astoria · 35 km

Alt Text: Hallmark Resort and Spa Cannon Beach ocean view from room

Images.alt: Hallmark Resort and Spa Cannon Beach ocean view from room

Hotel Tagline: Ocean-facing rooms look out across the beach to the water, with Haystack Rock sitting to the south.
Summary: The ocean-facing rooms have unobstructed views across the beach to the water, with Haystack Rock visible to the south. The spa addition gives it a specific reason to stay beyond the view. We’d request a room on the higher floors for the widest sightline and use the evening to watch the light change on the rock.
Address: 1400 S Hemlock St, Cannon Beach, OR 97110, USA

Images.alt: The Ocean Lodge Cannon Beach seen from the beach at golden hour

Summary: The lodge sits directly on the beach, and the view from the ocean-facing rooms is exactly what the name suggests — open water, sea stacks, and the particular Pacific light that makes this coast worth the drive. Breakfast is included. We’d have it with the view and plan the rest of the day from there.

Images.alt: Tolovana Inn Cannon Beach beachfront hotel facade with pink sky

Hotel Tagline: From the quieter southern end, the ocean opens directly in front with the full beach stretching north.
Summary: At the quieter southern end of Cannon Beach, Tolovana faces the ocean from a position that removes most of the foot traffic. The rooms are straightforward, the beach access is immediate, and the rates reflect neither the view nor the address. We’d use it as a base for the full stretch of beach and drive up to dinner.

Summary: Three connected properties — The Waves, The Argonauta Inn, and The White Heron Lodge — positioned steps from the beach in central Cannon Beach. The ocean-view rooms face directly west; the distance to Haystack Rock is walkable. A practical address that offers more character than the price suggests.

Hotel Tagline: From the upper rooms, the Tillamook Head Lighthouse marks the northern horizon above open ocean.
Summary: An intimate property at the northern end of Cannon Beach, with ocean views from the upper rooms and direct beach access. The position is quieter than the central stretch, and the Tillamook Head Lighthouse sits on the horizon to the north — a view that distinguishes it from the standard Haystack Rock orientation.

Hotel: InterContinental Table Bay Cape Town by IHG

Lounge with green velvet seating and floor-to-ceiling windows framing Table Mountain and the harbour in Cape Town.

Hotel room with white bed and window view of Table Mountain over Cape Town's cityscape under cloudy skies.

Hotel Tagline: Atlantic Ocean, Table Mountain, and the V&A marina converge at a single window.
Summary: Reopened in late 2025 following a R1-billion redesign, Cape Town’s first InterContinental occupies the Waterfront’s most coveted footprint: Atlantic Ocean, Table Mountain, and the V&A marina converging at the window. The new Club Floor suites raise that view by two levels above the marina.
Description ⚙: Among the splendid Cape Town waterfront hotels, the five-star InterContinental Table Bay Cape Town by IHG occupies the V&A Waterfront's most coveted footprint, with Atlantic Ocean, Table Mountain, and the harbour marina converging at the window. The property reopened in late 2025 following a R1-billion redesign as Cape Town's first InterContinental. The large rooms and suites are elegantly decorated and flooded with natural light; guests decide between a mountain-facing or sea-facing orientation. The Camelot Spa, a heated pool, and multiple dining venues complete the offering at this landmark address.
Address: Quay 6, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town, 8001, South Africa
Nearest Airports: CPT · Cape Town · 23 km

Alt Text: The Silo Hotel Cape Town suite bathroom with freestanding bathtub and signature Heatherwick faceted diamond windows overlooking Lion’s Head and the Cape Town city bowl

Images.alt: The Silo Hotel Cape Town suite bathroom with freestanding bathtub and signature Heatherwick faceted diamond windows overlooking Lion’s Head and the Cape Town city bowl
Room at The Silo Hotel with geometric windows framing Table Mountain and Cape Town cityscape, orange chair and white bed.
Restaurant at The Silo Hotel with faceted windows framing Cape Town city, hills and harbour at sunset.

Hotel Tagline: From the 8th floor up, Table Mountain, Signal Hill, Table Bay, and the V&A Waterfront form an unbroken arc.
Summary: For the full 360° of Cape Town, we’d request a Deluxe Superior suite on floors 8–10: Table Mountain, Signal Hill, Table Bay, and the V&A Waterfront in an unbroken arc. The 11th-floor rooftop pool and Silo restaurant sit above them all; the Willaston Bar, five floors below, is the after-dark alternative.
Description ⚙: The Silo Hotel, with its remarkable rooftop terrace housing a pool, bar, and restaurant, offers a 360-degree view over Cape Town capturing Table Mountain, the Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, and Table Bay stretching towards Robben Island. The architectural marvel shares its location with the Zeitz Museum of Contemporary Art Africa. Hotel rooms and suites exude pure luxury; the Royal Suites and Penthouse feature cinema rooms and private balconies. The Willaston Bar on the 6th floor and the rooftop Silo Restaurant on the 11th are both integral to the experience.
View Subjects: Table Mountain, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Robben Island
Address: Silo Square, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town, 8001, South Africa
Nearest Airports: CPT · Cape Town · 22 km

Alt Text: One and Only Cape Town suite living room with floor-to-ceiling windows framing Table Mountain and Lion’s Head above the city bowl

Images.alt: One and Only Cape Town suite living room with floor-to-ceiling windows framing Table Mountain and Lion’s Head above the city bowl
Balcony with table and chairs overlooking marina, waterfront buildings and Table Mountain at One&Only Cape Town.
Guest room at One&Only Cape Town with floor-to-ceiling windows framing Table Mountain at dusk.

Hotel Tagline: Table Mountain fills the glass from lobby to bathtub, the waterfront threaded below.
Summary: The Table Mountain Suite in Marina Rise sets the benchmark: 316 sqm, multiple bathrooms, and a bathtub positioned to face the mountain through oversized glass. For something quieter, the island suites on Isola trade the panorama for a world ringed by waterways and palms — a different kind of immersion entirely.
Description ⚙: Positioned in the Waterfront's most exclusive section, One&Only Cape Town dazzles guests with picture-perfect views of Table Mountain, carefully framed by the triple-height windows in the lobby, lounge, and bar. The Table Mountain Suite in Marina Rise spans 316 sqm and includes a bathtub positioned to face the mountain. The adjacent island of Isola adds a resort dimension, with suites ringed by waterways and palms. Nobu restaurant and the Vista Bar & Lounge complete an address recognised as one of Cape Town's finest.
Address: One&Only Cape Town, 0C Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town, 8001, South Africa
Nearest Airports: CPT · Cape Town · 23 km

Hotel: Mount Nelson, A Belmond Hotel, Cape Town
Alt Text: Mount Nelson A Belmond Hotel Cape Town outdoor pool with sun loungers surrounded by manicured gardens with Table Mountain rising directly behind the pink facade

Hotel Tagline: Nine acres of garden frame the unbroken sight line towards Table Mountain.
Summary: The garden-facing Mountain View suites at this 1890 Belmond icon look directly towards Table Mountain across nine acres of manicured grounds — one of the city’s most enduring vantage points. Afternoon tea on the terrace, below the pink façade, remains an unmissable Cape Town ritual.
Description ⚙: The iconic Mount Nelson Hotel, dating back to 1890, not only offers breathtaking views of Table Mountain but also distinguishes itself through impeccable service, exceptional amenities, opulent decor, and lush surroundings. Its soft pink exterior frames nine acres of manicured gardens. The Graham Viney-designed Presidential Suite stands as one of the finest suites in the country. Multiple pools, a tennis court, a fitness centre, and multiple dining venues including the award-winning The Verandah define this Belmond landmark.
Address: 76 Orange St, Gardens, Cape Town, 8001, South Africa
Nearest Airports: CPT · Cape Town · 21 km

Images.alt: The Westin Cape Town suite living room with floor-to-ceiling windows facing Lion’s Head and the Cape Town city bowl
Lounge with armchairs and tables facing floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking the harbor and sea in Cape Town.
Indoor pool at The Westin Cape Town with floor-to-ceiling windows framing harbor views at sunset, loungers alongside.

Hotel Tagline: Floor-to-ceiling glass frames Table Mountain, Lion's Head, and the V&A Waterfront in one unobstructed sweep.
Summary: The pick here is an Executive Waterfront View room on floors 14–18, where Table Mountain, Lion’s Head, and the V&A Waterfront converge through floor-to-ceiling glass in a single unobstructed sweep. The 19th-floor Club Lounge extends that same view into a private evening setting above the city.
Description ⚙: The Westin Cape Town, at the gateway of the V&A Waterfront, impresses with its glass facade and floor-to-ceiling windows showcasing Table Mountain, Lion's Head, and the Atlantic Ocean. Every room offers magnificent views. Executive Waterfront View accommodations on floors 14 to 18 grant access to the exclusive 19th-floor Club Lounge. The award-winning Heavenly Spa and a panoramic indoor infinity lap pool complete the offering at this CBD landmark.
View Subjects: Table Mountain, Camps Bay & Lion's Head, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront
Address: Convention Square, Lower Long St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town, 8000, South Africa
Nearest Airports: CPT · Cape Town · 21 km

Rooftop terrace at Taj Cape Town with fire pit, sofas, and Table Mountain views over the city at dusk.
Taj Cape Town room with dining setup and floor-to-ceiling windows framing Table Mountain and the city below.

Hotel Tagline: Floor-to-ceiling glass frames Table Mountain from the city's historic core, beside St. George's Cathedral.
Summary: Worth staying for the Mountain View rooms, which frame Table Mountain through floor-to-ceiling glass from the city’s historic core — right beside St. George’s Cathedral and the Company’s Garden. The J Wellness Circle’s indoor lap pool, sitting directly below the mountain, rounds out a rare CBD package.
Description ⚙: The opulent Taj Cape Town, a five-star retreat in the heart of the city, sits across from St. George's Cathedral and the Company's Garden, at the very birthplace of Cape Town. The breathtaking views of Table Mountain from the Mountain View guest rooms are unparalleled in the CBD. The J Wellness Circle spa features a heated indoor lap pool, sauna, and Indian-inspired therapies. Bombay Brasserie and Mint Restaurant are the two dining establishments, combining culinary excellence with the hotel's historic setting.
Address: 1 Wale Street corner Saint Georges Mall, Wale St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town, 8000, South Africa
Nearest Airports: CPT · Cape Town · 21 km

Hotel Tagline: Between the Twelve Apostles overhead and the Atlantic spread below, the two views divide the hotel between them.
Summary: Ask for an ocean-facing suite for the Atlantic at its widest, or a mountain room that puts the Twelve Apostles directly overhead. The outdoor spa gazebo, open to the sea breeze and perfumed by fynbos, is the experience worth building an afternoon around; the heated infinity pool faces the same ocean view.
Description ⚙: At the foot of the Twelve Apostles range, cocooned by the Table Mountain National Park, the Twelve Apostles Hotel & Spa offers a secluded haven on the Atlantic scenic route. The impeccably styled suites offer views of the majestic Twelve Apostles mountain range or the Atlantic Ocean. The outdoor massage gazebo, overlooking the Atlantic and perfumed by fynbos, is the signature experience. The Azure Restaurant, voted South Africa's best hotel restaurant in 2020 at the World Culinary Awards, and an on-site cinema distinguish this property from the Cape Town hotel landscape.
Address: Victoria Rd, Camps Bay, Cape Town, 8005, South Africa
Nearest Airports: CPT · Cape Town · 30 km

Hotel Tagline: Floor-to-ceiling glass frames Lion's Head and the open Atlantic from every suite.
Summary: Every suite here faces the Atlantic through floor-to-ceiling sliding glass, with a private terrace jacuzzi included as standard across all categories. Higher floors extend the sightline past Lion’s Head toward the open ocean; the Baptiste rooftop pool bar is the sunset spot on the Camps Bay strip.
Description ⚙: For those seeking a romantic getaway in Camps Bay, The Marly Boutique Hotel is positioned on the trendy sunset strip. Every suite faces the sea through floor-to-ceiling sliding glass doors and features a private terrace jacuzzi. The Baptiste rooftop pool bar and lounge is the sunset spot on the Camps Bay strip. Japanese restaurant Umi on site adds a dining option that complements the abundance of bars and restaurants along Victoria Road.
Address: The Promenade, 201 Victoria Rd, Camps Bay, Cape Town, 8005, South Africa
Nearest Airports: CPT · Cape Town · 26 km

Hotel Tagline: From a clifftop below Lion's Head, Atlantic, Clifton, Camps Bay, and mountain meet in one unbroken sweep.
Summary: Six rooms on a clifftop below Lion’s Head means guaranteed privacy at this boutique mansion. The 250 sqm Presidential Penthouse covers the Atlantic, Clifton, Camps Bay, and the mountain in a single 360° sweep — an unobstructed position that few Cape Town hotels can match at any price point.
Description ⚙: At the luxurious 21 Nettleton boutique hotel, an exclusive clifftop mansion sitting just below Lion's Head summit, the Atlantic vistas are truly unparalleled. With only six rooms and suites, including the 250 sqm top-floor Presidential Penthouse that offers 360-degree views of the ocean, garden, and mountain, this boutique hotel provides an exclusive and intimate setting. Each accommodation features bespoke art personally sourced by owner Hugo from around the world. The nearby Lion's Head hiking trail is among the city's most popular.
Address: 21 Nettleton Rd, Clifton, Cape Town, 8005, South Africa
Nearest Airports: CPT · Cape Town · 26 km

Living area at South Beach Camps Bay Boutique Hotel overlooking a pool, palm trees and the ocean in Cape Town.

Hotel Tagline: Lion's Head frames the right edge of the balcony as the Atlantic fills everything else.
Summary: The Atlantic is 50 metres from the front door, and the balcony rooms face it directly with Lion’s Head framing the right edge. After the beach, the sun terrace makes the most of the evening sequence: the mountain catching the last light as the Camps Bay promenade settles into dinner hour.
Description ⚙: The South Beach Camps Bay Boutique Hotel sets the bar for luxury, style, and sophistication in Camps Bay. With the Atlantic Ocean 50 metres from the front door and balcony rooms facing it directly with Lion's Head in frame, this five-star boutique property offers spectacular views of the azure sea, rugged boulders, and surrounding mountains. An inviting pool, fully equipped kitchen, modern gym, and a sun terrace complement the beachfront location.
Address: 6 Link Street, Camps Bay, Cape Town, 8005, South Africa

Suite at O' Two Hotel with floor-to-ceiling windows framing Table Mountain and Cape Town at dusk.

Hotel Tagline: From the balcony on Beach Road, Table Mountain, Lion's Head, and the Atlantic open without interruption.
Summary: At Mouille Point, the Penthouse Suites deliver the most unobstructed waterfront panorama in this section of the city: Table Mountain, Lion’s Head, DHL Stadium, and the Atlantic from a generous balcony on Beach Road. The heated rooftop pool with full bar service extends the same view into the evening.

Suite at Canopy by Hilton Cape Town with purple sofa and floor-to-ceiling windows framing Table Mountain.

Hotel Tagline: Table Mountain fills the window closer and more directly than any Waterfront hotel angle.
Summary: The Mountain View rooms and suites at Cape Town’s first Canopy by Hilton, opened in June 2025 in a 113-year-old heritage building in Gardens, place Table Mountain immediately in the window — sharper and closer than any Waterfront angle. Restaurant Ongetem, helmed by Bertus Basson, is the dining draw.
Description ⚙: Opened in June 2025 as Africa's first Canopy by Hilton, this 154-room hotel occupies a revitalised complex of 113-year-old heritage buildings in the Longkloof district of Gardens. The Mountain View rooms and suites place Table Mountain immediately in the window at a closer and more direct angle than most Waterfront hotels. Restaurant Ongetem, led by acclaimed chef Bertus Basson, delivers refined modern South African cooking. The Longkloof location provides walking distance access to Kloof Street and the Table Mountain Aerial Cableway.

Couple seated on a balcony with breakfast and mimosas overlooking the ocean at Radisson Collection, Cape Town.

Hotel Tagline: From the infinity pool above Beach Road, Robben Island and Table Mountain share the horizon.
Summary: Reopened in December 2024 after a full redesign, Cape Town’s first Radisson Collection brings floor-to-ceiling Atlantic views to 175 rooms. The infinity pool above Beach Road frames Robben Island in the distance and Table Mountain on the horizon, with a spa to extend the stay into the evening.
Description ⚙: Reopened in December 2024 after a full redesign as Cape Town's first Radisson Collection, this 175-room hotel delivers floor-to-ceiling Atlantic views from most of its rooms. The infinity pool above Beach Road frames Robben Island in the distance and Table Mountain on the horizon. The Amani Spa, a restaurant, and a bar with sweeping ocean views complete the offering at this Beach Road address between the V&A marina and the open Atlantic.

Description ⚙: The iconic Cape Grace, under the Fairmont umbrella since late 2023, occupies its own private quay in the V&A Waterfront. The ship-shaped design provides harbour views on one side and a superyacht marina with Signal Hill and Table Mountain on the other. The rooftop room with its Table Mountain-facing balcony is the standout accommodation. Heirloom restaurant, opened in May 2024 under chef Gregory Czarnecki, offers an eight-course tasting menu. The Bascule whisky bar, relaunched in June 2024, pours one of the city's most distinguished whisky collections.

Hotel Tagline: From the top-floor balcony, the Atlantic horizon runs unobstructed to the edge of the visible world.
Summary: The top-floor suite — private balcony, jacuzzi, and an unobstructed Atlantic horizon running to the edge of the visible world — is the standout room at this Bantry Bay boutique address. Below it, the 22-metre infinity pool extends the same ocean view at water level, with Clifton 1st and Saunders Rock beaches a short walk downhill.
Description ⚙: Recognised as one of Cape Town's premier boutique hotels, Compass House in Bantry Bay features rooms with breathtaking ocean vistas and a top-floor suite with a private balcony and jacuzzi overlooking the Atlantic. The 22-metre infinity pool extends the ocean view at water level. Clifton 1st and Saunders Rock beaches are a short walk downhill. Adults-only, the property emphasises an atmosphere of tranquility and impeccable service.

Hotel Tagline: Table Mountain, Lion's Head, and Signal Hill align in a single frame from the terrace rooms.
Summary: Three terrace rooms secure the clearest sightlines at this eight-room Oranjezicht boutique hotel: Table Mountain, Lion’s Head, and Signal Hill in a single frame. The rooftop deck adds the Atlantic to the horizon, with the Table Mountain National Park trailheads beginning at the gate.
Description ⚙: Perched high in the Oranjezicht area, Camissa House offers stunning panoramic views of Table Mountain, Lion's Head, Signal Hill, and the city. Located within the Table Mountain National Park, with walking trails to the mountain and a five-minute drive to Cape Town City Centre. Only eight rooms offer breathtaking vistas of the city's iconic landmarks. The three luxury terrace rooms with unobstructed sightlines, a rooftop deck, and a pool define this intimate boutique address.
View Subjects: Table Mountain, Camps Bay & Lion's Head

Hotel Tagline: Atlantic rollers, the Twelve Apostles rising directly behind the beach beyond the glass.
Summary: Sea-facing rooms at this intimate Camps Bay hotel look over the Atlantic with the Twelve Apostles rising directly behind the beach. The heated pool sits steps from the sand, making for a straightforward morning choice: sunrise over the mountains, then the ocean. The bar handles the rest.
Description ⚙: This intimate, sea-facing hotel in Camps Bay harmoniously blends with its surroundings, using elements of nature such as timber, stone, and glass. Sea-facing rooms look over the Atlantic with the Twelve Apostles rising directly behind the beach. The heated pool sits steps from the sand. Beach service, a 24-hour gym, and a cocktail bar allow guests to move between the beach and the hotel with ease. Kirstenbosch National Botanical Garden and Table Mountain trails are within easy reach.

Hotel Tagline: From Lion's Head, Clifton's beaches give way to Camps Bay across a full sweep of ocean.
Summary: Request a suite with an ocean-facing balcony at this 1906 Edwardian mansion on Lion’s Head for the full sweep from Bantry Bay — Clifton’s beaches below, Camps Bay in the middle distance. The two fully staffed private villas, covering 1,105 sqm and 400 sqm respectively, redefine the category entirely.
Description ⚙: Built in 1906 in the Cape Edwardian style, Ellerman House is a one-and-a-half-acre estate on Lion's Head offering top-notch quality, impeccable service, and panoramic ocean views from every room and suite. The art collection spans Gerard Sekoto and George Pemba. Two self-contained, fully staffed three-bedroom villas, one covering 1,105 square metres and the other 400 square metres, provide an even more exclusive option. A heated outdoor pool, spa, and extensive library complete this Bantry Bay landmark.
Address: 180 Kloof Rd, Bantry Bay, Cape Town, 8005, South Africa
Nearest Airports: CPT · Cape Town · 27 km

Hotel Tagline: Lion's Head rises directly from the Seahorse suite's top-floor balcony, the Atlantic spread below.
Summary: The Seahorse suite on the top floor is the room to request at this adults-only Camps Bay guesthouse: panoramic balcony, direct sightline to Lion’s Head, and the Atlantic from the bed. Sunset drinks on the terrace are a quiet ritual the staff here facilitate for every arriving guest.
Description ⚙: Located in Camps Bay and perfumed by the maritime breeze, the adults-only Azamare Guest House offers enchanting vistas of the ocean and mountains. The Seahorse luxury suite on the top floor presents an unobstructed vista of Lion's Head alongside panoramic ocean views from a private balcony. All seven rooms open to balconies or patios facing the Atlantic. A hot tub, outdoor pool, and complimentary evening drinks on the terrace are hallmarks of the guest experience.
Address: 133 Victoria Rd, Camps Bay, Cape Town, 8005, South Africa

Hotel Tagline: From the seventh-floor rooftop pool, the Atlantic opens wide across Cascais Bay.
Summary: Opened in 2024 on the site of the century-old Hotel Paris, EVOLUTION’s rooftop infinity pool delivers a wide-angle sweep over the Atlantic from Estoril. Sea-view rooms on the upper floors come with Atlantic balconies as standard. The seventh floor is the one to ask for.
Description ⚙: The EVOLUTION Cascais-Estoril Hotel opened in 2024 on the site of the century-old Hotel Paris, directly on the Estoril seafront. The seventh-floor rooftop infinity pool delivers a wide Atlantic sweep from Cascais Bay toward the coast, with The Upper Deck restaurant at the same level. Sea-view rooms on the upper floors have direct Atlantic balconies. The Wellness by Sayanna spa on the sixth floor maintains the same ocean sightline. Two restaurants, live music, and a lifestyle-forward approach complete the offer.
Address: Av. Marginal 7034, 2765-587 Estoril, Portugal

Hotel Tagline: From a clifftop perch, Conceição and Rainha beaches stretch below across Cascais Bay.
Summary: Perched on a cliff at the heart of Cascais, The Albatroz keeps the Atlantic close — the seaside pool sits at the edge of the rock, and most of the 52 rooms look across Conceição and Rainha beaches. Ask for a sea-view room on the upper floors of the historic wing.
Description ⚙: Perched atop a cliff at the center of Cascais, The Albatroz Hotel commands views of the Atlantic Ocean and the Conceição and Rainha beaches from most of its 52 rooms and suites. The seaside swimming pool sits at the edge of the rock, and the panoramic restaurant serves breakfast with the ocean as backdrop. Wine-tasting sessions in the old kitchen and personalized service define the character of this historic clifftop property.
Address: R. Frederico Arouca 100, 2750-353 Cascais, Portugal
Nearest Airports: LIS · Lisbon · 34 km

Summary: The Royal Suite on the ninth floor frames 180° of Atlantic and Cascais Bay from a single panoramic terrace. Below it, the third-floor infinity pool reads the same ocean from a wider angle. We’d book a terrace room and leave the balcony door open at sunrise.
Description ⚙: The Hotel Cascais Miragem Health & Spa stands at the entrance to Cascais with panoramic Atlantic Ocean and Cascais Bay views. The Royal Suite on the ninth floor opens onto a terrace with a 180-degree ocean panorama. The third-floor infinity pool faces the same sea, and the Miragem Water Lounge adds whirlpools, saunas, and relaxation pools. Most rooms and suites have spacious terraces, and Ratas Beach lies directly across the street.

Hotel Tagline: Atlantic views reach most of the 124 rooms along Cascais' western seafront.
Summary: Once the private residence of exiled Italian King Umberto II, Grande Real Villa Itália sits on Cascais’ western seafront with Atlantic views from most of its 124 rooms — and the Majestic and Royal penthouses extending that panorama across the rooftop.
Description ⚙: Once the private residence of exiled Italian King Umberto II, the Grande Real Villa Itália Hotel & Spa occupies a seafront position on the western edge of Cascais. Atlantic Ocean views extend from most of its 124 rooms and suites, and the Majestic and Royal penthouses bring panoramic rooftop terraces. Restaurants and bars face the sea with abundant natural light, and the Real Spa Therapy covers approximately 1,000 square meters with thalassotherapy and a full treatment menu.

Hotel Tagline: Balconies hang directly above the Atlantic, with the Sintra range and Cape Roca at the horizon.
Summary: A 17th-century fortress on the cliffs above Guincho Beach, 15 minutes west of Cascais — the view takes in the open Atlantic, the Sintra range, and Cape Roca. The junior suites have open balconies directly above the sea. The Michelin-starred restaurant makes the case for an overnight stay.
Description ⚙: Hotel Fortaleza do Guincho occupies a 17th-century fortress on the Atlantic cliffs above Guincho Beach, 15 minutes west of Cascais. The view from its junior suites — which have open balconies directly above the sea — spans the Atlantic horizon, the Sintra mountain range, and Cape Roca, Europe's westernmost point. The hotel holds a Michelin star and is affiliated with Relais & Châteaux. The Spot by Fortaleza do Guincho provides casual dining above the water, and the fortified Sala das Damas opens fully to the ocean.
Address: Estrada do Guincho 2413, 2750-642 Cascais, Portugal
Nearest Airports: LIS · Lisbon · 37 km

Images.alt: InterContinental Cascais-Estoril aerial view of pool gardens and seafront promenade with Cascais Marina and Atlantic Ocean

Hotel Tagline: Every balcony at this Cascais-to-Estoril stretch faces the Atlantic without obstruction.
Summary: Every room at the InterContinental Cascais-Estoril comes with a balcony facing the Atlantic — a standard that holds from the entry category upward. The Bago du Vin restaurant extends the same ocean-facing logic to dinner. Request an upper floor for the clearest read of Cascais Bay.
Description ⚙: Erected on the site of the former Hotel Atlântico and opened in 2015, the InterContinental Cascais-Estoril guarantees Atlantic Ocean balconies from every room category. Its position between Cascais and Estoril gives all rooms an unobstructed ocean sightline. The Bago du Vin restaurant features an Atlantic-facing terrace, and the hotel's ocean-facing pool overlooks the same coast. Tamariz Beach is a short walk from the entrance.
Address: Av. Marginal 8023, 2765-249 Estoril, Portugal
Nearest Airports: LIS · Lisbon · 30 km

Images.alt: Palácio Estoril Hotel breakfast table on stone balcony overlooking Estoril Gardens and Cascais Bay

Hotel Tagline: Upper floors look out over the Estoril Gardens to Cascais Bay beyond.
Summary: Open since 1930, Palácio Estoril carries its wartime history alongside views over the Estoril Gardens and Cascais Bay. The Superior Deluxe rooms on the upper floors of the main building return the clearest Atlantic sightlines. The Banyan Tree Spa is worth an afternoon of its own.
Description ⚙: The Palácio Estoril Hotel has been open since 1930, hosting European royalty and wartime diplomats. Superior Deluxe rooms on the upper floors of the main building deliver sea views over the Estoril Gardens and Cascais Bay. The Banyan Tree Spa occupies the adjacent wellness center. Indoor and outdoor pools, the Four Seasons Grill, and the historic Bar Estoril complete the offer at this landmark property on Portugal's Riviera coast.
Address: R. Particular, 2769-504 Estoril, Portugal
