Images.alt: The St. Regis on the Bund Shanghai corner guest room with floor-to-ceiling windows and view of the Huangpu River, Oriental Pearl Tower and Pudong skyline

Hotel Tagline: Pudong's skyline and the Huangpu River fill the glass from the southern Bund corridor.
Summary: Every room delivers Bund views, but the framing depends on the floor. The Shanghai John Jacob Astor Suite eliminates all ambiguity — panoramic Bund and Pudong sightlines from a hotel that opened in October 2024 and set a high benchmark for the southern Bund corridor immediately.
Description ⚙: The St. Regis on the Bund, Shanghai opened in October 2024 following acquisition and repositioning of the property. The hotel offers 192 rooms and suites along the historic Bund with staggering views of the Huangpu River, the futuristic Pudong skyline, and Shanghai's most famous landmarks. Set along the historic Bund and a short drive to Nanjing Road, this hotel features Art Deco-inspired interiors integrating architecture from different cultures. The 192 rooms range from 45 to 288 square yards; certain units have Bund views while others provide upgraded Panoramic Bund views. The Shanghai John Jacob Astor Suite offers unambiguous panoramic sightlines over the Bund and Pudong. St. Regis Butler Service is standard across all room categories.
View Subjects: Oriental Pearl Tower, Pudong Skyline, Huangpu River
Address: Dong 2nd East Road, 538 Zhong Shan Lu, 四牌楼 Huang Pu Qu, Shang Hai Shi, China, 200010
Nearest Airports: SHA · Hongqiao · 23 km; PVG · Pudong · 41 km

Hotel: Fairmont Peace Hotel on the Bund
Alt Text: Fairmont Peace Hotel on the Bund outdoor terrace dining table overlooking the Huangpu River and full Pudong skyline with Oriental Pearl Tower

Images.alt: Fairmont Peace Hotel on the Bund outdoor terrace dining table overlooking the Huangpu River and full Pudong skyline with Oriental Pearl Tower

Hotel Tagline: From the fifth to seventh floors, the Oriental Pearl Tower and Pudong skyline rise across the Huangpu River.
Summary: The Nine Nations Suites, floors five through seven, share the same Bund and Pearl Tower panorama — seen from a hotel whose pyramidal green roof has marked the skyline since 1929. Worth staying for the Sunday tea dance and the Old Jazz Band as much as for the view.
Description ⚙: Fairmont Peace Hotel on the Bund, with its iconic green pyramidal roof, has graced Shanghai's skyline since 1929, embodying an unmatched charisma and elegance. English actor Charlie Chaplin once favored this historic riverside address as his Shanghai hotel of choice (then the Cathay Hotel). The hotel has hosted presidential speeches, inspired acclaimed novels, and served as a filming location for Steven Spielberg's Empire of the Sun. The Cathay Room serves modern French cuisine, and Dragon Phoenix is a Shanghainese restaurant. The Nine Nations Suites, located between the fifth and seventh floors, reflect nine different countries' styles while sharing a magnificent river and skyline vista. The hotel features 270 guestrooms including 39 suites, the Willow Stream Spa with 11 treatment rooms, and a private sky-lit swimming pool. The Sunday tea dance and nightly Old Jazz Band performances are among the hotel's cultural traditions.
View Subjects: Oriental Pearl Tower, Pudong Skyline, Huangpu River
Address: 20 Nan Jing Dong Lu, Waitan, Huang Pu Qu, Shang Hai Shi, China, 200002
Nearest Airports: SHA · Hongqiao · 23 km; PVG · Pudong · 42 km

Alt Text: Regent Shanghai Pudong indoor infinity pool with mirrored ceiling reflecting the Pudong skyline and Oriental Pearl Tower through floor-to-ceiling windows

Images.alt: Regent Shanghai Pudong indoor infinity pool with mirrored ceiling reflecting the Pudong skyline and Oriental Pearl Tower through floor-to-ceiling windows

Hotel Tagline: From the jade-tiled pool on the 41st floor, the Pearl Tower anchors the full Lujiazui skyline.
Summary: The infinity pool on the 41st floor, jade-tiled and set against the full Lujiazui skyline, is the most photogenic reason to book. The Pearl Tower is the anchor; the two-bedroom Pearl River View Suite on the 37th and 38th floors keeps it in frame around the clock.
Description ⚙: Regent Shanghai Pudong stands in the 21st Century Tower at the heart of Lujiazui, surrounded by three of the city's most recognizable supertall buildings. The hotel's Art Deco-inspired rooms are finished in cream and chocolate tones with accents of China red, all with floor-to-ceiling windows delivering Pearl Tower and Lujiazui skyline views. Marble bathrooms feature deep-soaking tubs, separate rain showers, and twin gold-inlaid vanities. The two-bedroom Pearl River View Suite occupies the 37th and 38th floors with clear sightlines to the Pearl Tower. The three-bedroom Pearl View Presidential Suite extends to 432 square meters. Shang-Xi, the hotel's Cantonese restaurant, seats 22 in its main hall and offers five private dining rooms named after precious gemstones. Camelia serves authentic Italian cuisine with indoor and outdoor seating. FLARE Spa occupies the 41st floor alongside a jade-tiled infinity-edge pool and hot tub with panoramic views of Shanghai's financial district and the Pearl Tower. Regent Shanghai Pudong is recommended by the MICHELIN Guide.
Address: 210 Shi Ji Da Dao, Lujiazui, Pu Dong Xin Qu, Shang Hai Shi, China, 200120
Nearest Airports: SHA · Hongqiao · 24 km; PVG · Pudong · 38 km

Alt Text: Grand Hyatt Shanghai guest room with red upholstered sofas and panoramic wrap-around windows overlooking the Oriental Pearl Tower and Pudong skyline

Images.alt: Grand Hyatt Shanghai guest room with red upholstered sofas and panoramic wrap-around windows overlooking the Oriental Pearl Tower and Pudong skyline

Hotel Tagline: From the 87th floor, the Bund and Pearl Tower sit level with the clouds below.
Summary: The Jin Mao Tower has been part of Pudong’s skyline since 1999. Cloud 9 on the 87th floor still holds the views; rooms on the 70th floor and above frame the Pearl Tower and the Bund at close range. The 33-story hotel atrium spiraling below is a view unto itself.
Description ⚙: Grand Hyatt Shanghai crowns the Jin Mao Tower — the 88-story pagoda-inspired landmark that has defined Pudong's skyline since the building opened in 1999 — occupying floors 53 through 87 of the 420.5-meter structure. The hotel's most dramatic architectural feature is its 33-story barrel-vaulted atrium beginning on the 56th floor, a 27-meter-wide void that spirals 115 meters upward to the building's crown. The 548 rooms and suites are distributed across 18 different room styles, all with floor-to-ceiling windows and views of the Bund, the Pearl Tower, and the Huangpu River. Canton on the 55th floor occupies the full level and serves traditional Cantonese cuisine. Club Jin Mao on the 86th floor delivers Shanghainese cuisine with panoramic views. Cloud 9 on the 87th floor is the hotel's sky bar, voted by Forbes as one of the world's best bars in 2000. The ON56 collection on the 56th floor encompasses Kobachi (Japanese), Cucina (Italian), and The Grill (seafood and prime meats). The Club Oasis Spa and Sky Pool occupy the 57th floor.
View Subjects: The Bund, Oriental Pearl Tower, Huangpu River
Address: 88 Shi Ji Da Dao, Lujiazui, Pu Dong Xin Qu, Shang Hai Shi, China, 200120
Nearest Airports: SHA · Hongqiao · 24 km; PVG · Pudong · 39 km

Hotel: Waldorf Astoria Shanghai on the Bund
Alt Text: Waldorf Astoria Shanghai on the Bund suite bedroom with mahogany four-poster bed and window view of Oriental Pearl Tower and Pudong skyline

Images.alt: Waldorf Astoria Shanghai on the Bund suite bedroom with mahogany four-poster bed and window view of Oriental Pearl Tower and Pudong skyline

Hotel Tagline: Floor-to-ceiling windows in Suite 304 frame Pudong's skyline across the Huangpu River.
Summary: The Tower wing’s 20 suites have painted timber panelling and polished mahogany — and Pudong’s skyline through floor-to-ceiling windows. Ask for Suite 304 for the most direct Bund read. The Long Bar, 34 meters of original Shanghai Club architecture, offers the same view with a cocktail.
Description ⚙: Located at No. 2 on the Bund, the Waldorf Astoria Shanghai on the Bund offers guests breathtaking views of the Huangpu River and the Pudong skyline, with iconic landmarks such as the Oriental Pearl Tower, Shanghai Tower, and Jin Mao Tower. The hotel spans two buildings: the historic Astoria Club, which preserves original features from its days as the exclusive Shanghai Club, and the Astoria Tower, with 240 exquisitely appointed rooms looking onto Sichuan Road and 20 top-notch suites overlooking the Bund. The Bund-facing Suite 304 is the most direct view room. Wai King Kok serves exquisite Shanghainese, Cantonese, and Huaiyang specialties. The Grand Brasserie provides a mix of Asian-inspired and European dishes for all-day dining. The Long Bar — a historic space once famous for being the longest bar in the Far East at 34 meters — invites guests to enjoy classic cocktails in a timeless ambiance reminiscent of old Shanghai.
View Subjects: Oriental Pearl Tower, Pudong Skyline, Huangpu River
Address: 2 Zhong Shan Dong Yi Lu, Waitan, Huang Pu Qu, Shang Hai Shi, China, 200002
Nearest Airports: SHA · Hongqiao · 22 km; PVG · Pudong · 42 km

Alt Text: The Ritz-Carlton Shanghai Pudong FLAIR rooftop terrace at night with Oriental Pearl Tower illuminated in pink at close range and the Bund below

Images.alt: The Ritz-Carlton Shanghai Pudong FLAIR rooftop terrace at night with Oriental Pearl Tower illuminated in pink at close range and the Bund below

Hotel Tagline: From the 58th floor, the Pearl Tower fills the window while the Bund waits across the river.
Summary: FLAIR on the 58th floor has one of the city’s most dramatic bar views — the Pearl Tower fills the window and the Bund sits across the river. We’d time a dinner at Jin Xuan on the 53rd floor, Michelin one-star for nine consecutive years, and pair it with a Premier Bund View Suite.
Description ⚙: The Ritz-Carlton Shanghai, Pudong is a luxurious escape set atop a 58-story tower in the heart of the financial district, allowing for omnipresent views of Shanghai's skyline, the Huangpu River, the Oriental Pearl Tower, and the Bund on the opposite shore. Every detail captures the elegance of the Art Deco era, from the rich wood and chrome accents in the lobby to the refined cream and mahogany tones in the rooms and suites, each featuring floor-to-ceiling windows and marble bathrooms with signature gold accents. The Premier Bund View Suite grants access to the Club Lounge and delivers jaw-dropping vistas of the Oriental Pearl Tower and the Bund. FLAIR on the 58th floor serves Asian-inspired dishes and handcrafted cocktails alongside dramatic views. The Michelin-starred Jin Xuan — which has held a one-star rating for nine consecutive years as of the 2026 Michelin Guide — occupies the 53rd floor and specializes in refined Cantonese cuisine. Scena Di Angelo, helmed by Michelin-starred chef Angelo Aglieno, offers Mediterranean fare. The Water's Edge Spa on the 55th floor features ten treatment rooms and a heated indoor pool.
View Subjects: The Bund, Oriental Pearl Tower, Huangpu River
Address: 8 Shi Ji Da Dao, Lujiazui, Pu Dong Xin Qu, China, 200120
Nearest Airports: SHA · Hongqiao · 25 km; PVG · Pudong · 39 km

Alt Text: Mandarin Oriental Pudong Shanghai suite living room with wood and leather interior and window view of Oriental Pearl Tower and Bund skyline at dusk

Images.alt: Mandarin Oriental Pudong Shanghai suite living room with wood and leather interior and window view of Oriental Pearl Tower and Bund skyline at dusk

Hotel Tagline: From the east bank, the Oriental Pearl Tower and Huangpu River compose a portrait that shifts all day.
Summary: The Premier River View Suite puts the Pearl Tower and the river in a portrait that changes throughout the day. Over 4,000 artworks distributed through the building make the lobby arrival its own event. We’d book the Mandarin River Themed Room for the same view at a quieter scale.
Description ⚙: The Mandarin Oriental Pudong, Shanghai opened on the east bank of the Huangpu River in Lujiazui and quickly became a benchmark for modern luxury. The hotel resembles a museum showcasing over 4,000 artworks, half by Chinese artists, including Miao Tong's 20-meter-high The Sound of Heaven mosaic in the lobby. The 362 rooms and suites, designed by Bernardo Fort-Brescia, span over 50 square meters and feature panoramic views, premium furnishings, and Frette linens. The Premier River View Suite creates a portrait combining the Oriental Pearl Tower and the sinuous river. Dining options include Fifty 8 Degree Grill (modern French), Yong Yi Ting (Michelin-starred Cantonese, 2024 Michelin Guide Shanghai), the Mandarin Cake Shop, and Qi Bar. The hotel features a 2,415-square-meter spa and a 25-meter indoor pool. Wellness facilities include sunset yoga sessions and Fusion Fitness lessons.
View Subjects: The Bund, Oriental Pearl Tower, Huangpu River
Address: 111 Pu Dong Nan Lu, Lujiazui, Pu Dong Xin Qu, Shang Hai Shi, China, 200120
Nearest Airports: SHA · Hongqiao · 25 km; PVG · Pudong · 39 km

Alt Text: Park Hyatt Shanghai restaurant at night with angled floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking the illuminated Bund waterfront and Huangpu River far below

Images.alt: Park Hyatt Shanghai restaurant at night with angled floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking the illuminated Bund waterfront and Huangpu River far below

Hotel Tagline: From the 85th floor, the Lujiazui skyline stretches below the infinity pool's edge.
Summary: Between the 79th and 93rd floors of the World Financial Center, every room looks out over the full Lujiazui skyline. The infinity pool on the 85th floor delivers the sensation that earns a separate mention. The Bund View Suite is the one to reserve for the room-side version — no rooftop required.
Description ⚙: The Park Hyatt Shanghai, set atop the Shanghai World Financial Center, occupies the 79th through 93rd floors and delivers unmatched panoramas of the Huangpu River, the Bund, the twisted Shanghai Tower, and the Oriental Pearl Tower from every room. The 173 rooms and suites, designed by Tony Chi and Associates, combine minimalist luxury with floor-to-ceiling windows and marble bathrooms. The 100 Century Avenue restaurant on the 91st floor serves prime beef, market-fresh seafood, and premium poultry against floor-to-ceiling windows framing the full skyline. The Dining Room on the 87th floor specializes in Chinese cuisine. The 86th-floor Park Salon offers complimentary champagne and panoramic views in select rates. The Bund View Suite is the most requested accommodation. The Water's Edge Spa and 24-hour fitness center serve the hotel's mid-floors. The infinity pool on the 85th floor offers the sensation of swimming above the Lujiazui skyline.
View Subjects: The Bund, Oriental Pearl Tower, Huangpu River
Address: 100 Shi Ji Da Dao, Lujiazui, Pu Dong Xin Qu, Shang Hai Shi, China, 200120
Nearest Airports: SHA · Hongqiao · 25 km; PVG · Pudong · 39 km

Hotel: Four Seasons Resort Sharm El Sheikh
Alt Text: Four Seasons Resort Sharm El Sheikh private balcony breakfast table overlooking the Red Sea with Tiran Island in the distance

Images.alt: Four Seasons Resort Sharm El Sheikh private balcony breakfast table overlooking the Red Sea with Tiran Island in the distance

Hotel Tagline: Ten terraces face the Gulf of Aqaba, with Tiran Island fixed on the horizon.
Summary: The funicular ride from resort level to the beach takes under a minute — and the Gulf of Aqaba expands the whole way down. Tiran Island sits on the horizon from every terrace, every balcony, every one of the ten al fresco dining spaces that face the same water.
Description ⚙: Four Seasons Resort Sharm El Sheikh is the only property in the region with a private funicular connecting the clifftop grounds to the beach. The resort's 197 rooms and suites all include a private balcony or terrace; upper floors command an unobstructed panorama across the gulf to Tiran Island. Ten al fresco dining venues face the water, including Zitouni (Lebanese) and Yatai (Asian). A PADI dive centre operates from the beach with access to one of the region's best-rated house reefs. The spa offers 12 treatment rooms including two outdoor cabanas facing the Red Sea.
Address: 1 Four Seasons Boulevard, El-Salam, ثان شرم الشيخ، محافظة جنوب سيناء 8765201, Egypt
Nearest Airports: SSH · Sharm El-Sheikh · 11 km

Alt Text: Mövenpick Resort Sharm El Sheikh open-air restaurant terrace at dusk with Naama Bay and Sinai mountains in the background

Images.alt: Mövenpick Resort Sharm El Sheikh open-air restaurant terrace at dusk with Naama Bay and Sinai mountains in the background

Hotel Tagline: Arabesque terraces step down to Naama Bay, the arc of water visible from every balcony.
Summary: At Naama Bay's edge, the pool appears level with the water — an illusion the Arabesque terracing makes convincing. From the Liwa Restaurant table or the balcony of any of the 289 rooms, the bay arcs below in both directions, and after dark the lights of the promenade settle into the reflection.
Description ⚙: Mövenpick Resort Sharm El Sheikh is arranged in Arabesque terraces down the hillside above Naama Bay, with 289 villas and rooms angled so the water stays in the sightline of every private balcony. The Liwa Restaurant terrace and the infinity pool face the bay at the same elevation. Three further restaurants serve Egyptian, Italian, and Indian cuisine. Facilities include two heated pools, a spa with Turkish bath and mineral bath, tennis courts, and a fitness centre.
Address: Naama Bay 46619، Sharm El Sheikh 1, South Sinai Governorate 46619, Egypt
Nearest Airports: SSH · Sharm El-Sheikh · 12 km

Alt Text: Grand Rotana Resort Sharm El Sheikh pool deck with striped sunloungers facing the Red Sea and Tiran Island on the horizon

Images.alt: Grand Rotana Resort Sharm El Sheikh pool deck with striped sunloungers facing the Red Sea and Tiran Island on the horizon

Hotel Tagline: Tiran Island sits on the horizon across a flat arc of deep-blue water, framed by Mezzaluna's windows.
Summary: The cove at Shark's Bay absorbs most of the sound — the resort sits inside it, not beside it, and the transition from pool to beach to reef happens without leaving the grounds. Upstairs, Mezzaluna's window tables look straight out at Tiran Island across a flat arc of deep-blue water.
Description ⚙: Grand Rotana Resort sits inside Shark's Bay with the cove's natural shelter keeping the water quiet on three sides. The 325 rooms and suites all have a private balcony; upper floors look directly across the bay to the open Red Sea, where Tiran Island holds the horizon on clear days. The Mezzaluna Italian restaurant faces the water; Silk Road serves Eastern and Western dishes. A lagoon-style outdoor pool flanks the private coral beach, and a private jetty gives direct snorkelling access to the house reef.
Address: Shark's Bay 296, أول شرم الشيخ، محافظة جنوب سيناء 8764020, Egypt
Nearest Airports: SSH · Sharm El-Sheikh · 10 km

Alt Text: SUNRISE Diamond Beach Resort Sharm El Sheikh terrace jacuzzi with Moorish arch framing a direct view of the Red Sea

Images.alt: SUNRISE Diamond Beach Resort Sharm El Sheikh terrace jacuzzi with Moorish arch framing a direct view of the Red Sea

Hotel Tagline: From the upper floors, the Red Sea, the southern coastline, and the Sinai ridgeline stack into a single frame.
Summary: From the upper floors, three things appear in the same frame: the Red Sea below, the coastline bending south toward Ras Mohammed, and the full ridgeline of the Sinai mountains behind. At beach level, the reef begins within swimming distance of the jetty.
Description ⚙: SUNRISE Diamond Beach Resort rises on the Om el Sid headland so that the upper floors take in three layers simultaneously: the Red Sea directly below, the coastline bending south toward Ras Mohammed National Park, and the full ridgeline of the Sinai mountains behind. The resort operates all-inclusive with five à la carte restaurants, four outdoor pools, and 12 bars. An international diving centre provides reef access directly from the beach; an aqua park, spa, and fitness centre are also on the grounds.
Address: 30 Taher Street, Om el Sid Hill, أول شرم الشيخ، محافظة جنوب سيناء 8762632, Egypt
Nearest Airports: SSH · Sharm El-Sheikh · 23 km

Hotel: Park Regency Sharm El Sheikh Resort
Alt Text: Park Regency Sharm El Sheikh Resort king room with glass doors opening to a sea-view balcony

Images.alt: Park Regency Sharm El Sheikh Resort king room with glass doors opening to a sea-view balcony

Hotel Tagline: Every balcony in all 421 rooms faces the Red Sea, south toward Ras Mohammed.
Summary: At Gardens Bay, the sea-view balcony is not an upgrade — it is every room's starting point, across all 421. The three private coves anchor the resort south of Naama Bay; outdoor restaurant tables sit directly above the water, which from here opens toward Ras Mohammed.
Description ⚙: Park Regency Sharm El Sheikh Resort — formerly Hyatt Regency Sharm El Sheikh prior to December 2020 — occupies three private beach coves at Gardens Bay. Every room in the 421-key inventory comes with a furnished sea-view balcony as standard: no garden-view or pool-view tier exists. Outdoor dining terraces at all five restaurants sit above the Red Sea, which from here faces south toward Ras Mohammed National Park. Five outdoor pools, three private beaches, and a Club Olympus fitness centre with squash and tennis courts are supplemented by a spa.
Address: 58, Gardens Bay, أول شرم الشيخ، محافظة جنوب سيناء 8763310, Egypt
Nearest Airports: SSH · Sharm El-Sheikh · 12 km

Alt Text: Meraki Resort Sharm El Sheikh white suite with sculptural gold coral headboard and floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking the Red Sea

Images.alt: Meraki Resort Sharm El Sheikh white suite with sculptural gold coral headboard and floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking the Red Sea

Hotel Tagline: Floor-to-ceiling glass, and beyond it, only the Red Sea.
Summary: White walls, a sculptural gold coral headboard, and then — floor to ceiling — the Red Sea. The design leaves nothing between the room and the water except glass. Adults only, all-inclusive, and close to SOHO Square; the view is not incidental here, it is the argument.
Description ⚙: Meraki Resort is an adults-only boutique property on the Red Sea beachfront at El-Salam, 1.2 km from Sharm International Airport. The design logic is visible the moment you enter the room: all-white interiors, sculptural gold-and-white coral-pattern headboards, pendant lighting, and then floor-to-ceiling windows with nothing between you and the sea. The outdoor pool sits at beach level with direct access to the private shore and reef. The restaurant serves Greek, Italian, seafood, and international cuisines. The property operates exclusively all-inclusive for adults (18+).
Address: 96 A, Ra's Nasrani, Sharks Bay, أول شرم الشيخ، محافظة جنوب سيناء 8766103, Egypt
Nearest Airports: SSH · Sharm El-Sheikh · 13 km

Alt Text: Rixos Premium Seagate Sharm El Sheikh room window framing palm trees, beach umbrellas, and the Red Sea

Images.alt: Rixos Premium Seagate Sharm El Sheikh room window framing palm trees, beach umbrellas, and the Red Sea

Hotel Tagline: Salt's terrace opens onto 180 degrees of Nabq Bay, the Red Sea three metres from the sand below.
Summary: Dinner at Mangal is at a table on the sand — open fire, Red Sea three metres away. One floor up, Salt's terrace offers the full 180-degree arc of Nabq Bay. Eight pools, eleven bars, and nine restaurants give the Rixos more scale than most resorts in Sharm; the view never narrows.
Description ⚙: Rixos Premium Seagate occupies the Nabq Bay shoreline 9 km north of central Naama Bay, within the Nabq Protected Area. The scale is the defining characteristic: nine à la carte restaurants, 11 bars, and eight pools, anchored by the Mangal BBQ restaurant at sand level and Salt Seafood above it with a 180-degree sea view. The Premium Ultra All Inclusive concept covers food, drink, and most activities on the property. An indoor heated pool operates in winter; an aqua park, Turkish bath, spa, gym, and tennis courts complete the facilities.
Address: Nabq Bay EGYPT, Sharm El Sheikh 2, South Sinai Governorate 46628, Egypt
Nearest Airports: SSH · Sharm El-Sheikh · 15 km

Alt Text: Park Hyatt Shenzhen suite with Chinese-influenced wall art and floor-to-ceiling corner windows overlooking Shenzhen Bay toward the hills of Hong Kong

Images.alt: Park Hyatt Shenzhen suite with Chinese-influenced wall art and floor-to-ceiling corner windows overlooking Shenzhen Bay toward the hills of Hong Kong

Hotel Tagline: From the Ping An Finance Centre's upper floors, Shenzhen spreads 270 degrees in every direction.
Summary: The Sky View Deluxe rooms frame 270 degrees of Shenzhen from the Ping An Finance Centre’s upper floors — and guests with time to spare can ascend to the 116th-floor Free Sky observation deck. The Attic bar, a floor above The Glasshouse restaurant, is the right place to end the evening.
Description ⚙:
The Park Hyatt Shenzhen commands attention from its position in the striking Ping An Finance Centre, one of the world's tallest buildings (after its completion in 2017). The luxurious hotel occupies floors 36 to 44, offering guests an extraordinary vantage point over Shenzhen's dynamic cityscape. Enjoying the most astonishing vistas in Shenzhen is as effortless as it gets for guests staying at this property: taking the lift to the Free Sky observation deck on the 116th floor is enough to do the trick.

From this elevated sanctuary, guests can marvel at sweeping views of Shenzhen's ever-evolving skyline through vast floor-to-ceiling windows that define public spaces and accommodations. The view takes on different personalities throughout the day — from crisp morning vistas over the city awakening to the dramatic display of urban lights at nightfall.

The hotel's design aesthetic merges contemporary luxury with subtle cultural touches, creating sophisticated spaces that feel both global and distinctly Chinese. The 171 rooms and 24 suites are private retreats in the sky, each featuring architectural windows that frame the metropolitan panorama below. The 300-square-meter Presidential Suite is the crown jewel, featuring double-height windows and a separate living room offering a spectacular 180-degree cityscape panorama. With almost the same exceptional vistas, the Sky View Deluxe rooms are also a fabulous option.

Unsurprisingly, food, drinks, and vistas always seem to be close. Starting the night with a drink at The Attic bar followed by a meal at The Glasshouse or the Garden Pavilion is the recipe for a long-lasting memory. The Living Room transforms throughout the day from a breakfast venue to an elegant dinner destination, particularly striking at sunset.

On the 8th floor, the fitness center and 25-meter swimming pool continue the hotel's theme of elevated luxury with city views. The spa offers treatments that blend Asian and modern therapeutic techniques, featuring traditional Chinese massage among its signature offerings.

The hotel's location in the Futian District places guests in a prime position near the Convention and Exhibition Center. They have easy access to high-end shopping and the convenient Shenzhen Metro system.
Address: 5023 Yi Tian Lu, 中心城 Fu Tian Qu, Shen Zhen Shi, China, 518046
Nearest Airports: SZX · Bao'an · 36 km

Alt Text: Shangri-La Nanshan Shenzhen hotel bar at dusk, curved green counter with brass globe pendants and floor-to-ceiling windows over the city at sunset

Images.alt: Shangri-La Nanshan Shenzhen hotel bar at dusk, curved green counter with brass globe pendants and floor-to-ceiling windows over the city at sunset

Hotel Tagline: From the 66th floor and above, Shenzhen Bay opens south toward the Hong Kong ridgeline.
Summary: South-facing rooms above the 66th floor open onto Shenzhen Bay and the Hong Kong ridgeline — a sightline unavailable from any other hotel in the city. The 77th-floor Wellness Club, at over 300 meters, adds a gym with a 180-degree bay panorama worth factoring into the booking decision.
Description ⚙:
Shangri-La Nanshan, Shenzhen opened in 2024 within the Shenzhen Bay Super Headquarters Base, Nanshan's most ambitious new coastal district. The hotel occupies floors 66 to 78 of a supertall at Shenwan Huiyun Center — a building whose geometry was conceived to evoke homing birds spreading their wings toward the water. The metaphor is apt: from the south-facing rooms, Shenzhen Bay leads the eye directly to the hills of Hong Kong's New Territories, visible on clear days as a pale ridgeline across the strait. It is a view angle that no other hotel in the city shares.

The 272 rooms and suites, all beginning above the 66th floor, are oriented around four distinct sightlines. South-facing rooms hold the most coveted position, with floor-to-ceiling windows framing Shenzhen Bay and Hong Kong in a single unobstructed arc. North-facing rooms look toward the OCT Mangrove Ecological Park, one of Shenzhen's protected wetland reserves. East-facing rooms take in the Overseas Chinese Town waterfront; west-facing rooms survey the Shahe Golf Course greens far below. Room categories with wraparound corner windows compress all of this into a single panorama.

On the 77th floor, the Wellness Club houses a gym that guests have described as one of the best-equipped in Shenzhen — elevated to over 300 meters, with a 180-degree bay panorama as the backdrop to every session. The full-service spa and indoor swimming pool share the same floor, both offering unobstructed views of the city spreading toward the coast.

The hotel's four dining venues span Cantonese and international cuisines, with an all-day dining restaurant and a dedicated Chinese restaurant serving as the two anchor options.

Connectivity is one of the Nanshan's quiet strengths. The hotel is directly connected to the RAIL IN shopping mall and sits within walking distance of Hongshuwan Station (Metro Line 2) and Hongshuwan South Station (Lines 9 and 11) — three lines that provide direct access to Futian, Luohu, and Nanshan's technology campuses. Shenzhen Bay Port, the ferry crossing to Hong Kong, is a 15-minute drive. Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport is 35 minutes away.
Address: Block J, Phase 5, Shenwan, Huiyun Center, Nan Shan Qu, Shen Zhen Shi, Guang Dong Sheng, China, 518000
Nearest Airports: SZX · Bao'an · 31 km

Alt Text: Mandarin Oriental Shenzhen corner guestroom with wraparound floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking Lianhuashan Park forest and the Shenzhen skyline

Images.alt: Mandarin Oriental Shenzhen corner guestroom with wraparound floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking Lianhuashan Park forest and the Shenzhen skyline

Hotel Tagline: From the 67th floor up, Lotus Hill sits to the north while Shenzhen Bay opens south toward Hong Kong.
Summary: A Michelin Key hotel since 2025, with a bar ranked among Asia’s 50 best — the case for staying still comes down to the rooms. The SHANG XIA-furnished Oriental Suite wraps its living areas in floor-to-ceiling glass: Lotus Hill to the north, Shenzhen Bay and Hong Kong to the south.
Description ⚙:
The Mandarin Oriental, Shenzhen, one of the best hotels in this fast-paced Chinese city, soars above the vibrant Futian district, occupying the upper floors (67th to 79th) of an architectural masterpiece in the heart of China's technology hub. The hotel's elevated position delivers breathtaking panoramic views — to the north, guests can trace the undulating outline of Lotus Hill (Lianhuashan Park), while the south-facing rooms offer a stunning vista of Hong Kong's New Territories across Shenzhen Bay.

Each of the 178 rooms and suites features floor-to-ceiling windows that frame spectacular city and mountain views. The interior design artfully blends contemporary luxury with subtle Chinese motifs. Starting at 56 square meters, even the entry-level rooms rank among the most spacious in Shenzhen. The hotel's crown jewel is its collection of specialty suites, particularly the SHANG XIA-furnished Oriental Suite, which offers separate living and dining areas surrounded by wraparound windows, creating an infinity-edge sensation high above the city.

For dining, the property hosts several distinctive restaurants, including The Bay by Chef Fei for refined Cantonese cuisine, the French brasserie OPUS 388, and RIN for Japanese Teppanyaki, all offering those same spectacular views that have become synonymous with the property. In 2025, the Mandarin Oriental, Shenzhen became one of the first hotels in mainland China to receive a Michelin Key. The MO Bar, on the 79th floor, was simultaneously ranked No. 21 on Asia's 50 Best Bars List — a dual distinction that cements the property as a destination well beyond accommodation.

The wellness facilities are on the 68th floor. They feature a 20-meter indoor swimming pool with floor-to-ceiling windows and a fitness center where guests can work out while gazing across the cityscape. The spa combines traditional Chinese healing philosophies with contemporary techniques.

On the 78th floor, The Mandarin Club is a destination on its own. Apart from displaying vistas that stretch as far as Hong Kong, it takes guests to cloud nine with exclusive services like private check-in and check-out, state-of-the-art meeting rooms, and culinary offerings that will make it hard to leave.

Location-wise, the five-star hotel is ideally positioned within the Futian business district, connected to premium shopping at UpperHills Mall, and within walking distance of the Futian high-speed railway station, offering easy access to Hong Kong.
Address: Block A UpperHills No, 5001 Huang Gang Lu, Fu Tian Qu, Shen Zhen Shi, Guang Dong Sheng, China, 518026
Nearest Airports: SZX · Bao'an · 39 km

Alt Text: Four Seasons Hotel Shenzhen guestroom at night, floor-to-ceiling window framing the illuminated Futian skyline including the Ping An Finance Centre

Images.alt: Four Seasons Hotel Shenzhen guestroom at night, floor-to-ceiling window framing the illuminated Futian skyline including the Ping An Finance Centre

Hotel Tagline: Corner suites hold Lianhuashan Park across their full width, the city converging behind it.
Summary: Premier rooms on the south side face Hong Kong across the border — a quiet geographical fact most guests don’t expect to find in Futian. Floor-to-ceiling windows throughout; corner suites give Lianhuashan Park its full width, with the city converging behind it.
Description ⚙:
Rising gracefully above Shenzhen's futuristic cityscape in the heart of Futian District, the Four Seasons Hotel Shenzhen embodies the spirit of this innovative metropolis. Inside, floor-to-ceiling windows throughout the luxury property capture sweeping views of a contrasting skyline with the lush greenery of Lianhuashan Park.

The elegant and comfortable rooms and suites serve as refined urban retreats, offering expansive views. The design emphasizes clean lines and natural light, with premium touches like marble bathrooms and advanced technology seamlessly integrated throughout. Premier rooms face south towards Hong Kong, while others offer views of the city center or Lianhuashan Park.

This particular hotel is also a family-friendly address: apart from pools that ensure fun moments, the little ones become over the moon with suites with tents and colored linens, as well as hidden surprises for kids throughout the stay.

Located in Futian's central business district, the Four Seasons Hotel Shenzhen provides convenient access to the Convention and Exhibition Center, high-end shopping destinations, and public transportation links to Hong Kong.
Address: 138 Fu Hua San Lu, 中心城 Fu Tian Qu, Shen Zhen Shi, Guang Dong Sheng, China, 518017
Nearest Airports: SZX · Bao'an · 37 km

Alt Text: The Ritz-Carlton Shenzhen suite living room with wide windows showing the Shenzhen daytime skyline and a mountain ridge in the distance

Images.alt: The Ritz-Carlton Shenzhen suite living room with wide windows showing the Shenzhen daytime skyline and a mountain ridge in the distance

Hotel Tagline: Lianhua Mountain and the city skyline share the view from the panoramic bathtub.
Summary: The panoramic bathtub is a standard fixture across most room categories here — positioned to face the skyline and the green line of Lianhua Mountain at once. Worth staying on Club Level for five daily food presentations; the Ritz-Carlton Suite adds a two-floor private entrance.
Description ⚙:
The Ritz-Carlton, Shenzhen, is a luxury hotel located in the heart of Futian District, Shenzhen's central business hub, directly opposite the Shenzhen Convention and Exhibition Center. It offers panoramic views of the city skyline and Lianhua Mountain from a 28-floor building that has anchored this stretch of Futian since 2007.

One thing the 239 rooms and 41 suites share is space (this is a Ritz-Carlton property, after all), so one doesn't even need to book the extravagant Ritz-Carlton Suite to feel at ease. Elegance and luxury are also standard for all accommodations, such as fluffy blankets and, in most cases, a panoramic bathtub facing the Shenzhen skyline and the soothing sight of a park.

The hotel's wellness facilities include a landscaped outdoor pool, fitness center, yoga studio, and a full-service spa on the 6th floor. Add to the mix a convenient location, with easy access to the Shenzhen Convention and Exhibition Center, high-end shopping, and public transportation, and you'll soon realize why this is one of the best hotels in Shenzhen.
Address: 116 Fu Hua San Lu, 中心城 Fu Tian Qu, Shen Zhen Shi, Guang Dong Sheng, China, 518048
Nearest Airports: SZX · Bao'an · 37 km

Alt Text: The St Regis Shenzhen upper-floor lounge with mirrored sculptural installation and panoramic windows with an aerial view of the Shenzhen cityscape

Images.alt: The St Regis Shenzhen upper-floor lounge with mirrored sculptural installation and panoramic windows with an aerial view of the Shenzhen cityscape

Hotel Tagline: From the top floors of Kingkey 100, Shenzhen's skyline stretches south toward Hong Kong.
Summary: The top 25 floors of the Kingkey 100 building give the St. Regis a sightline that extends south toward Hong Kong. Ask for a Skyline room to get Bang & Olufsen audio paired with the city from above the clouds; the Iridium Spa on the 75th floor extends the altitude further.
Description ⚙:
Rising majestically above Shenzhen's effervescent Luohu District, The St. Regis Shenzhen, the brainchild of architect Terry Farrell, occupies the top 25 floors of the iconic Kingkey 100 building, one of the tallest skyscrapers in the city. This extraordinary location allows for sweeping panoramic views across Shenzhen, from the urban skyline to the scenic backdrop of the nearby mountains and even extending south toward Hong Kong, the bustling metropolis only 30 kilometers away from the city.

The fabulous 290 rooms and suites, impeccably designed by CCD/Cheng Chung Design studio, provide luxurious comfort. Expansive windows frame these stunning vistas for guests' joy, especially when personal playlists echo through the Bang & Olufsen sound systems in place. The interiors blend contemporary elegance with refined touches, including spacious marble bathrooms, advanced in-room technology, and custom furnishings.

For dining, guests can have a break from Chinese cuisine and sample Italian delicacies at the on-site Elba Restaurant; however, this time, Shenzhen's skyline has replaced the Tyrrhenian Sea vistas.

Guests looking for wellness and relaxation will find the hotel's Iridium Spa on the 75th floor, which features tailored treatments, a serene ambiance, and inspiring views. The 28-meter indoor swimming pool, particularly at night, provides the illusion of merging with the city's supertall skyscrapers.
Address: 5016 Shen Nan Dong Lu, Luo Hu Qu, Shen Zhen Shi, Guang Dong Sheng, China, 518001
Nearest Airports: SZX · Bao'an · 41 km

Hotel: San Domenico Palace, A Four Seasons Hotel
Alt Text: San Domenico Palace Taormina clifftop restaurant terrace overlooking the Ionian Sea at sunset

Images.alt: San Domenico Palace Taormina clifftop restaurant terrace overlooking the Ionian Sea at sunset

Hotel Tagline: From the clifftop pool, Mount Etna and the Bay of Taormina share the same view.
Summary: A reimagined 14th-century Dominican convent on a promontory above the Ionian Sea. The 21-metre clifftop infinity pool and the private plunge pool suites — the setting the world recognized from The White Lotus Season 2 — frame Mount Etna and the Bay of Taormina in the same view.
Description ⚙: Perched on a rocky promontory 200 metres above the Ionian Sea, San Domenico Palace occupies a reimagined 14th-century Dominican convent on the edge of Taormina. The 21-metre clifftop infinity pool merges visually with the water below, while convent gardens frame simultaneous views of Mount Etna and the Bay of Taormina. Three restaurants including a Michelin-starred dining room and the Botanica Spa complete a property recognized globally as the primary filming location for season two of The White Lotus.
Address: Via S. Domenico, 5, 98039 Taormina ME, Italy
Nearest Airports: CTA · Catania Fontanarossa · 70 km

Hotel: Grand Hotel Timeo, A Belmond Hotel, Taormina
Alt Text: Grand Hotel Timeo Taormina stone terrace with Mount Etna and Bay of Giardini Naxos view

Images.alt: Grand Hotel Timeo Taormina stone terrace with Mount Etna and Bay of Giardini Naxos view

Hotel Tagline: Opposite the Greek Theatre, with Mount Etna filling the horizon beyond ancient stone columns.
Summary: Opposite the Greek Theatre of Taormina, with Mount Etna filling the horizon from the Literary Terrace & Bar. The Michelin-starred restaurant frames ancient stone columns in the foreground, the full width of the volcano behind — the view that defined Taormina's hotel tradition.
Description ⚙: Positioned directly opposite the Greek Theatre of Taormina, Grand Hotel Timeo commands one of the most photographed terrace views in Sicily. The Literary Terrace & Bar places Mount Etna at the centre of a panorama that extends across the Bay of Giardini Naxos; the Michelin-starred Restaurant Timeo frames the ancient theatre ruins in the foreground with the full width of the volcano behind.
View Subjects: Mount Etna, Greek Theatre of Taormina, Taormina Bay
Address: Via Teatro Greco, 59, 98039 Taormina ME, Italy
Nearest Airports: CTA · Catania Fontanarossa · 71 km

Alt Text: Mazzarò Sea Palace Taormina pool terrace with two rocky headlands framing Mazzarò Bay

Images.alt: Mazzarò Sea Palace Taormina pool terrace with two rocky headlands framing Mazzarò Bay

Hotel Tagline: Balconies hang directly above the water, framed by two wooded limestone headlands.
Summary: At sea level on Mazzarò Bay, all rooms opening onto balconies directly above the water. Two pools and a private beach sit between two wooded limestone headlands, and the funicular to Taormina's historic centre stops 50 metres from the hotel entrance.
Description ⚙: Mazzarò Sea Palace sits at sea level on the bay from which it takes its name, between two wooded limestone headlands that frame the hotel's private beach and two pools. All rooms open onto furnished balconies directly above the water, and the cable car to Taormina's historic centre operates from 50 metres from the entrance.
Address: Via Nazionale, 147, 98030 Taormina ME, Italy
Nearest Airports: CTA · Catania Fontanarossa · 66 km

Hotel: Villa Sant'Andrea, A Belmond Hotel, Taormina Mare
Alt Text: Villa Sant'Andrea Taormina infinity pool with rocks and boats in Mazzarò Bay

Images.alt: Villa Sant'Andrea Taormina infinity pool with rocks and boats in Mazzarò Bay

Hotel Tagline: From the panoramic pool, Etna sits on the horizon beyond the Taormina cliff.
Summary: A 1919 Cornish villa on Mazzarò Bay, with subtropical gardens descending to a private beach. The Brizza dining experience places tables at the water's edge; the panoramic pool looks directly across the bay toward the Taormina cliff, with Etna on the horizon.
Description ⚙: Built in 1919 as a private villa by the Cornish Trewhella family, Villa Sant'Andrea sits in subtropical gardens on Mazzarò Bay, directly facing the Taormina cliff. The 66 rooms offer sea-facing balconies; the panoramic pool looks across the bay with Etna on the horizon. The Brizza dining experience positions tables at the water's edge.
Address: Via Nazionale, 137, 98039 Taormina ME, Italy
Nearest Airports: CTA · Catania Fontanarossa · 67 km

Alt Text: Atlantis Bay Taormina deck terrace over turquoise Baia delle Sirene with Taormina cliffs

Images.alt: Atlantis Bay Taormina deck terrace over turquoise Baia delle Sirene with Taormina cliffs

Hotel Tagline: Balconies hang directly over the turquoise water of the cove, framed by two rocky promontories.
Summary: Built into the cliff above Baia delle Sirene, room balconies and a floating sea platform sit directly over clear turquoise water. The panoramic restaurant terrace frames an unobstructed view of the Ionian coast between the two rocky promontories that define the hotel's private cove.
Description ⚙: Built into the cliff above Baia delle Sirene, Atlantis Bay occupies one of the most dramatic seafront positions in the Taormina area. Room balconies look directly over the clear turquoise water of the cove, and a private floating sea platform extends the guest experience to the surface of the bay.
Address: Via Nazionale, 161, 98030 Taormina ME, Italy
Nearest Airports: CTA · Catania Fontanarossa · 66 km

Alt Text: Grand Hotel San Pietro Taormina room balcony with panoramic Ionian Sea view

Summary: A 17-room boutique hotel above Taormina's historic centre, on a terrace that simultaneously frames Mount Etna, the Strait of Messina, and the Bay of Giardini Naxos. Breakfast faces all three views at once; binoculars are provided during active Etna eruptions.
Description ⚙: A 17-room boutique hotel above Taormina's historic centre on the road to Castelmola, Hotel Villa Ducale occupies a panoramic position that simultaneously frames Mount Etna, the Strait of Messina, and the Bay of Giardini Naxos. Images.alt: Fairmont Singapore Singapore upper-floor bar interior with floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking Marina Bay Sands ArtScience Museum and harbor

Hotel Tagline: At night, the lit skyline of Marina Bay fills the frame from every private balcony.
Summary: Every one of the 778 rooms has a private balcony — the Deluxe Marina Bay View category faces the water directly, and at night the lit skyline fills the frame from there. The Fairmont Gold Marina Bay rooms add lounge access with evening cocktails at the same angle. Worth choosing on floor alone.
Description ⚙: Situated above City Hall and Esplanade MRT stations, the Fairmont Singapore is one of the city's largest luxury hotels, with 778 rooms and suites each featuring a private balcony. The Deluxe Marina Bay View and Fairmont Gold Marina Bay View rooms face the water directly, with the lit skyline filling the frame after dark. Drawing from Singapore's Peranakan heritage, the hotel encompasses twelve restaurants and bars, two outdoor pools, and the Willow Stream Spa. The Fairmont Gold programme provides lounge access with evening cocktails and bay views from the upper floors.
Address: 80 Bras Basah Rd, Singapore 189560

Images.alt: Mandarin Oriental Singapore Singapore bar lounge at sunset with floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking Singapore CBD skyline and Esplanade dome

Hotel Tagline: South-facing windows frame the Formula 1 circuit, the Esplanade, and Marina Bay in one uninterrupted sweep.
Summary: South-facing rooms here frame the Formula 1 circuit, the Esplanade, and Marina Bay in a single uninterrupted sweep — the same window that turns remarkable at night during fireworks season. BAY@5’s alfresco terrace delivers the same panorama at ground level, over dinner or a late drink.
Description ⚙: Designed by John Portman and opened in 1987, the Mandarin Oriental, Singapore occupies a prime position on Raffles Avenue with views of Marina Bay, Raffles Place, and the city skyline. The 510 rooms and suites were refreshed by designer Jeffrey Wilkes in late 2023. South-facing rooms frame the Formula 1 circuit and Marina Bay's nightscape; club-access rooms include entry to HAUS 65, the reimagined lounge on the 21st floor. BAY@5's alfresco terrace delivers panoramic views of Marina Bay Sands and the Esplanade. The hotel's 25-metre rooftop pool overlooks the bay.

Nearest Airports: XSP · Seletar · 17 km; SIN · Changi · 19 km; JHB · Senai · 58 km

Images.alt: Marina Bay Sands Singapore SkyPark infinity pool at dusk with swimmer and Singapore CBD skyscrapers

Hotel Tagline: From the 57th-floor infinity pool, the city wraps three sides with water underfoot.
Summary: The 57th-floor infinity pool is the view as much as it frames one — city on three sides, water underfoot, MBS’s own towers reflected back at you. The renovation of all three hotel towers was completed in 2025. We’d request a high floor on the city-facing side and stay through the Spectra light show.
Description ⚙: Marina Bay Sands, opened in 2010 and designed by Moshe Safdie, is Singapore's most iconic integrated resort. The 2,561 rooms across three towers underwent a $1.75 billion renovation completed in 2025, introducing marble, African stone, and luxury suite finishes throughout. The 57th-floor SkyPark infinity pool offers panoramic city views on three sides. Dining is anchored by celebrity chef venues including Gordon Ramsay, Wolfgang Puck, and Daniel Boulud. The resort also houses a casino, a three-level luxury shopping mall, and the nightly Spectra light show on Marina Bay.
Address: 10 Bayfront Ave, Singapore 018956
Nearest Airports: XSP · Seletar · 19 km; SIN · Changi · 20 km; JHB · Senai · 64 km

Alt Text: PARKROYAL COLLECTION Marina Bay Singapore outdoor pool and lawn with Esplanade Theatres dome and Singapore CBD skyline

Images.alt: PARKROYAL COLLECTION Marina Bay Singapore outdoor pool and lawn with Esplanade Theatres dome and Singapore CBD skyline

Hotel Tagline: At night, 1,380 fibre-optic lights turn the pool into a starfield beneath the Marina Bay skyline.
Summary: The pool at night is the reason to stay: 1,380 fibre-optic lights turn the water into a starfield, with the Marina Bay skyline as backdrop. Signature Marina Bay Rooms have a private balcony over the bay, and the Collection Club Lounge serves breakfast and cocktails at the same angle, higher up.
Description ⚙: Singapore's original Garden-in-a-Hotel, PARKROYAL COLLECTION Marina Bay was designed by John Portman and features a 21-level internal atrium anchored by a 13-metre Green Wall. All 583 rooms face the Singapore harbour and city skyline through floor-to-ceiling windows; Signature Marina Bay Rooms add private balconies. Following a comprehensive 2020 renovation, the property introduced an Urban Farm supplying farm-to-table dining across its restaurants. The 25-metre outdoor pool is embedded with 1,380 fibre-optic lights that illuminate after dark against the Marina Bay skyline. The COLLECTION Club Lounge on the upper floors serves breakfast and cocktails with panoramic views.
Address: 6 Raffles Blvd, Singapore 039594
Nearest Airports: XSP · Seletar · 18 km; SIN · Changi · 19 km; JHB · Senai · 62 km

Hotel Tagline: Floor-to-ceiling windows hold the Singapore skyline from the uppermost suites on Orchard Road.
Summary: The President, Royal, and Ambassador suites sit at the top of a property that has spent three decades refining its position on Orchard Road. Floor-to-ceiling windows throughout; the 2018 refresh kept the views intact while updating the palette. Two outdoor pools, one adults-only, on the upper floors.
Description ⚙: The Four Seasons Hotel Singapore on Orchard Road has operated for over three decades, maintaining a curated collection of more than 1,500 pieces of Asian art throughout the property. Hirsch Bedner Associates refreshed the rooms and suites in 2018. Floor-to-ceiling windows frame city skyline views throughout; the President, Royal, and Ambassador suites occupy the finest positions in the property. Two outdoor pools — one adults-only and one family-friendly rooftop pool — complement indoor and outdoor tennis courts and a Nobu restaurant.

Hotel Tagline: Built over the water, with Marina Bay Sands rising from the bay below.
Summary: The only hotel built directly over Marina Bay’s water — and the 100 rooms make it intimate enough that the view never feels shared. Premier Bay View rooms have a private jacuzzi with the bay below. The Lantern rooftop bar at dusk, with the MBS towers lit behind the water, is the defining image.
Description ⚙: The Fullerton Bay Hotel Singapore is the only hotel built directly over Marina Bay's waters, occupying a former customs house at 80 Collyer Quay. Its 100 rooms and suites feature floor-to-ceiling windows with views of the bay and Marina Bay Sands. Premier Bay View rooms offer private jacuzzis facing the water. The rooftop Lantern bar is the hotel's signature view space, offering illuminated skyline panoramas after dark. The hotel is positioned adjacent to the historic Clifford Pier, minutes from Raffles Place MRT station.

Summary: The 30th floor crowns a copper-coloured Chinatown tower with an infinity pool facing the port — a different skyline angle from the marina, wider and less familiar. The QĪN restaurant on a lower floor adds a view of heritage shophouses alongside the skyscrapers. The room to ask for faces east.
Description ⚙: The Clan Hotel rises 30 stories above Chinatown in a copper-coloured tower designed by DP Architects, with interiors by KKS International. Launched in 2021, the 324-room property draws its identity from the cultural heritage of its Chinatown location. Floor-to-ceiling windows frame Singapore's financial district skyline and heritage shophouses. The 30th floor offers an infinity pool overlooking the port alongside a fitness centre. The QĪN restaurant provides city views paired with contemporary Asian cuisine. Guests access cultural programming including sessions with local artisans.

Hotel Tagline: Tropical gardens and traveller's palms fill the suites' private terraces, a world apart from the city beyond.
Summary: The 115 suites look onto tropical gardens and traveller’s palms — a deliberately different city view, more intimate than the Marina Bay panoramas elsewhere. The 3rd-floor pool adds a wider city outlook. The Writers Bar and Long Bar are reasons to stay past dinner, regardless of the room category.
Description ⚙: Raffles Singapore, a designated national monument, is a 19th-century colonial landmark classified as one of Singapore's finest addresses. Following a meticulous three-year renovation, the hotel reopened in 2019 with 115 all-suite accommodations. Views overlook lush tropical gardens and traveller's palms, with suites featuring private terraces above the hotel's courtyard. The 3rd-floor outdoor pool adds broader city views. Dining spans the Long Bar, home of the Singapore Sling, the Writers Bar, Yì By Jereme Leung, and the North Indian Tiffin Room.

Summary: The sky gardens span 15,000 square metres of rainwater-irrigated greenery on the building’s terraced exterior — a view that looks inward as much as outward. The 5th-floor infinity pool frames the city skyline, and the Orchid Club rooftop lounge adds a quieter angle over the same panorama.
Description ⚙: Designed by the local WOHA architecture studio, PARKROYAL COLLECTION Pickering, Singapore is an eco-friendly hotel adjacent to Chinatown, featuring 15,000 square metres of rainwater-irrigated sky gardens terraced across the building's exterior. The 5th-floor infinity pool overlooks the cityscape, and the Orchid Club rooftop lounge offers refreshments with panoramic views. The hotel is within walking distance of Clarke Quay, Raffles Place, and Pagoda Street.

Hotel Tagline: Marina Bay Sands stands in direct view from balconies flanked by Doric columns.
Summary: The Loft Suites unveiled in 2022 each have a private balcony flanked by Doric columns with Marina Bay Sands in direct view — the Town Restaurant delivers the same panorama over breakfast from 6:30am. The building itself is a national monument; the view from it carries that sense of permanence.
Description ⚙: A designated public monument, The Fullerton Hotel Singapore occupies a majestic 1920s neo-classical building that once housed the General Post Office and Library. Since opening as a hotel on January 1, 2001, it has established itself as a landmark Marina Bay address. Eight Loft Suites with the signature of Aston Design were unveiled in 2022, each featuring spiral staircases and private balconies flanked by Doric columns with direct views of Marina Bay Sands. The Town Restaurant commands panoramic views over Marina Bay and Raffles Place and hosts one of Singapore's most celebrated Sunday brunches.
Address: 1 Fullerton Sq, Singapore 049178
Nearest Airports: XSP · Seletar · 19 km; SIN · Changi · 21 km; JHB · Senai · 66 km

Hotel Tagline: From the bathtub, Marina Bay fills the octagonal window.
Summary: The octagonal bathroom windows are the most discussed feature here — positioned along the exterior walls so the bathtub faces Marina Bay, Kallang River, or Raffles Place depending on the floor and orientation. High-floor Premier Suites ending in 26 command the widest read of the bay.
Description ⚙: Designed by Pritzker laureate Kevin Roche and opened in 1996, The Ritz-Carlton, Millenia Singapore occupies three hectares of gardens on the Marina Bay waterfront. The 32-story glass-and-steel hotel is distinguished by its octagonal bathroom windows positioned along exterior walls, framing Marina Bay, Raffles Place, Kallang River, or island views from the bathtub. High-floor Premier Suites ending in 26 command the widest bay panoramas. The hotel holds a 4,200-piece collection of art including works by Frank Stella and Dale Chihuly. Dining includes the Colony Sunday brunch and Summer Pavilion for Cantonese cuisine. La Mer spa features treatments accompanied by live music.
Address: 7 Raffles Ave., Marina Bay, Singapore 039799
Nearest Airports: XSP · Seletar · 17 km; SIN · Changi · 19 km; JHB · Senai · 62 km

Alt Text: Swissotel The Stamford Singapore Singapore high-floor bar interior with floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking Marina Bay Sands ArtScience Museum and harbor

Images.alt: Swissotel The Stamford Singapore Singapore high-floor bar interior with floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking Marina Bay Sands ArtScience Museum and harbor

Hotel Tagline: From the 70th floor, Marina Bay Sands stands straight ahead and Indonesia waits on the horizon.
Summary: On clear days the view from the 70th floor stretches to Indonesia — and JAAN by Kirk Westaway (2 Michelin stars, 35 seats) occupies exactly that floor. The SKAI bar next door makes the same altitude accessible without a reservation. Room balconies face Marina Bay Sands from a straight-on angle.
Description ⚙: Swissôtel The Stamford Singapore opened in 1986 as the world's tallest hotel at the time and continues to offer some of Singapore's most expansive panoramic views. The 70th floor houses both the SKAI bar and JAAN by Kirk Westaway, a two-Michelin-star restaurant with 35 seats and a 360-degree skyline backdrop. On clear days, Indonesian and Malaysian islands are visible from the upper floors. Over a thousand rooms feature Marina Bay and Singapore skyline views from private balconies, with the Marina Bay Sands towers visible from reserved rooms. The hotel sits directly above City Hall MRT and connects to Raffles City Shopping Centre.
Address: 2 Stamford Rd, Singapore 178882
Nearest Airports: XSP · Seletar · 17 km; SIN · Changi · 20 km; JHB · Senai · 57 km

Alt Text: Pan Pacific Singapore Singapore renovated hotel room with balcony overlooking Marina Bay and Singapore CBD skyline

Images.alt: Pan Pacific Singapore Singapore renovated hotel room with balcony overlooking Marina Bay and Singapore CBD skyline

Hotel Tagline: Marina Bay opens outward from the Skyline Suites, 35 floors above the Portman atrium.
Summary: The Skyline Suites deliver the clearest read of Marina Bay from this address — the Portman atrium rises 35 floors above you and the vistas open outward from there. The Pacific Club Lounge on the 38th floor is worth booking into; the 38-second elevator ride up is part of the experience.
Description ⚙: The Pan Pacific Singapore, designed by John Portman, features a dramatic 35-floor internal ziggurat atrium rising above Marina Square. A $30 million renovation completed in 2023 refreshed the lobby, driveway, and over 500 rooms while preserving the hotel's iconic architectural elements. The Skyline Suites on upper floors deliver the property's finest Marina Bay panoramas. The Pacific Club Lounge on the 38th floor is accessible via a 38-second express elevator and offers city vistas alongside full club amenities. The Japanese restaurant Keyaki occupies a garden setting on the property grounds.
Address: 7 Raffles Blvd, Singapore 039595
Nearest Airports: XSP · Seletar · 17 km; SIN · Changi · 19 km; JHB · Senai · 63 km

Images.alt: Andaz Singapore Singapore Mr. Stork rooftop bar at sunset with harbor view and cargo ships at anchor

Hotel Tagline: From the 39th floor, Marina Bay, the Singapore Flyer, and I.M. Pei's Gateway towers spread 360 degrees.
Summary: Mr. Stork on the 39th floor is the carousel’s rooftop bar with the widest angle — 360 degrees over Marina Bay, the Singapore Flyer, and I.M. Pei’s Gateway towers directly below. The Andaz King Suite has floor-to-ceiling windows facing the marina from a height that makes the city feel immediate.
Description ⚙: The Andaz Singapore is part of the Duo development in the Bugis district, designed by German architect Ole Scheeren with interiors by André Fu. The honeycomb shell facade hints at interiors inspired by Singapore's spice trade and traditional architecture. The 347 rooms and suites frame Marina Bay skyline and Bugis shophouse views, with the Andaz King Suite delivering the most expansive marina panorama. Mr. Stork, the 39th-floor rooftop bar, provides 360-degree views across Marina Bay, the Singapore Flyer, and I.M. Pei's Gateway towers. The pool is also at the 39th floor.
Address: 5 Fraser St, Singapore 189354
Nearest Airports: XSP · Seletar · 16 km; SIN · Changi · 19 km; JHB · Senai · 57 km

Hotel: JW Marriott Hotel Singapore South Beach
Alt Text: JW Marriott Hotel Singapore South Beach Singapore 18th floor rooftop pool at night with illuminated CBD skyline and Esplanade

Images.alt: JW Marriott Hotel Singapore South Beach Singapore 18th floor rooftop pool at night with illuminated CBD skyline and Esplanade

Hotel Tagline: Marina Bay Sands and the Esplanade share the same window from the 18th floor pool deck.
Summary: Lord Norman Foster’s 2015 complex stacks offices, residences and hotel floors into a single composition — the 18th floor opens onto a panoramic pool deck with the Marina Bay skyline in full view. Marina Bay hotel suites frame MBS and the Esplanade in the same window. Flow18 Sky Garden for events.
Description ⚙: The JW Marriott Hotel Singapore South Beach opened in 2015 as part of a mixed-use development masterminded by Lord Norman Foster. The complex integrates offices, residences, retail, and the 634-room hotel in a prime location between the convention centre and Raffles Singapore. The 18th floor serves as the hotel's recreational crown, with a panoramic pool deck, fitness centre, and Singapore skyline views. Marina Bay hotel suites frame Marina Bay Sands and the Esplanade. The Flow18 Sky Garden and Ebb6 Sky Garden provide alfresco event spaces with city panoramas. Dining extends across The NCO Club, a heritage building serving multiple themed restaurants.
Address: 30 Beach Road, Nicoll Hwy, Access via, Singapore 189763
Nearest Airports: XSP · Seletar · 17 km; SIN · Changi · 19 km; JHB · Senai · 62 km

Alt Text: Valverde Sintra Palácio de Seteais palace bedroom with tall silk-curtained windows, antique carved bed and chandelier, overlooking the lawn with Pena Palace visible on the hilltop

Images.alt: Valverde Sintra Palácio de Seteais palace bedroom with tall silk-curtained windows, antique carved bed and chandelier, overlooking the lawn with Pena Palace visible on the hilltop

Hotel Tagline: From one balcony, the Moorish Castle and Pena Palace share the same sky.
Summary: The 1787 neoclassical palace, rebooted in January 2024 under the Valverde Group and now a member of The Leading Hotels of the World. All 30 rooms face the mountains; ask for the Royal Suite for simultaneous sightlines to both the Moorish Castle and Pena Palace from one balcony.
Description ⚙: The Palácio de Seteais has operated as a hotel since 1954, and since January 2024 under the Valverde Group — a 30-year concession from the Portuguese State — as the Valverde Sintra Palácio de Seteais, a member of The Leading Hotels of the World. All 30 rooms and suites face the Sintra Mountains; the Royal Suite captures both the Moorish Castle and Pena Palace from the same balcony. Restaurante Seteais serves Portuguese and international cuisine in a frescoed ballroom, and the spa is set between the manicured gardens.
Address: R. Barbosa du Bocage 8, 2710-517 Sintra, Portugal

Hotel Tagline: Sintra Mountain terraces rise above a 14th-century monastery deep inside the natural park.
Summary: The only hotel inside the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, acquired in 2026 by an LVMH-backed consortium, with Ritz-Carlton still in charge. Two Michelin-starred restaurants, a 27-hole championship golf course, and the Imperial Suite for the best Sintra Mountain terrace.
Description ⚙: The only hotel inside the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, set within the grounds of a 14th-century monastery. Acquired in March 2026 by an investment consortium backed by LVMH (L Catterton) with Ritz-Carlton management unchanged. Rooms and suites face the golf course or the Sintra Mountains; the Cascais, Sintra, and Imperial suites add oversized terraces. Two Michelin-starred restaurants on site: Midori, Portugal's first Michelin-starred Japanese restaurant, and LAB by Sergi Arola. A 27-hole championship golf course designed by Robert Trent Jones Jr. completes the resort.
Address: Estrada da Lagoa Azul, Linhó, 2714-511 Lisboa, Portugal

Hotel Tagline: From the pool, the Moorish Castle sits framed on the hillside above Sintra.
Summary: Nine rooms, a walled garden, and the clearest view of the Moorish Castle from any pool in Sintra. We’d book the top-floor suite — 70 square meters, castle and village framed together — seven minutes on foot from the historic centre, with complimentary evenings of wine and fado.
Description ⚙: A 19th-century eclectic manor fully restored in 2017, seven minutes on foot from Sintra's historic centre. The heated outdoor pool looks directly at the Moorish Castle on the hillside; the breakfast terrace and the top-floor suite (70 square meters) share the same sightline. Nine individually decorated rooms across two buildings set in 14,000 square meters of walled garden. Complimentary evening wine and cheese social, full breakfast, and free parking included.
Address: Av. Barão Almeida Santos 7, 2710 Sintra, Portugal

Revamped in 2017, the Arribas Terrace restaurant serves fresh Atlantic seafood above the ocean.

Hotel Tagline: Cedar groves, vermilion torii gates, and stone lanterns, with the Tokyo skyline rising behind.
Summary: Adjacent to the centuries-old Hie Shrine in Akasaka, this property combines shrine views — cedar grove, vermilion torii gates, stone lanterns — with the city skyline rising behind. A shrine-facing room, with the Hie Shrine’s torii tunnel approach before dinner at Origami on the lobby floor.
Description ⚙: Adjacent to the centuries-old Hie Shrine in Akasaka, this property combines shrine views — cedar grove, vermilion torii gates, stone lanterns — with the city skyline rising behind.

Hotel Tagline: Green at eye level, where Tokyo's largest urban forest fills the view instead of the skyline.
Summary: Tokyo’s largest urban forest as the view rather than the skyline — this intimate property faces Yoyogi Park directly, offering something the city’s tower hotels can’t: green at eye level. A park-facing room, the rooftop before the city wakes, and the afternoon in the contrast Shibuya provides five minutes away.
Description ⚙: Tokyo's largest urban forest as the view rather than the skyline — this intimate property faces Yoyogi Park directly, offering something the city's tower hotels can't: green at eye level.

Hotel Tagline: Rainbow Bridge, the skyline, and Mount Fuji share the same bay-facing window.
Summary: From the shores of Tokyo Bay, this 30-story hotel frames Rainbow Bridge, the city skyline, and Mount Fuji in a single bay-facing panorama — the kind of view where the subject changes with the hour and the weather. A bay-facing room, with the SkyLounge Stellar on the top floor for the evening transition from daylight to the lit city reflecting across the water.
Description ⚙: From the shores of Tokyo Bay, this 30-story hotel frames Rainbow Bridge, the city skyline, and Mount Fuji in a single bay-facing panorama — the kind of view where the subject changes with the hour and the weather.

Hotel Tagline: Six floors of triple-glazed glass hold Tokyo Station's brick dome in silent theater below.
Summary: Sixty rooms across six floors of the Pacific Century Place skyscraper, with triple-glazed windows turning the Tokyo Station district into silent theater below. The highest available suite, with Motif for dinner — the station’s brick dome at close range from above is a perspective that altitude usually removes rather than provides.
Description ⚙: Sixty rooms across six floors of the Pacific Century Place skyscraper, with triple-glazed windows turning the Tokyo Station district into silent theater below.

Hotel Tagline: From 40 stories above Shibuya, the famous scramble crossing shrinks to a pattern below.
Summary: The 40-story silhouette above Shibuya puts the city’s most energetic district in frame from a height where the famous scramble crossing becomes a pattern rather than a crowd. A city-facing room on the upper floors, the Noh theatre in the basement before dinner at Sakuragaoka — the contrast between the two is what Cerulean Tower does best.
Description ⚙: The 40-story silhouette above Shibuya puts the city's most energetic district in frame from a height where the famous scramble crossing becomes a pattern rather than a crowd.
